title: A NEW YEAR
body: It’s a new year. We need new calendars.
Aside from that, not much is special about today other than we eat more, we watch football, and have more people nursing hangover headaches than any other day of the year.
It’s New Year’s Day.
Hopefully we have learned a few things this past year: Things that have made us wiser, better. Hopefully we grew closer to the Lord and to our loved ones.
Hopefully we will grow even closer this year.
affirmation: I am going to make a real effort to get to know the Lord and His Word better this year.
verse: “Forgetting those things which are behind, and reaching forth unto those things which are before.” (Philippians 3:13)

title: IF I HAD...
body: Many people think if they had a mate they would be happy.
Or if they had a better job they would be happy.
Or if they had a child, lived somewhere else, had more money, a bigger house, or a better education, or if they didn’t have so many problems they would be happier.
People find all kinds of excuses to not be happy. Happiness doesn’t depend on things or circumstances.
The way to be happy... Is to not think about the things that make you unhappy!
Happiness is a choice.
If you want to be happy, BE!
affirmation: I am happy because I have God’s love in my heart and His presence in my life.
verse: “Happy is that people whose God is the Lord.” (Psalms 144:15)

title: WE ALL SPEAK IT
body: We all need love. We all understand loving concern.
We may not speak a foreign language, but there is one universal language everyone speaks, everyone understands. Love transcends borders, cultures, and age.
We don’t have to all agree on politics. We don’t all have to see eye to eye on religion, social issues, or sports.
But we can love. We can be loving in our dealings with others.
Gentle, tender, respectful.
Tolerant, patient, and accepting.
Caring, sharing, and kind:
LOVE.
affirmation: I am loving and considerate of others in my dealings with them.
verse: “Let us love one another: for love is of God; and every one that loves is born of God, and knows God.” (1 John 4:7)

title: UNDER YOUR NOSE
body: Many people are looking in the wrong places.
People are looking for love. They are looking for something better. But often, very often, what we seek is closer than we think.
God is good. And He has promised to supply ALL of our needs.
So, if you think you are lacking something, look up and ask the Lord to help you see things like He sees them.
What you are looking for may just be there right under your nose!
affirmation: I know you want and have the very best for me, Lord, and I thank You for being so good to me.
verse: “Open Thou mine eyes that I may see.” (Psalms 119:18)

title: HOW IT WORKS
body: You see it all the time: Someone is big one day, and the next they have been reduced to nothing.
They become rich and famous, and haughty and arrogant. But then they say or do something wrong, and down they come!
Politicians, heads of state, dictators, and presidents are suddenly assassinated. Big Wall Street bankers and titans of industry end up in prison for fraud.
Very few people are able to handle fame and fortune and the praise of man without taking the glory to themselves. And God doesn’t like it when we are proud and self-satisfied, boastful and arrogant.
The Bible says, “The high places shall be brought low.”
That’s how it works!
affirmation: Everything I am and have comes from You, Lord, and without You I am nothing.
verse: “Wherefore let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.” (1 Corinthians 10:12)

title: IN GOD’S TOMORROW
body: Time shall be no more.
There will be no more sadness or pain, nor sorrow, broken dreams or Internet connections.
Email will be obsolete, wifi unnecessary, no more computers, and no one will care.
We will have nutritious food, be able to bathe in every stream, and sleep under the stars.
Everyone will be pretty, all will be healthy, no one will take himself too seriously, or react in a huff.
There will be fun things to do, pleasures galore, and every day a party. The perfect place where mosquitoes don’t bite, all wrongs are made right, no one will frown and all will have smiles.
We’ll find time for our neighbor, feel content in our labor, and learn new things each day.
We will have learned to love, in God’s tomorrow... Where time shall be no more!
affirmation: I know the day is coming, and it’s going to be good!
verse: “He will teach us His ways, and we will walk in His paths. Nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.” (Isaiah 2:3-4)

title: THE ANSWER
body: True religion is not about a harsh, vengeful, controlling, guilt tripping God that wants people to conform to an unrealistic set of inflexible rules. True religion, in fact, is not about rules!
True religion is about love and a voluntary giving of oneself to God and to our fellow man.

God’s Word says that in order “to build and to plant” we have to first “root out, and pull down, to destroy and to throw down” (See Jer. 1:10). And that is what we are doing: tearing down people’s misconceptions of what God is like.
We challenge the false image of God that has been foisted upon humanity now for centuries.
Love *is *the answer. Love is the way.
God is love. And His love always wins... In the end.
affirmation: I have found the answer to life’s questions and freedom from the bondage of religious rules in the simplicity of God and His love.
verse: “Know the love of Christ which surpasses knowledge that ye might be filled with the fullness of God.” (Ephesians 3:19)

title: ACHIEVING YOUR DREAM
body: Ideas are cheap.
All of us have great ideas, but that doesn’t make us special.
That’s why if you are looking for someone to back your idea, other than your mother, it’s probably not going to happen!
Hard work is what counts.
And it’s only with hard work, day in and day out, and with the Lord’s help...
That you can make your dream come true!
affirmation: I am not sitting around waiting to be *discovered*. I make things happen in my life.
verse: “Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with all thy might.” (Ecclesiastes 9:10)

title: BEACONS
body: Where are the beacons in this world?
So many people out there seek their own glory. So many voices sing their own praise. So many flakey and superficial people are idolized and held up as role models.
But where are the beacons? Where are the people we can really look up to and admire?
Mother Teresa was a beacon of love. She was a beacon of compassion.
Mother Teresa didn’t live a life of self exaltation, but a life of service. She spent her
entire life helping the poorest of the poor. Yet most of her good deeds went unnoticed, known only to God.
Mother Teresa was and always will be a true beacon to the world.
affirmation: I try to be a good example of God’s love and kindness to the people in my life.
verse: “When you have done it unto the least of these, you have done it unto Me.” (Matthew 25:40)

title: THE MIDDLE ROAD
body: Can you have too much of a good thing?
Too much sugar is bad. Too much salt. Too much sun is bad. Eating in excess is not good.
Almost everything in excess is not good. Even exercising.
Things in excess also seem to be the cause of many of the world’s problems:
Too much work. Too much leisure. Too much worry. Too much waste.
The Bible tells us to be moderate in all things. MODERATION is God’s secret to health, well being, and happiness.
affirmation: I avoid excesses in my life and walk the middle road of moderation in gratitude to You, Lord.
verse: “The fruit of the spirit is... temperance.” (Galatians 5:22-23)

title: “I HAD FUN”
body: A famous actor died recently. And in describing his life, his last words were, “I couldn’t have had more fun.”
You hear that frequently these days.
Life for many people has been reduced to just having fun: eating, drinking, traveling, flaunting their money, and having a good time.
Many wealthy people have set that poor example by spending exorbitant amounts of money on cars, houses, throwing parties, as well as many frivolous pursuits. “Living the good life,” they call it.
When we come to the end of the road the Lord is going to ask us all, “What did you do with your life?”
Only what we do to help others will last. I’m not sure saying “I had fun” will cut it!
affirmation: I spend my time helping others and doing things that are truly worthwhile.
verse: “Labor not for things that won’t last, but rather labor for those things that are eternal.” (John 6:27)

title: THIS PRISON WORLD
body: We are living in a prison world. Many people are slaves to their jobs. They have little to look forward to as far as change. Many on Earth live in poverty. They live in a prison of perpetual need, while the rich live in ivory towers and are in constant fear of losing their riches.
Many people are prisoners of their health.
Others are imprisoned by the fear of death. Almost the entire human race lives in fear of one kind or another.
Entire nations live in fear: Fear of their neighbors. Fear that the other guy will use his nuclear weapons first.
The saddest part is that man himself is largely to blame. We created these prisons.
We ourselves built the walls that separate us from our fellow man.
But only LOVE can break them down.
affirmation: God has set me free from the chains of this world through His love.
verse: “If the Son therefore shall make you free, you shall be free indeed.” (John 8:36)

title: WHO CARES?
body: Some people worry a lot about what people think. They worry about what people say.
But as we get older we realize that most people are so busy with their own lives that they don’t care about you or what you do.
We waste so much of our time worrying about what people think that it keeps us from doing many things. We hold back. We limit ourselves. We need to stop that.
As long as we are doing the right thing, and as long as we are doing the loving thing, we don’t have to fear what others think. Who cares?
God knows. God sees.
And that’s all that matters!
affirmation: I do the right thing and it doesn’t matter what people say.
verse: “We ought to do what’s right in the sight of God.” (Acts 4:19)

title: IF
body: If you are faithful to read the Word, and tell others about the Lord, and walk in love, and pray...
You will grow in the Lord.
You will find strength that you knew not of.
And God will never fail to protect you, and supply all your needs,
and care for you.
Period!
affirmation: I pray, I read the Word, and I find ways to be a blessing to others.
verse: “Do justly, love mercy, and walk humbly with thy God.” (Micah 6:8)

title: NOW OR NEVER
body: People work hard to build up their businesses. Others invest in the stock market. They want to make a lot of money.

People live as if this world were going to last forever. They get so caught up in the things of this present life. They work and labor and sacrifice for things that will not last, things that will be soon forgotten.

We go through this life but once, and NOW is the only time we have to build for eternity. Now is the only chance we’ll get to invest in souls: Living souls who will be our friends forever in the world to come.
It’s now or never.
The only time we have is NOW!
affirmation: I am investing in eternity and things that will last.
verse: “Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon Earth, but lay up for yourselves treasures in Heaven.” (Matthew 6:19-20)

title: THE PATH TO PEACE
body: We see a lot of division in the world: opposing political beliefs, different religious views, gaps between generations, peoples, and nationalities.
Tension, strife, and disunity are tearing families, neighborhoods, and countries apart. All this fussing, fighting, and arguing is not solving anything. It’s only making us sadder and sadder!
We all basically want the same things, and we have so much more in common than the things that we are letting divide us.

The solution is to start focusing on those things and be grateful for our many blessings. Only then will we see things in their proper perspective and be empowered to solve our problems and bring about change.
God blesses unity, people working together, living in harmony and peace with each other. Nothing else will ever truly solve man’s problems!
Spread the WORD!
affirmation: I dwell on the many positive things we all have in common.
verse: “God is not the author of confusion, but of peace.” (1 Corinthians 14:33)

title: FRIENDSHIP
body: Friendship’s a funny thing isn’t it?
There are people that you just like from the start. And people who seem to like you.
And then there are others who no matter how hard you try, for some reason just won’t let you be their friend. Why is that?
Maybe we aren’t meant to be really close friends with just everybody. Maybe that’s what makes friends so special, their uniqueness.
A true friend will stand beside you no matter what. Jesus is that kind of friend.
He loves us, not for anything we have done, but because HE is the embodiment of love itself, the Spirit of the loving Creator of the universe.
God’s love is the cement that binds friends and sustains a true friendship.
May we be loving, patient, and kind to those we love, and to those who love us... TRUE friends.
affirmation: I am a true friend and am there for people when they really need me.
verse: “There is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.” (Proverbs 18:24)

title: GOD KNOWS WHAT HE’S DOING
body: One of the biggest lessons we have to learn in life is to wait for God to work.
God has His times, and if we try to rush things and push our way through we may end up not being happy with the results.
Most of us get impatient. Most of us seem to think we know better than God. We want things right now!
But God’s condition is clear: Put Him first, and then, after you have done THAT, He will give you the desires of your heart.
God knows what’s best.
Wait on Him.
affirmation: I wait on the Lord, I trust in Him, and let Him do things in His timing.
verse: “No good thing will He withhold from those who walk uprightly.” (Psalms 84:11)

title: BE GRATEFUL
body: May we always be grateful for our many blessings.
May we always thank the Lord for every bite we eat, and for having a place to lay our heads.
May we never take for granted our loved ones and friends, because everything can be lost in an instant.
Life is full of surprises, and in this world of uncertainty you never know what’s going to happen next.
Lord help us to remember that.
And always, always be grateful!
affirmation: I am grateful for everyone and everything in my life and take nothing for granted.
verse: “Enter into His gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise: be thankful unto Him, and bless His name.” (Psalms 100:4)

title: DON’T HANG UP!
body: Most people seem to think that prayer is venting to God and letting Him know what they want and think. But prayer is really more about hearing what God has to say to us.
We can begin by talking to the Lord, telling Him our concerns and worries. But the time should come when we stop talking, and instead of hanging up on Him, give Him a chance to speak to our hearts.
Samuel in the Old Testament had it right when he said, “Speak Lord, for thy servant heareth.”
We all have two ears and one mouth. And that’s the proportion we should use them in when it comes to prayer. If we do we won’t be disappointed.
God does answer prayer. And He WILL answer yours. But you’re going to have to quiet your own spirit first and,
LISTEN to what He has to say!
affirmation: I stop the noise all around me, Lord. I quiet my spirit, and seek You in prayer.
verse: “The Lord speaks in a still, small, voice.” (1 Kings 19:12)

title: LIKE A TREE
body: Trees are nice things.
Trees provide shelter from the sun, refuge for animals, and surrender their fruits to man.
Birds like them. Carpenters need them, and children play in them.
A tree spreads its arms upward in continual praise to God as a silent testimony of His goodness.
Who doesn’t like a tree?
May we be like trees, deeply rooted in God’s love, so that we may be a blessing to many!
affirmation: I am yielded to You, Lord, and am letting You use me for Your glory.
verse: “He shall be like a tree... and whatever he does shall prosper.” (Psalms 1:3)

title: AT DEATH’S DOOR
body: One time I almost died.
And as I lay there at death’s door I saw how most of the things we place value on in this life are just not important.
We really do have our sense of priorities mixed up. We don’t see things as God sees them.
Facing death, I saw that everything we strive for, everything we get all worked up about and sacrifice for just doesn’t matter in the world to come.
I saw at that precise moment how blessed I was. I saw how good I had it in this life.
I also realized that it was really easy to please God. And I knew that all He was asking of me was to be happy, to be grateful, and to help others do the same.
How cool is that!
That’s what I learned that day. The day I almost died.
affirmation: Thank You for making it easy to please You, Lord, and for the wonderful life You have given me.
verse: “We brought nothing into this world. Having food and clothing may we be content.” (1 Timothy 6:7-8)

title: WATCH OUT!
body: It’s so silly to hold grudges.
Some people hold grudges for years. It’s ridiculous.
Bitterness will get you nowhere. The past is past. And dwelling on the past will only rob you of the joy of today.
Bitterness destroys you more than anyone else, because it pumps its poison into your body as well as your soul.
The only way out of the dead end of bitterness is to start counting your blessings and be grateful for what you have.
So, stop your whining! Stop feeling sorry for yourself and pretending to be the victim.
God hates murmuring!
If you complain long enough, He may really give you something to whine about.
You better watch out!
affirmation: I am not living in the past, and I do not complain or give place to grudges and bitterness.
verse: “Do all things without murmurings.” (Philippians 2:14)

title: SPACED OUT!
body: Space is out there and is a never ending eternal testimony of God’s greatness.
They say this star, or that planet, or galaxy is a million light years away, or a billion! Astronomical numbers and distances so big that it’s hard to even wrap your brain around them!
It must be cool to be able to look into the universe through one of those big telescopes.

When you only start to have the most minimal sense of the vastness and enormity of space, when you only begin to grasp its size, that isn’t even a drop in the bucket of what’s really out there. It is so incredibly beyond our grasp and our comprehension. And that’s what God intended.
Space is there to give us an idea of the unfathomable greatness of God!
affirmation: I look up at the stars and the moon and praise the Lord for His never ending mercy, love, wisdom, and might.
verse: “The heavens declare the glory of God.” (Psalms 19:1)

title: IMAGINE
body: Man spends trillions of dollars on war, security, and defense, every year!
Imagine how the world would be if even half that amount of money went to building hospitals, parks, and schools.
Imagine how everything would change if everyone was fed, clothed, and warm.
With that kind of money we could free man from the burden of poverty, ease the agony of uncertainty, sleep at night without fear, and all smile once again. Imagine that!
People tell me that isn’t realistic. They say it’s impossible. They insist it can’t be done.
They say we are dreamers.
We are.
affirmation: It can be done. We can do it together!
verse: “To him that believeth ALL things are possible.” (Mark 9:23)

title: SEEING THE INVISIBLE
body: We know the wind exists, because we see what it does. Although we can’t see the wind, we see it blowing through the trees.
But we have to open our window if we want to feel it.
Likewise it is when we open our hearts to God and His love that we begin to see Him in our lives moving behind the scenes and working for our good.
God and the spirit world exist, even though we cannot see either of them.
But we can know them...
If we just open the windows of our hearts!
affirmation: I keep my heart open to God and His love.
verse: “If any man open the door I will come in to him.” (Revelation 3:20)

title: CONTROL FREAKS
body: Parents feel frustrated because their children don’t do what they want. Wives get frustrated with husbands. Husbands with wives. Kids with their parents.
We want our computers to work perfectly, our cars to run smoothly, our jobs to be ideal, and for everyone to agree with us and our way of doing things and seeing things.
We want everything our way all the time.
And we get bent out of shape when we don’t get it.
So many problems in life come from our trying to control others. We really are becoming control freaks.
We all need to let go and trust that God is in control of our lives!
affirmation: I give You control of my life, Lord, and let You use me to be a blessing to others.
verse: “He must increase, but I must decrease.” (John 3:30)

title: WHO CAN YOU TRUST?
body: In this world nowadays it seems like everything is becoming disposable, including relationships.
You see it everywhere: people turning on each other. Politicians do it. Even life long friends will suddenly get mad at each other, often over the most trivial of things and call it quits.
Unscrupulous dealings in business are commonplace. Cheating in academics, sports, and romance are widespread. People are growing colder. Who can you trust? Who can you rely on any more?
When this old world has nothing left to offer, you will still have Jesus. When all others forsake you, He will still be there by your side.
You’ve tried everything else. Try Him.
Ask Him into your life and He WILL come in and help you, show you. His love is never ending.
Only God will never let you down!
affirmation: I trust in Thee, Lord. I give You my all, my everything. Thy will be done in my life.
verse: “He is no fool who gives a life he cannot keep, to gain a love he will never lose.”

title: NEW BEGINNINGS
body: As we grow older we have to go slower. We find that we sometimes don’t have the energy or stamina we used to have.
At first it’s hard to accept. But then as we adjust to it we see it as the blessing that it is.
We start to see things we missed in our haste.
We take time for things we didn’t even notice before. And we appreciate some things more.
As we begin new stages in our lives, let’s look forward to the opportunities, experiences, and new beginnings that await us.
Let’s take some things more in stride, and stop and take time to see the beauty that surrounds us.
affirmation: I accept these changes in my life and embrace them as new opportunities for spiritual growth.
verse: “Get wisdom. Get understanding. Forsake them not.” (Proverbs 4:5)

title: GOOD OR BAD?
body: People seem to be confused when it comes to pride. Is pride a good thing? Or is it bad?
It’s ok to be proud of your children when they do something good. We all have a sense of pride in our work, or should.
But when it comes to a high and haughty attitude, that type of pride is not good. Pride and arrogance and contentious spirits are not of God. Nor is a hard unforgiving spirit.
Satan’s sin was pride and rebellion. And when we are proud we are reflecting him.
Our God is love. And the fruits of living in His presence are love, meekness, joy, goodness, and a peaceful spirit toward others.
There are two paths. The choice is ours.
affirmation: I choose the path of humility and dependence on Thee, O Lord.
verse: “A meek and humble heart Thou, Lord, will not despise.” (Psalms 51:17)

title: TAKE THE PLUNGE
body: Communication is everything.
People today are starving for real, deep, and heartfelt communication.
Fear has a grip on some people and keeps many from opening up.
But where there is love there is no fear, because fear and love cannot reside in the same place at the same time.
Make an effort to talk with your mate, your child, your friend. Make yourself vulnerable and communicate from your heart.
Take the plunge.
And you will inspire others to do the same!
affirmation: I am open and share my feelings with my loved ones.
verse: “To do good and communicate forget not.” (Hebrews 13:16)

title: THINGS ARE NOT AS THEY SEEM
body: It’s so easy to think we know more than others, to think we could do things better. We are often critical and harshly judge people for the job they are doing. Why is that? I guess it is just human nature.
They say that ninety percent of an iceberg is under water. We see only a fraction of it.

So it is with most things in life. We often think we see the whole picture when we only see a small part. We are tempted to think we know so much about some things when we can’t begin to appreciate the sacrifice, the labor, the blood, sweat, and tears that have gone into them!
We all need to be forgiven for so many things. Each one of us really needs to remember a few of the mistakes we ourselves have made, and not be so demanding.
Lord help us to be more loving, more understanding, and more accepting of others!
affirmation: I know I don’t see everything. I wait until You, Lord, show me the complete picture.
verse: “If any man thinks he knows anything, he knows nothing as he should.” (1 Corinthians 8:2)

title: GOING TO HELL?
body: A lot of people say that you have to “do this”, or “don’t do that” or you’ll “go to hell”. But did you know that nowhere in the Bible does it even say that?

God will forgive any sin, if we ask Him. And many things we are taught as “sins” aren’t even sins in the first place. The Bible says the only thing that will not be forgiven us, the only thing that God cannot forgive, is rejection of Him!
So I don’t believe that anyone is going to hell for not keeping a whole bunch of rules!

In fact, I believe that “hell” will simply be far away from God’s presence. After all, if God is love, then to be far away from Him would sure be hell! And the only way to get there is by outright rejection of God and His love.
Pleasing God isn’t hard. Don’t worry about being *good enough *for God. Just love Him, and you’ll have nothing to worry about.
Pretty simple, isn’t it?
affirmation: Thank You, dear Heavenly Father, for being so gracious, merciful, and loving though none of us deserve it.
verse: “He that loveth hath fulfilled the law.” (Romans 13:8)

title: IT STARTS WITH YOU
body: We should ALL be on a journey of self improvement. Bookstores have entire sections devoted to this subject, so a lot of people are reading them. But the majority of us aren’t.

We can whine and complain all we want, and we can see ourselves as *victims*, but what does that accomplish? Doing nothing gets us nowhere. Only a proactive attitude can bring about change. And that change must first come from within.

Your circumstances don’t determine where you can go. They merely determine where you start. You do have the power to change the world: YOUR world, your family and friends, the world you live in. But you are going to have to start with yourself.
Ask God to give you His love for those around you. And ask Him to show you the areas YOU need to change in.
You’ll be a lot happier!
affirmation: God is changing me and making me into what He wants me to be.
verse: “I can do all things through Christ who strengtheneth me.” (Philippians 4:13)

title: DEEP DOWN INSIDE!
body: Have you noticed how people when they find themselves in imminent danger cry out to God? People who otherwise would not be caught praying, do so when they get scared.
That just goes to show how deeply ingrained in our beings the certainty of God is.

Man’s longing for love is a longing for God, a yearning to be reunited with Him. One that will never be satisfied any other way, no matter how many things of this temporal transitory life we throw at it.
Only God can satisfy that longing in your heart. Only He and His love can ever fill that aching void.
He’s waiting. But He won’t force Himself upon you. If you open your heart to Him, He will most certainly come in and reveal Himself to you in a unique and special way.
Then you will KNOW He is real... Deep down inside!
affirmation: Help me to know You, Lord, very up close and personal.
verse: “Draw nigh to God and He will draw nigh to you.” (James 4:8)

title: WE HAVE A LOT!
body: We all have a lot to be thankful for, yet we often find ourselves complaining. Lord help us!
All you have to do is look at the world today with its famines, wars, natural disasters, and plagues to realize how much you have to be grateful for.

If you have food in your belly, clothes on your back, and a place to lay your head, you’re richer than many people on this planet. And if you have someone in your life who loves you, you are richer than a king!
Let’s not be heard to murmur and complain.
It must grieve God’s heart to hear us complain when He has been so good to us.
We are mightily blessed.
And we have a LOT to be grateful for!
affirmation: I count my blessings and am grateful for my life and the people and things I have in it.
verse: “Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits.” (Psalms 68:19)

title: IT’S OUR FAULT!
body: If God exists why doesn’t He do something?
You hear people saying that all the time.
After natural disasters, during wars, in times of social upheaval, illness, and death they blame God. “Why doesn’t God do something?”
Why doesn’t He?
Because God has given man free will to do as he pleases. And all of the problems we face today are the accumulated effect of man’s lack of love toward his fellow man.
Be patient. God does exist. Stop blaming Him!
affirmation: God is in control and everything is playing out according to His plan.
verse: “For yet a little while, and He that shall come will come, and will not tarry.” (Hebrews 10:37)

title: SAY “I LOVE YOU”
body: I saw a TV show where two brothers were really close but were afraid to hug or say they loved each other.
This show painted saying “I love you” to someone we care about as a weakness, something that somehow *real *men just don’t do!
Look at what we have become. This world certainly is ass-backwards.
God is our anchor and His Word is our light.
And we sure need both in this world that is so confusing and where so many people don’t seem to know up from down anymore. As the Bible says, “calling light dark, and darkness light.”
God is love. He shows us that He loves us in so many ways. God continually tells us, “I love you.” We should also say “I love you” to those we love.
Real men DO say “I love you”!
affirmation: Love and showing affection are of God. They are not weaknesses.
verse: “Woe unto them that call evil good, and good evil.” (Isaiah 5:20)

title: FOOD, GLORIOUS FOOD!
body: Food is good. A steak is great.
But also eat plenty of fruits and vegetables, and don’t forget nuts, whole grains, and fish when you can.
Sugar substitutes are deadly. Dietary supplements are risky.
Limit white sugar, refined foods, and things with all those chemicals.
That’s what we think. This is what works for us.
Above all, avoid excesses. Moderation is the key to good health.
And before you eat,
Don’t forget to ask God to bless your food!
affirmation: I eat healthy and generally try to avoid excesses, and God blesses my efforts.
verse: “Let your moderation be known to all men.” (Philippians 4:5)

title: WHICH DO YOU DO?
body: Did you know that the religious fanatics criticized Jesus the most? They called Jesus a sinner!
Surely you have had the unpleasant experience of being told that something you were doing was a “sin”, and that “God didn’t like it”. People judge you in the name of God!
But Jesus didn’t condemn the average person.
Jesus only condemned the self righteous, holier than thou religious fanatics of His day. That’s why they ended up killing Him, because He exposed them for the hypocrites that they were.
Jesus’ whole message was that we should love each other. By HIS EXAMPLE He taught us to love, not to condemn.
How sad it is that some people STILL haven’t figured that out!
affirmation: I follow Jesus’ example and as much as possible show love. I do not criticize anyone.
verse: “Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children.” (Ephesians 5:1)

title: MAKE TIME!
body: We find time in our busy lives for all sorts of things. But we find so little time for God and things of the Spirit. Almost anything and everything is more important than Him!

We exclude God by keeping ourselves busy with other things: Temporal things, frivolous things, all kinds of things take first place in our lives. Things we want to do. Things we think we have to do. Things that won’t last.
We give Him whatever time we have left over, if any at all. How can we expect God to bless us when we exclude Him?
No wonder our lives unravel.
“God doesn’t want our SPARE time. God wants our PRECIOUS time!”
affirmation: Heavenly Father, I give You first place in my life, my home, and my heart.
verse: “Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, with all thy soul, with all thy mind, and with all thy strength.” (Mark 12:30)

title: EMBRACING DEATH
body: Death is not a bad thing. It’s our graduation!
And just as birth is a joyful event, death can also be if you have the Lord.

We all have a certain apprehension regarding death, especially if we think about any pain that might come along with it. But death is inevitable, and it pays to face our fears and discuss them openly.

Many people who have had near death experiences state that they saw a bright light and felt God as a warm, welcoming, loving presence. They weren’t afraid. They felt so good, that they didn’t even want to come back!
So don’t be afraid of death. Death is not the end. It is only the beginning! A mere stepping stone to all the pleasures and blessings of God.
It will be your day of glory, if you have made peace with God...
And are now living in HIS love!
affirmation: I know that death will be my graduation into the next life and I have nothing to fear.
verse: “Death is swallowed up in victory.” (1 Corinthians 15:54)

title: LIFE IS BEAUTIFUL
body: The world is a mess. There is no disputing that. But there’s still a lot of beauty around us. There is a lot of good in this world.
God’s beautiful creation surrounds us as an everlasting testament of His presence and love.
Many people live unselfishly, thinking of others, working to make the world a better place. Many products make our lives easier. Many projects give us hope for tomorrow.
So if sometimes you feel discouraged, if sometimes everything you see depresses you, remember:
What you see, depends a lot on what you’re looking for.
Look for the GOOD!
affirmation: I look on the positive side of things and see the good and beauty that surrounds me.
verse: “What things are good, pure, lovely, and of good report... dwell on these.” (Philippians 4:8)

title: TIRED OF WAITING?
body: We have all grown so used to things getting done right here and right now.
We expect God to answer us immediately. But God doesn’t work that way. He takes time to make a flower, a little baby, or a sunset. He is seldom in a hurry.
It’s hard to wait. Waiting shows faith and real trust that God is in control and that He knows what’s best.
A lot of people get bored with waiting for God to work in their lives. But the fact is God is waiting for them! God is waiting for them to start DOING something.
Get busy and stay busy loving and helping others, doing good in some way while you are waiting for God’s answer.
THAT’S the kind of person who gets mightily blessed by the Lord.
affirmation: I am doing what I know I can today, and know that God will take care of the rest in His perfect time.
verse: “Let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not. As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men.” (Galatians 6:9-10)

title: LOVE ABOVE ALL
body: Before you start your day, before you rush into your job, your worries, your life...
Remember: In everything we do God wants us to show love to each other. That’s the only reason we are here in this world: To learn to love.
Love is the mystery. And love is the reward. It doesn’t matter what others do or say.
What’s done with love will last.
We will never regret those things we did to help others. We will never feel bad that we chose love. We will never regret the things we did with unselfish sacrificial love for someone else.
Love is stronger than hate.
Forgiveness is more powerful than revenge. Truth will conquer, ultimately.
God is love. And those who live in love live in God and God in them.
Rest assured, love WILL win, in the end!
affirmation: God uses me to help usher in His love here on Earth.
verse: “Tis love the whole world yearns for, and ‘tis love that bids them heed Thy call.”

title: LONELINESS
body: A lot of people are lonely nowadays.
People get online looking for love, looking for companionship. Loneliness is killing people.
But I have found there is a really simple cure for loneliness. And that is to get out of yourself and into others.
When we start thinking of others it often makes our own problems seem small. When we get busy trying to help someone else, our own problems just disappear!
It really is that simple.
We all need each other.
And God blesses us when we reach out to others!
affirmation: I put others first in my life and am blessed with love and companionship in abundance.
verse: “Whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.” (Galatians 6:7)

title: SHOW APPRECIATION
body: We all like to feel special. We all like to feel appreciated.
It’s really nice when someone appreciates our efforts. We feel good when someone notices the job we are doing, and the sacrifices we make. That encourages us to do an even better job.
So let’s make the people we love feel special by showing them that we really appreciate them. Let’s show them we care.
It doesn’t take much to encourage someone. A word of encouragement goes such a long way...
And can really make someone’s day!
affirmation: I appreciate the people around me and encourage them to be their best.
verse: “Do unto others as you would have them do unto you.” (Matthew 7:12)

title: MODERN MAN
body: Man wasn’t always as unbelieving as he is today. Man wasn’t always as rebellious against God.

Many people nowadays say God is a myth, and that man only believes in Him out of ignorance. They paint God as a fairy tale personality, something that anyone with even an ounce of brain can clearly see is not real.
Such is our modern world. And only modern man has rejected God.
God is as alive today as He ever was. And He works in people’s lives as much now as ever.
The only way to know God, is to open your heart and receive Him. Then, you too, will know.
Then, you too, will BELIEVE!
affirmation: I know You are with me, Lord, and I thank You for my many blessings.
verse: “God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.” (Psalms 46:1)

title: READ IT!
body: Without God’s Word you can’t have light, or joy, or might. Without God’s Word you can’t be successful, loyal, or fruitful. Without God’s Word you’ll never find your place; you’ll be unprepared, be lost in space.
You won’t go far without it. You’ll wear out, you’ll give up. If you neglect it, you will regret it.
The secret to God’s blessings, the key to God’s favor, is living in His Word. The things of this earth will pass away. But God and His Word will live forever.
We will NEVER regret the time we spent reading and studying God’s Word.
affirmation: Your Word, Lord, is the most important thing in my life, and I make time for it daily.
verse: “Forever, O Lord, Thy Word is settled in heaven.” (Psalms 119:89)

title: WHERE TO START?
body: The Bible can be confusing. It is so big you may not know where to begin.
The Bible is unlike other books, other than a dictionary maybe, in that you may not really want to read it all the way through from the beginning.
The gospels are a good place to start. They are at the beginning of the New Testament.
Then you can continue on from there.
Take breaks when you want and read something from the Old Testament. Maybe a Psalm or two, a chapter in Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, or the beginning of Genesis.
Pray a little prayer and ask God to lead you, to speak to you before you start each time. Most people open their Bibles too soon, and close them too soon.
As you get into God’s Word you will discover all kinds of cool things. And you will understand how and why it has stood the test of time!
affirmation: I love spending time in Your Word, Lord. Through it You show me so many amazing things.
verse: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.” (John 1:1)

title: BE THAT CHANGE!
body: What we do day in and day out is what we believe. You can’t say you believe in eating healthy if you live on junk food. You don’t believe in exercise if you never exercise. You don’t believe in helping others if you’re not doing something to help those in need. And you can’t say you believe in love if you’re not loving to those around you.

Good intentions, no matter how noble, have no power to change anything. It is the *doing *that counts. It is only by doing that we can make a difference. We don’t become what we say we believe, we become what we do every day.

If you want the world to be more loving, YOU be more loving. If you want the world to be more tolerant, work on being more accepting yourself. This is the only way that we can ultimately make a difference. Change must first come from within. Someone once said: “Be the change you want to see in the world.”
affirmation: I daily DO the things that will bring about the changes I seek in my life.
verse: “Be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.” (James 1:22)

title: LIKE KIDS AGAIN!
body: What makes a baby so beautiful? Is it the wonder in his eyes? Or is it the innocence of his soul?
A baby sees no evil. He judges no one. He accepts you with a love that is true and pure. A baby hasn’t learned to be distrustful yet. A baby hasn’t learned to fear.
Jesus said we should be like little children. Jesus said that theirs was the Kingdom of Heaven.
Heaven will be like Disney World, only better: a lot, lot better!
In Heaven we will all be children again, all giggles and smiles.
We will have all learned to trust. We will have ALL learned to love.
affirmation: Lord help me to have the innocence, trust, and joy of a little child once again.
verse: “You, Lord, hide Your secrets from the wise and prudent but reveal them unto little children.” (Matthew 11:25)

title: A LIFE OF LOVE
body: When we are young we think life will last forever.
But the years have a way of passing by without our noticing until we have neither the strength nor the opportunities we used to have. And we wonder where our lives went.
We look back at so many of the things that we obsessed over at the time and thought were so important, things we sacrificed for, and we realize they weren’t worth it.
We have nothing now to show for them. They are long forgotten by everyone else but us. Must this happen to us all?
We get one shot at life.
Think long and hard about your choices.
affirmation: I put God and His love first in my life and in all my dealings with others.
verse: “Rejoice young man in thy youth, and walk in the ways of thine heart. But know that for all these things God will bring thee into judgment.” (Ecclesiastes 11:9)

title: LEARNING BY LISTENING
body: It seems like everybody’s talking nowadays.
Have you ever been in a room and it seemed like everyone was talking all at once? Funny isn’t it!
But you know, you can’t learn anything as long as you are talking.
God gave us two ears and one mouth. Lord help us to use them in that proportion. But that takes a real effort. It’s not easy sometimes.
I have been trying to talk less and pay more attention to others lately.
And I’ve been amazed at the things you can learn, if you just observe, and listen!
affirmation: I listen more than I talk and I learn more that way.
verse: “Be not rash with thy mouth... Let thy words be few.” (Ecclesiastes 5:2)

title: LIFE’S STRUGGLES
body: So many people are struggling today.
Many have money problems. Others poor health.
A lot of people don’t know where to turn.
But God always takes care of His own.
He will always provide for those who seek Him, and unconditionally surrender to Him.
God will do His part. But we’ve got to do ours.
And our part is to put HIM first!
affirmation: You are first in my life and my greatest joy is to please You, Lord.
verse: “If they obey and serve Him, they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures” (Job 36:11)

title: LOVE IS GIVING
body: They say there are two types of people: givers, and takers.
Selfishness sure seems to be rampant in the world today. No wonder people are unhappy.
But love is all about giving, and when two people give, they both receive.
When we care about someone, we put their happiness above our own.
You can give without love...
But you can’t love, without GIVING!
affirmation: I put other people’s happiness above my own, and I am blessed.
verse: “Freely ye have received, freely give.” (Matthew 10:8)

title: STRIVE TO BE HAPPY
body: Happiness is a funny thing. It’s so easy at times. Yet at others it’s hard.
When we let go of our worries and cares we can’t help but be happy. If we only live in the future, we can’t really be happy now. And dwelling on the past will keep us on a bummer.
But living in the present and thinking of others is a sure thing to make us glad.
Happiness attracts money, good health, and more happiness. And being happy is a sure fire way to attract the opposite sex!
We don’t have a sad God.
We have a happy God who wants us to be happy!
affirmation: I am happy and grateful and blessed, and I share that joy with the world.
verse: “The joy of the Lord is your strength.” (Nehemiah 8:10)

title: YOUR CHOICE
body: Negativity is a killer. Negativity gets you nowhere.
Negativity will bring you down, reduce you to nothing, and ultimately defeat you.
Negativity is contagious, and you must avoid it like a plague.
But being positive has the opposite effect on you! A positive attitude will build you up.
Being positive is a choice that will attract life and love and overcoming into your world!
Positive? or negative? What’s it gonna be? The choice is yours!
affirmation: I am positive and speak positively to everyone that I meet.
verse: “Our spirits are contagious. We are either lifting up or beating down. There is no middle ground.”

title: THE POWER OF LITTLE
body: Sometimes we feel so small! Sometimes we feel there is not much we can do.
This world has gotten so big and complex.
No wonder we feel small at times.
But a little key can open a BIG door.
The tiniest seed can grow into the mightiest tree.
And even a single candle can be seen a mile away in the midst of darkness.
Don’t underestimate the influence you have. Little is much, if God is in it!
affirmation: I know that I am strong in You, Lord, and there is nothing that we, together cannot do.
verse: “When I’m weak then I am strong. For God’s strength is made perfect in my weakness.” (2 Corinthians 12:9-10)

title: WE NEED EACH OTHER
body: Sometimes it’s hard to get along with people.
And some people can really be hard to get along with!
But it’s the people who are in your life right now that God wants to use to teach you the things that He wants you to learn.
That’s the main reason we are here on this earth: To learn to love.
And for this... we need each other!
affirmation: The people God put in my life are what I need to grow in grace and in the fruits of His Spirit.
verse: “Let us love one another, for love is of God.” (1 John 4:7)

title: THE REAL ENEMY
body: The world is getting to be a scary place: so many problems, so much evil.
But you can’t get rid of darkness by trying to beat it out.
The only way to get rid of darkness is by letting the light in.
The real enemy in this world is ignorance. The challenge is to enlighten men’s hearts and minds. That’s our battle.
And that battle will only be won with LOVE.
affirmation: I am a bearer of hope and love to a dark and dying world.
verse: “Darkness shall cover the earth, and gross darkness the people: but the Lord shall arise upon thee, and His glory shall be seen upon thee.” (Isaiah 60:2)

title: TO FORGIVE IS TO LOVE
body: Sometimes we are treated unjustly, and it’s hard to forgive.
But when we realize that we need to be forgiven for many things, it makes it easier to forgive others.
Only God can give us love to forgive.
Forgiveness isn’t a weakness. Forgiveness is a sign of TRUE strength!
affirmation: I forgive those who have mistreated me, because I know I also need to be forgiven for many things.
verse: “Be kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God has forgiven you.” (Ephesians 4:32)

title: MAKING SENSE
body: Most people place more importance on their cars, their careers, their family, fun, and friends than on God.
They enjoy eating, playing, traveling, and sleeping. They have time for TV, or fishing, drinking, and all kinds of things.
But they spend so little time reading the Bible. Most of them in fact never read it at all! No wonder their lives unravel.

No wonder people feel lost. It’s not surprising the sadness, hopelessness, and despair that so many experience. Man cannot find direction in life if he neglects the one compass that can guide his way.
God’s Word is as good for us today as it has ever been. Spend more time in it. Read it. Study it. Contemplate it.
And life will start making more sense to you!
affirmation: Your Word, O Lord, is my guiding light through this complicated maze of life.
verse: “His delight is in the Word of the Lord, and in His Word doth he meditate day and night.” (Psalms 1:2)

title: ONLY GOD UNDERSTANDS
body: People vote for the rich and famous to be their leaders. They vote for celebrities, lawyers, and even billionaires!
But how can the rich even begin to under- stand the plight of the poor and the needs of the average person?
No wonder nothing changes.
Only God understands. Only God cares. And only God can lead us all, each one of us along life’s path. God alone can fill our needs, give us love, and bring meaning to life.
Don’t look to man to solve your problems.
Look to God!
affirmation: I look to You, dear Heavenly Father, and depend on You for help and guidance.
verse: “It is better to trust in the Lord than to put confidence in men.” (Psalms 118:8)

title: YOU ARE WHAT YOU EAT
body: If we eat good food we will have strong, healthy bodies. But if we eat a lot of junk, we will pay for it down the line.
We BECOME what we eat.
The same holds true for us spiritually.
We should be careful about the music we listen to, the movies we watch, and the books we read.
If we read, listen to, and watch negative and depressing things, they will become a part of us. And we will reflect their negative values little by little.
But if we fill our eyes and ears with positive and uplifting things, they will have their good effect on us also over time.
We BECOME what we eat!
affirmation: I don’t waste my time on things that do not help me in a positive way.
verse: “A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth good fruits, and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth evil fruits. For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.” (Luke 6:45)

title: JESUS IS ALL ABOUT LOVE
body: Did you know that many of the things religions teach us nowadays never even came
from Jesus and His teachings? In fact, Jesus’ entire message can be summed up in one word: LOVE.

He taught us to love God, put God first in our lives, and to love our neighbor, those around us. This is Jesus’ simple message. And it is as good for us today as it was for people back then in His day.
Jesus isn’t religion. Jesus is love. And God’s ONLY LAW is love.
Imagine what the world would be like if everyone only lived by Jesus’ SIMPLE rule!
affirmation: I know I don’t need to worry about keeping a whole bunch of rules. I just need to be loving.
verse: “All the law is fulfilled in one thing: Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.” (Galatians 5:14)

title: SOME DAY
body: Everything God does is done with love.
Sometimes He gives us something, and sometimes He takes something away from us. But whatever He does, He does it with love; even though sometimes at the moment we don’t understand what He is doing.
His ways are not our ways.
Only God sees ahead. Only He knows the entire plan. Only God is wise.
And we would be wise to trust Him and believe that He knows best: that ALL things are for our good, eventually.
Some day we WILL see that He was right.
Some day we will see the happy ending.
Some day we will see His love in the things that we do not NOW understand.
affirmation: I never regret having put my trust in Him.
verse: “If you give good things to your children, how much more will your Heavenly Father give good things to them that ask Him?” (Matthew 7:11)

title: EAT THAT ELEPHANT!
body: How do you know you can’t do something, if you’ve never done it?
We have learned that you can do pretty much anything, if you just do it ONE step at a time. Like someone said, “How do you eat a whole elephant? One bite at a time!”
Whenever you feel like you can’t do something, remember, it’s probably because of the limitations you yourself create. Just the fact that you’ve never done something doesn’t mean you can’t do it.
So next time you want to do something, and you are not sure if you’ll be able to do it, remember that if we are working with God, anything is possible!
You can tackle any *elephant*, any big job, any thing that seems impossible.
If you just do it...
One step at a time!
affirmation: I can do anything, because I walk hand in hand with God.
verse: “All things are possible to him that believeth!” (Mark 9:23)

title: AS SURE AS THE LAW OF GRAVITY
body: Some people just don’t seem to get it.
It seems like many haven’t figured out that before you can harvest, you first have to plant.
We can only reap what we have first sown. And if we want to receive abundantly, we first have to be generous in our planting.
Yet many people live like paupers here on Earth, spiritually, emotionally, as well as materially, because they expect to receive without ever giving.
The law of gravity says, “What goes up must come down.” And God’s rule of giving is simple: What goes around comes around.
Our lives are like mirrors. If you want to receive...
You’re going to have to start by GIVING!
affirmation: I give of what I have generously and unselfishly.
verse: “Give, and it shall be given unto you... With the same measure that ye give it shall be measured to you again.” (Luke 6:38)

title: LIKE HIM
body: Why did God put us here? To learn to be like Him! And what is God like? God is LOVE. God is the very Spirit and essence of love.
We are here on Earth to learn to be like Him.
God never forces us. He waits for us with open arms, and is never judgmental or condemning. And He wants us to reflect Him and to show this type of love to others.
Some day when we are finally reunited with God in our heavenly home, we will see that His love for us here on earth was unconditional.
Until then...
Let’s try to be more like HIM!
affirmation: I let You shine through me, Lord, so that I may be a blessing to many.
verse: “Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works and glorify your Father which is in Heaven.” (Matthew 5:16)

title: FREEDOM FROM BITTERNESS
body: Some people hold grudges over petty things. And they hold them for years! They seem to think they will accomplish something by doing so. But that’s not true.
Holding on to a grudge is actually pride and self-righteousness. You feel you were treated wrong. You deserve better. You are not like the other guy. You are “superior”!
But grudges and bitterness, no matter how we paint them can never bring us any good. Someone once said, “Bitterness only hurts the vessel in which it is stored.” We get nowhere by holding grudges.
God wants to help you. God can set you free from the things that hold you down.
But you’ve got to ASK HIM to!
affirmation: I have no time for grudges or bitterness in my life.
verse: “Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamor, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice.” (Ephesians 4:31)

title: THE DISTANCE
body: Someone once said, “How far away God seems to you is a measure of the distance that YOU have put between yourself and God.”
God isn’t far away. It’s US who keep Him at arms length. Some people feel that God is far away because they are holding on to something that He wants them to give up.
It may be some character trait or bad habit that they need to leave behind. And then there are others that may not feel worthy of God and His love due to something they have done in the past.
But God is always there, and He is only waiting for you to let go of the things that are keeping you down, if you will just come to Him with a believing heart.
Seek Him in prayer. Get quiet before God. And He WILL answer!
affirmation: I know that You hear me, Lord, and I thank You for being right beside me in everything I do.
verse: “Draw nigh to God, and He will draw nigh to you.” (James 4:8)

title: FOREVER
body: Lately they’re talking more and more about trying to make people live longer, increasing life expectancy.

Some people are claiming that the day is not far away when we could live for hundreds of years and maybe never even die. We will actually be immortal, live forever, or close to it. Some people are even freezing their bodies until that day comes.
My God, I don’t want to stay here in this world for all eternity!
I believe we are here on a mission, that God gave each of us the opportunity to come to earth that we might grow spiritually.
We were not meant to stay here forever, but to fulfill our mission and then go back home to be with the Lord.
There, we WILL live forever!
affirmation: I am here to learn lessons of love, forgiveness, and obedience to my dear Heavenly Father, and in His time He will take me home.
verse: “Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him.” (1 Corinthians 2:9)

title: ARE YOU BUSY?
body: I read about a man whose company is worth three billion dollars, and he answers every one of the 200 emails he receives every day. Imagine that!
He says he gets up at 4 am to do it. He fi that if someone takes the time to write him, the least he can do is write ‘em back an email in response. How cool is that!

No doubt most of the people who email him receive just a line or two which he probably “copies and pastes” into the email, but at least he sends them something! Instead of snubbing them he acknowledges them. With that kind of attitude no wonder he’s so successful!
We are ALL busy these days. None of us like to be ignored.
Jesus said, “Do unto others.” That’s not just only being considerate...
It’s also good business!
affirmation: I show respect to every person who I encounter, no matter what their station in life may be.
verse: “Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord.” (Romans 12:11)

title: STEPPING STONES
body: Someone told me the other day that she was not very lucky in love. She said that she had made some wrong choices in her life as far as relationships went.
Well, for a true believer in God, nothing happens by coincidence. Nothing happens by chance.
Everything in our lives, all the experiences, every relationship we have, both good and bad, are mere stepping stones to make us what God wants us to be; if we will but let Him.
Many things in life don’t turn out as we plan. But that doesn’t mean we have made bad choices.
Sometimes God gets His greatest victories out of seeming defeat. And we learn lessons of faith, humility, and trusting the Lord that way.
So no matter how things sometimes appear...
With God everything turns out ok in the end. ALWAYS!
affirmation: I know that all things that happen to me are ultimately for my good.
verse: “All things work together for good to them that love God.” (Romans 8:28)

title: CHRIST’S IMMINENT RETURN
body: Companies think nothing about deceptive advertising. Politicians are famous for not keeping their promises.
Religion, instead of bringing people together, is creating more divisions. Greed is widespread.
People only think of themselves.
In spite of all of man’s supposed higher education, the world is not getting better.
All these things are only signs of the times we are living in. Jesus said that before things can get better they will get worse.
Only Christ’s return will set things straight, right all wrongs, heal all hurt, and usher in a Kingdom of true righteousness based on God’s ONLY law of LOVE!
affirmation: I know everything is going according to God’s plan, and there will be a happy ending for us all.
verse: “As the waters cover the sea, so shall the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the Lord.” (Habakkuk 2:14)

title: LEAN ON HIM
body: People say they prayed, but God didn’t answer them.
It’s not that God didn’t answer: It’s just that the answer is on the way, and they haven’t seen it yet! It’s that simple.
God always answers prayer. His answer may not always be exactly what we have in mind, but the day will come when we will see that He knew better, He wanted the best for us, and that’s what we got!
God is trying to teach us to trust Him instead of our own natural reasoning. Don’t doubt.
He wants you to lean on Him.
affirmation: I know Your love is everlasting and I trust in You, dear Jesus.
verse: “Trust in the Lord with all thine heart and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge Him and He shall bring it to pass.” (Proverbs 3:5-6)

title: IF JESUS WERE HERE
body: What would you think of Jesus if He were here with us today?
Jesus didn’t have money.
Jesus had no college education.
He was homeless!
He hung around with drunks and prostitutes, and was rejected by the religious leaders of His day.
If we measure Jesus with the same parameters we measure success nowadays, He was a complete failure.
Jesus sure didn’t have much.
But just look at what He ACCOMPLISHED!
affirmation: You, O Lord, are the yardstick by which I measure everything in my life.
verse: “Be ye therefore followers of Christ, as dear children.” (Ephesians 5:1)

title: THE BEAUTY OF SILENCE
body: Isn’t the world getting noisy!
Some people seem to surround themselves with noise. They live with the radio blaring, or they have the television on all day long and even through the night while they sleep.
All that non-stop noise!
People go to the country, to a park, or to the beach, and they have to take their noise with them.
It seems like some people have forgotten the beauty of silence, the wind blowing through the trees or the waves on the sea shore. No wonder they have trouble hearing the voice of God.
God speaks in a calm, gentle voice. He whispers to your heart if you quiet your own spirit and truly want to hear.
But you’ll never hear God speaking if you live in a world of constant noise and confusion.
Get quiet before Him, and He WILL speak.
Hear ye Him!
affirmation: I get quiet before You, Lord, and wait until You speak to my heart.
verse: “Be still, and know that I am God.” (Psalms 46:10)

title: EAT, DRINK, AND BE MERRY?
body: If God and Heaven don’t exist and the Bible is a fairy tale, then it makes sense to get the most out of life in the way of food, fun, travel, and good times, as so many people do these days.

The spirit of this world is all about having fun, making money, enjoying life, and being cool. Yet reality for most people is one of working, struggling, coping, surviving. The good life they see on TV is like an apple that is perpetually dangled in front of their nose: ever so close, yet never fully attained.
If this world is not the end, if there IS an afterlife, then we would do well to heed Jesus’ advice to not live just for a “me first” feel-good life style and start thinking more of others.
This present world is temporary. Only what we do in kindness for others will last.
affirmation: I’m just passing through this world. I have my sights set on Heaven.
verse: “We look not at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen: for the things which are seen are temporal; but the things which are not seen are eternal.” (2 Co. 4:18)

title: THEY DON’T LISTEN
body: Most people don’t listen nowadays.
As you get older you try to share your knowledge, your experience, your wisdom with your children. But they don’t listen either.
They pretend to, but they really don’t. And this can be frustrating.
It’s natural to want to help them. But the things we consider useful and valuable, the things we have learned over the years, each new generation will have to learn for themselves.
We have to realize this and let them.

We need to pray for these we would help. We have to pray they will learn the lessons of life, hopefully without too much grief. We have to commit them to the Lord and remember that He loves them even more than we do.
We can’t help them not make mistakes.
But as they say, “A wise man learns from his mistakes.” Let’s pray that our children do too!
affirmation: I trust that God is working in the lives of my children, and I commit them to Him in prayer.
verse: “The prayer of a righteous man accomplishes much.” (James 5:16)

title: TUNNEL VISION
body: We all go through hard places. We all have difficulties at times. Sometimes everything around us seems dark, and we can barely see one step ahead of us.

We go through “tunnels” in life, hard places. Some are short. Others are longer. But if you think about it, usually nothing very bad (or very good!) ever lasts very long! And somehow, over time, things have a funny way of working themselves out.
Sure, it’s hard at the time. It’s not easy to stay positive when everything is coming down around your ears.
But, if you have Jesus by your side, the light is always shining at the end of the tunnel.
ALWAYS!
affirmation: I know that You, Jesus, will lead me through every storm of life.
verse: “Though I walk in the midst of trouble, Thou wilt revive me.” (Psalms 138:7)

title: IT MAKES NO SENSE
body: There’s no logic in faith.
Faith is the most illogical thing there is. Because faith is believing in an invisible God, and following Him even when you don’t understand what He is doing.
You trust the Lord, because you know He loves you. You know He sees the path ahead and will take care of you no matter what.
With God seeing isn’t believing: BELIEVING is seeing!
That’s love. That’s trust. That’s faith. It makes no sense. But it works!
So don’t worry about having to try and figure everything out. You don’t always have to understand. God is the one who has to understand. And He does. He loves you.
Stay CLOSE to Him.
affirmation: I don’t need to understand everything, Lord, to know You love me and watch over me.
verse: “For we walk by faith and not by sight.” (2 Corinthians 5:7)

title: THE POWER OF WEAKNESS
body: These days we hear about people who want to empower themselves.
We see products, pills, plans, and philosophies that promise to empower us, make us feel better, stronger.
Exercise is good for all of us of course, but aside from that, we as believers are not here to empower ourselves. We are here to glorify the LORD.
It is when we feel weak, when we feel incapable and insufficient, that God has an opportunity to work. This is one of the main lessons for a real Christian.
It is not about us empowering ourselves. It is about learning to tap into His power in our lives.
And His power can only shine through when we get out of the way...
And let HIM work!
affirmation: I let You use me for Your glory dear Lord, and know I am nothing of myself.
verse: “When we are weak then are we strong, because HIS power is made perfect in our weakness.” (2 Corinthians 12:9-10)

title: THE MOST IMPORTANT THING
body: The secret to power, faith, anointing, and light... is the Word.
It is God’s Word that gives us strength for the trials. It is in reading the Bible that we find the help and guidance that we seek.
If you are reading it daily, if you are memorizing verses that stand out to you and praying for God to work in your life, He will!
Take time for God.
MAKE time for His Word!
affirmation: I make time daily for Your Word. Through it You lift my soul and enlighten my way.
verse: “Study to show thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.” (2 Timothy 2:15)

title: THE POWER WE HAVE
body: We all have influence.
We influence the lives of those around us.
And the people we surround ourselves with influence our lives as well.
No man is an island. Each of our lives is bound to influence others, like it or not.
We can make lives, or we can shatter them with our words and with our actions. We all have more power than we realize.
May God help us to use that power to inspire, encourage, and motivate those around us...
For GOOD!
affirmation: I am a reflection of You, Lord, and Your love, patience, and goodness.
verse: “No man lives to himself, and no man dies to himself.” (Romans 14:7)

title: WHAT GOD IS REALLY LIKE!
body: Too many people today promote a religion of a keeping of rules. But that’s not what God wants.
God is our loving Creator who wants us to be happy. He has given us many blessings and pleasures to enjoy.
God is compassionate. He knows our trials and tribulation and always comforts us.
God is our best friend. He is always there for us and will never leave our side!
MOST of all... God is the very spirit of love.
He is more concerned with us showing love to each other than keeping any set of rules.
THAT is what God is REALLY like!
affirmation: Thank You Lord for making it so easy. All You ask for is our love.
verse: “I am come that they might have life and that they might have it more abundantly.” (John 10:10)

title: SOMEONE SPECIAL
body: Is there someone special in your life? Someone who accepts you no matter what?
A friend like that is something to be cherished.

Someone that you feel relaxed with. You feel at home in their presence. Someone that you can talk to. Someone that understands you. Someone that knows everything about you, and loves you anyway. A friend who is happy to see you, sad when you leave, and patient until you return.
Sounds like Jesus doesn’t it?
Do you know someone like this? Aren’t you blessed.
May we ALL be this kind of special friend!
affirmation: I am grateful for my friends and the people who love me and care about me.
verse: “A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.” (Proverbs 17:17)

title: IN CLASS NOW
body: Death is not the end. Death is only the beginning.
This world is our classroom. And we each voluntarily came here to this life on a personal and individual mission to learn the lessons that are unique to each of us.
We chose to come here of our own free will, for our spiritual enrichment and for our own good. And when we have learned all the things that God wants us to learn, He will take us home.
We will have graduated and we will then move on to even greater things, because Heaven is not the end, Heaven is only the beginning!
We will never stop learning, even right through Eternity.
How exciting is that!
affirmation: Thank you Lord for such fun things to look forward to, no only in this world but also in the world to come.
verse: “Get wisdom, get understanding: forget it not; neither decline from the words of My mouth.” (Proverbs 4:5)

title: GOT A MINUTE?
body: You wake up in the morning, and what’s the first thing you do?
Most people jump right into their day.
But if you will just take a few minutes to seek the Lord, if you will make time to talk to Him, you will find that your days will go a lot better.
And if, throughout your day you take little pauses to touch base with the Lord, you will not only have greater peace, but you will also be more productive.
God is more willing to give than we are to receive. But He never forces anybody. God doesn’t guilt trip anybody.
He waits for us patiently, and is always there to respond to our needs, if we will but let Him.
Start the day off right...
By first seeking the Lord!
affirmation: I put first things first and seek God in prayer every morning before starting my day.
verse: “My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.” (Psalms 5:3)

title: GOD’S MYSTERY GAME
body: People think that serving the Lord is drudgery and hardship and abstinence, and “Oh no, having to pray” having to “read the Bible” and whatever.

But that’s not it. It’s loving Jesus, and letting Him lead and guide our lives. And that’s like a mystery. It’s kind of like a computer game, where maybe you go a little way, and then you say, “Oh oh, I’m lost. I don’t know what to do. I need a clue now.” So you stop, you get a clue and continue the game.
Serving the Lord is like an adventure, with Him showing us the path of life each step. And there are little treasures along the way, rewards and things.
It’s like a big puzzle and we’re detectives with our magnifying glasses looking for clues.
Yes, the Lord loves a mystery. He likes to see us trying to figure things out, and seeking His leadings and guidance. He is there for us throughout our lives just waiting to assist us any way He can.
And with His help, we will win the game!
affirmation: I enjoy discovering Your plan for me, Lord. Thank You for being with me every step of the way.
verse: “Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the Kingdom of God.” (Mark 4:11)

title: IT’S RELATIVE
body: You see others who have more than you, especially on TV, and it is hard to not be tempted to complain. Sometimes you feel like you are missing out on something.
But then again if you look around, it isn’t hard to see many who have it a lot worse than you. It’s all relative.
That’s why we shouldn’t compare ourselves with others. There will always be people who have more, and there will always be those who have less.
Let’s be grateful for what we have and thank God for our many blessings.
God never fails to take care of His own when we put Him...
FIRST in our lives!
affirmation: I am mightily blessed and have a lot to be grateful for.
verse: “Be content with such things as ye have.” (Hebrews 13:5)

title: LOVE GOD?
body: Many people say they love God. Many people say He is first in their lives. And that is fine, but many people are only looking to receive.
Every one of God’s children should be asking themselves: What am I doing to get out the Word? What am I doing to share God’s message with those around me?
If we are not actively sharing His Word, we are failing God, and we are failing others. Because that’s what we are here for, to spread His gospel of hope and love.
The true message of Christ was not one of receiving, but one of giving.
He gave His all to us.
Should we not do the same for Him?
affirmation: I share Your message of love with those around me.
verse: “Freely ye have received. Freely give.” (Matthew 10:8)

title: HOW DEEP!
body: Aren’t people interesting?
Some people are really active. They are always in motion. Others are laid back. Nothing seems to bother them.
Some people talk a lot. Others talk very little.
Some have great ambitions. They are going places. And some are perfectly content where they are.
It’s beautiful how different we all are. We can all learn something from everyone we meet.
Don’t forget, we were all made in God’s image.
Think about it:
How deep is THAT!
affirmation: I appreciate the differences in every person I meet, and learn something from each one.
verse: “God has given everyone different gifts, but they all come from His same Spirit.” (1 Corinthians 12:4)

title: GOD ISN’T A MONSTER
body: Do you know why many people don’t want anything to do with God nowadays?
Because many people paint God out to be a cruel tyrant that sends people to hell if they don’t keep their set of dumb and inflexible rules.
But that’s not God.
Jesus didn’t teach us that to please God we had to observe a burdensome bunch of rules and prohibitions. God isn’t a monster.
God wants you to be happy, and He wants you to enjoy life. So don’t worry about those who would condemn you and try to tell you how you should live. That’s between you and God.
After all, it’s God who you’re going to have to answer to, not them!
affirmation: I know You want me to be happy and loving, Lord, and not worry about trying to be so “good”.
verse: “Do good, love mercy, and walk humbly with Thy God.” (Micah 6:8)

title: ONLY A MOMENT
body: Taking time out from our normal activities and daily events to spend time with the Lord is never time wasted. God wants our time more than anything, and if we give Him that, He will bless it.

We should all have moments of reflection and seeking the Lord: Times when we ask God what areas we need to make progress in, what character faults we need to work on; times to let the Lord make us vessels more fi for His use. These should be times to take stock of our lives, apologize to anyone we’ve wronged, and seek forgiveness of those we’ve hurt.
Throughout the ages God’s men and women have found that no matter how busy they are, if they take time for the Lord, God will do His part and make them more productive in everything they do.
You never lose by putting God and His Kingdom first… Above all the other things we think we need to do.
affirmation: I don’t let anyone or anything take away from my time with You, Lord.
verse: “They that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength.” (Isaiah 40:31)

title: OLD AND LOVING IT
body: It’s funny how some people are afraid of growing old. They are so afraid of wrinkles and the extra pound here and there.
They spend huge sums of money on creams, procedures, potions, and things to retard aging. To try to look younger, they say. It’s sad.
We shouldn’t be afraid of growing old. We should welcome each new stage of our lives with dignity. Wrinkles are nothing to be ashamed of. To grow old gracefully is beautiful.
We will have that glow. We will have that gleam in our eyes, if we have grown in wisdom.
We can grow old and love it...
If we live in love and mature in the knowledge of God!
affirmation: I am happy and grateful to be where I am now in life and know that the best lies yet ahead.
verse: “Gray hair is a crown of glory if it be found in the way of righteousness.” (Proverbs 16:31)

title: IN THIS TOGETHER
body: We don’t need more division:
We need more love.
We need to realize that at the core we are all the same. We need to realize that the things we have in common are so much greater than the things that we tell ourselves make us different.
The world is being torn apart by strife, competition, and greed.
But it is only together in a spirit of mutual respect and kindness that we can ever hope to achieve the things we really want.
We are all here on this earth together.
And only by working TOGETHER with love, can we break down the walls!
affirmation: I look for the things I have in common with people and build upon them.
verse: “Let us follow after things which make for peace.” (Romans 14:19)

title: WHAT TO BELIEVE
body: Sometimes you don’t know what to believe.
Some say go this way, some say go the other. Some say do this. Some say do that. And yet others say do neither.
The line is getting more and more blurred between what’s good and what isn’t.
The gap is growing between what we used to consider right and acceptable, and what many consider right and acceptable nowadays.
Now we accept as normal things that we wouldn’t have just a few short years ago.
