This world is full of uncertainties, and if we don’t keep our eyes on the Lord and stay in constant communication with Him we are doomed to failure.
God is our rock. God is our refuge.
God never fails to lead and guide His children...
If we will but seek HIM!
affirmation: You are my rock, O Lord. On Thee I stand.
verse: “The eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and His ears are open unto their cry.” (Psalms 34:15)

title: CELEBRITY STATUS
body: Celebrities are so glorified in this world. They tell us every little detail about their lives: stupid things like what they put on their hot dogs, or when their cat’s feeling depressed. It’s ridiculous!
The excesses of celebrities are flaunted in our faces in magazines, movies, on television. It’s disgraceful how the press holds up many of these people as role models.
Some have contributed very little to the world. The only trait, the only defining “quality” some have is that of being a “celebrity”. And kids are growing up wanting to be like them! How tragic.
If you want fame, riches, and glory in this world, you’d better enjoy them while you can, because they won’t last: You can’t take them with you!
But if you have lived unselfishly for others here on Earth, in God’s eternal kingdom you will be remembered.
There, YOU will be a celebrity!
affirmation: I don’t want to be famous in this world. I want my reward in Heaven.
verse: “Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.” (Colossians 3:2)

title: HE CAME FOR LOVE
body: Jesus was crucified. A man who committed no crime whatsoever met with the most gruesome death, just because He exposed the decadent religious system of His day. Jesus, for His example of love, suffered at the very hands of those He was trying to save.

Jesus had no earthly possessions other than his tunic and sandals. He had no house or any place to lay his head at night. He had no college education, was not a learned doctor of the law, had no credentials from Rome nor any religious order of His day.

Yet His doctrine of love, with its one simple rule of “doing unto others as you want them to do unto you” transformed lives, created jealous enemies, set the captives free, shook up the world, and has continued to do so to this very day.

There is one name that is above every other name that has ever existed or ever will, and that is Jesus. There is one name that can set man free, one name that makes Satan tremble, and only one name through which we can solve all of man’s problems, win the world, and usher in the only kingdom of true righteousness, equality, justice, peace, and love for all.
Jesus came for love, lived and died for love. Jesus was God’s ultimate expression of love to us.

title: WHAT’S GOING ON?
body: People are slaves to their jobs and have little time for anything else.
Grocery stores are packed with products that only deliver chemicals and empty calories.
Entertainment has become little more than aggression and noise.
For many people religion has lost its meaning, and is little more than a flaky show.
Our lives are filled with people, but we have few meaningful relationships. We are being reduced to glitter and glitz, vanity and nothingness.
What’s going on?
We are living in the days Jesus talked about in Matthew chapter 24.
affirmation: We are living in exciting times, and I am thankful to be a part of it.
verse: “As in the days that were before the flood, so shall the coming of the Son of God be.” (Matthew 24:38-39)

title: THE VOCIFERATION PLAGUE
body: One of the main problems we have in the world today is people who spend their time being very vocal about every little thing they don’t like. Why are we giving them so much attention?

These vociferators will never be satisfied until the world and everything in it is to their liking. Until everyone and everything is like they think it should be, and that of course is never going to happen.
Often these people criticize others who are doing something, while they themselves are doing nothing, other than criticizing!
They are a leading cause of the problem. And they don’t even see it.
We need to ignore them and stop letting them call the shots and dictate how we all should live and think.
affirmation: I don’t pay any attention to the complainers who just want to criticize. I am too busy being part of the solution.
verse: “Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good and edifying that it may minister grace unto the hearers.” (Ephesians 4:29)

title: MONEY, MONEY, MONEY
body: For many people money seems to be the only yardstick. How sad.
“Got a problem?” Fix it with money.
“Want to be happy?” Get more money. That’s what people think.
Money sure is a poor way of keeping score.
Yet it is what the world worships. But God’s currency is all the good things that money can’t buy. Funny world isn’t it!
Money is a necessary evil. We all need some, if we don’t want to starve. We just have to keep it in its place and not let it control our lives.
Got money problems?
Ask God to help you. He will if you keep money in the right perspective.
The person who puts God above everything else in their life will never lack anything!
affirmation: Money is my servant. I do not permit it to be my master.
verse: “The love of money is the root of all evil.” (1 Timothy 6:10)

title: YOU ARE WHAT YOU THINK
body: Thoughts are real things.
They can either build up, or they can destroy. And negativity kills.
It kills initiative. It kills creativity.
That’s why it is so important to dwell on the positive, to speak positive.
We actually BECOME what we think!
affirmation: I have a positive attitude and see good and possibilities, because God is with me and everything is under control.
verse: “As a man thinks in his heart, so is he.” (Proverbs 23:7)

title: FEARLESS IN HIM
body: Have you noticed that most of the things we worry about never happen? Our fears are almost always over some future event: something that hasn’t even happened yet!
Fear is a crippler, and if we give way to our fears we will end up powerless, useless.
Fear is one of our spiritual enemy’s favorite weapons, and the only way he can defeat us is by tempting us to fear.
But God’s Word says to, “Cast our cares upon the Lord and He will sustain us.” (Psa. 55:2)
He will see us through. God tells us we are not to fear but to trust Him. His shoulders are broad enough to carry any load.
Fear not. Let God work in your life!
affirmation: I do not permit fear in my life, and know You are with me, Lord.
verse: “For God hath not given us a spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.” (2 Timothy 1:7)

title: THE DAY IS COMING
body: Sometimes it seems hard to believe.
Sometimes it just doesn’t seem possible.
But the day will come when love WILL triumph over greed, light will conquer the darkness, and fear will flee away.
A day when worries vanish, freedom reigns, and loneliness will be no more.
The mighty will be brought low.
And the meek, at last, shall inherit the Earth.
affirmation: I know that day is coming. I know it will most surely come.
verse: “Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth. Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled. Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.  Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.” (Matthew 5:5-8)

title: HOW LITTLE WE KNOW
body: Have you noticed that everywhere you look we are surrounded by “experts”?
It seems like many people think they have everything all figured out. Whatever it is, they
have the solution. They always seem to know what needs to be done.
You see people all around talking like they know the answers. They act as if they aren’t afraid of anything. But if they really know what they’re doing, if they really are so sure of themselves...
Why are they so afraid to die?
The older we get, the more we realize how little we really know, and how much we need to seek the Lord and His guidance daily in our lives.
We don’t have all the answers. Thank God He does!
affirmation: Thank You Lord. You have NEVER let me down.
verse: “They that seek the Lord understand all things.” (Proverbs 28:5)

title: TROUBLE SLEEPING?
body: “If I were rich I’d be happy.” That’s what many people think. They think their problems would be over. They really think they would be happier.
But it seems to me that the rich and famous have more than their share of problems.(1) People are out to get them. Everything they do is criticized. They are always living in fear.
When you’re rich your problems aren’t over.
King Solomon said, “The sleep of a laboring man is sweet, whether he eat little or much: but the abundance of the rich will not let him sleep.”(2)
He should know as he sure had it all! That’s why he said we should, “Seek neither poverty nor riches.”(3)
I’m grateful I’m not rich.
And I REALLY enjoy sleeping well at night!
affirmation: You are always faithful to supply all my needs, Lord.
verse: 1 Timothy 6:9 Ecclesiastes 5:12 Proverbs 30:8

title: LET GOD SOLVE YOUR PROBLEMS
body: We are all so busy these days.
Sometimes we feel overwhelmed with the demands our jobs, families, and even friends place on us.
But if we make an effort to seek the Lord’s strength, if we get quiet in prayer before Him, we will find that we will be more productive. We will end up getting more done.

We will be putting ourselves in a position where God can show us a different perspective to our problem, and a solution that we didn’t see on our own. Other times we find that when we make time for the Lord, our problems seem to have a way of solving themselves!
His ways are not our ways. To find His ways, we have to let Him have His way.
And the way we do that... is by stopping everything, and seeking Him!
affirmation: I stay out of your way Lord, and let You work through me.
verse: “Seek the Lord and His strength. Seek His face continually.” (1 Chronicles 16:11)

title: HOW WILL THEY KNOW?
body: There are so many of God’s children throughout the world. Some belong to one of the many, many churches and denominations that exist. Others do not.
Yet they all believe in the God of love. They have all accepted Jesus, and they are all fulfilling His will as they let Him lead and guide them through their lives.
Some people profess their faith outwardly.
Others are more discrete about theirs.

But Jesus said that the only way the world would know that we all are His children, the way the world would know that we have the Truth, would be when they SEE OUR LOVE, the love we have for each other.
There is no getting around it: We cannot say we love God but are not loving to each other.
“Beloved, let us love one another.”
affirmation: I am one of God’s children here on Earth and am grateful to be in service to our King.
verse: “By this shall all men know that ye are My disciples, if ye have love one to another.” (John 13:35)

title: BLESSED IN HIS SERVICE
body: We are blessed to have the Lord.
We are grateful that we can help the needy, pray for the sick, encourage people, and generally just be a blessing to others.
We see people who have a lot more than us, but that is only materially. Because we are blessed with having the Lord by our side.
We are blessed with His guidance, protection, and love.
What else could we wish for in this life?
What else could we wish for in the life to come?
We are indeed blessed.
affirmation: You have surpassed all my dreams, dear Lord.
verse: “Blessed be the Lord, Who daily loadeth us with benefits, even the God of our salvation.” (Psalms 68:19)

title: HAPPINESS
body: Some people are never happy, because they think things will make them happy. And they never have enough!
Some think that being with a particular person will bring them happiness.
And others place happiness in the future, as some future event or destination, one that is always somehow unattainable.
But happiness comes when you break free from that driving spirit of busyness and thinking you have to get somewhere, do something, or have something to be happy.
And it all begins with counting your blessings!
affirmation: I do not let people or external things affect the way I feel.
verse: “I have learned in whatever state I am, to be content.” (Philippians 4:11)

title: SPLIT DECISIONS
body: It takes a long time to build something.
Yet so much can be lost in just one moment of carelessness.
Whether it be the construction of a building, the execution of a project, or the preservation of a valued relationship, the repercussions of the smallest deed can have far reaching results.
That’s why we have to be careful and resist the temptation to do things, to say things in haste.
It’s easy to blow out a match...
But oh so difficult to put out the forest fire that tiny match can start!
affirmation: I walk circumspectly and am prayerful about everything I do and say.
verse: “Be not rash with thy mouth, and let not thine heart be hasty to utter anything... Let thy words be few.” (Ecclesiastes 5:2)

title: YOU ARE NOT ALONE
body: Have you noticed how depressing the news is?
If we believed half of what they tell us on TV we would all want to give up, jump off the nearest cliff, or live like a hermit in a cave!
But in spite of all of their negativity, exaggerated news stories, and sensationalism it is still a beautiful world. It just seems like they don’t want us to know about it.
So if you sometimes feel all alone, if you feel like all of your loving efforts are in vain...
Remember, there are a LOT of other people doing great and wonderful things for the world.
Cheer up.
You are NOT alone!
affirmation: I know my efforts are not in vain and will produce good fruit eventually.
verse: “One man plants, another man waters, but it is God who makes it grow.” (1 Corinthians 3:7)

title: TIME TO LISTEN
body: A lot of people today are starving just for someone to talk to once in a while. And they are blown away when they see that you are willing to stop everything and show them you care enough to listen.
Do you want the key to every heart? Do you want the secret to winning people’s favor? Stop trying to push your own agenda for a few minutes.
Stop trying to make people listen to you, and show some sincere interest in them. They too have their story. Let them speak their piece.
If you truly want to hear what they have to say, and you listen with an open mind as well as an open heart, they will not only feel that someone showed them God’s love that day...
But you may also even learn something yourself!
affirmation: I make time for people and always give them a listening ear.
verse: “Let every man be swift to hear and slow to speak.” (James 1:19)

title: THE GOD OF LOVE
body: I don’t believe in “religion”. I believe in love.

Man has waged war in the name of religion. Man has terrorized the world in the name of religion. Man has committed all kinds of heinous acts in the name of religion. And man has made a *god *out of religion!
But my God is love. My God is the very spirit of love. My God is not religion. Love is my God.
Only love has the power to heal hearts, mend wounds, lift up those in need, and free the oppressed. Only love can feed the poor, end all wars, and bring true peace to the world.
But it has to start with US!
affirmation: I believe that God’s love is the answer to all of man’s problems, past, present, and future.
verse: “This is the victory that overcometh the world: our faith.” (1 John 5:4)

title: ME OR OTHERS?
body: Do we go through our lives thinking only of ourselves? Or do we think of others?
It says in the Bible that all our deeds should be done in love, “in honor preferring one another.”
It takes a real effort to create a habit of thinking of others instead of just ourselves. And it’s not always easy.
As we go through this day, let’s make an effort to see what we can do for others, see who we can cheer up, see if there’s someone we can make happy.
Let’s be more loving. Let’s be more giving.
God help us to be a blessing today to those around us by putting their needs above our own...
And to not think of ourselves, but to think of others!
affirmation: I love others, because God loves them through me.
verse: “Beloved, if God so loved us, we ought also to love one another.” (1 John 4:11)

title: A SIMPLE PLAN
body: People often ask me:
What’s life all about? Or, Why are we here in this world?
I believe we are all here on a mission.
I believe we are all here to learn to love.
I believe that when we learn this lesson we will have grown spiritually, we will have accomplished our mission, and we will be called back home, back to the “mother ship”.
Simple plan now. Isn’t it!
affirmation: God is in control, and my life is unfolding according to His highest and most perfect will.
verse: “For ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a peculiar people; that ye should show forth the praises of Him who hath called you out of darkness into His marvelous light.” (1 Peter 2:9)

title: GOD’S BLESSINGS
body: People sure complain a lot these days.
You read about them in the news. You see them on television.
They complain about the government. They complain about the system. They complain about other people.
And some people even complain about things that happened hundreds of years ago, things that our forefathers did, as if we should somehow be held responsible for them today. How dumb is that!
Complaining is contagious. Negative people are self destructing.
There is only one way to be happy in this world, and that is to be grateful for all the good in your life.
And THANK GOD for your blessings!
affirmation: Thank you, dear God for being so good to me!
verse: “In everything give thanks for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.” (1 Thessalonians 5:18)

title: FOR A HAPPIER MARRIAGE
body: Communication in marriage is more than just talking and saying what you want or think.
Communication is also listening.
To listen, doesn’t only mean to hear with our ears, but to listen with our ears, hearts, and minds.
It means to put yourself in your spouse’s place, to really try and understand what they mean. To listen to their heart.
To be a good communicator we must first ask questions and be willing to change our viewpoint. We must be open to seeing things as others see them.
May God help us to be better communicators.
May God help us to see things with a Heavenly perspective...
And show His love to our mate by TRULY listening.
affirmation: I listen to my mate and show I really care!
verse: “To do good and communicate forget not, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.” (Hebrews 13:16)

title: IT’S EASY!
body: Praying is not hard.
Praying is talking to the Lord, and more importantly, listening to Him.
You can pray wherever you are throughout your day. You don’t have to go somewhere special or get down on your knees to be heard.
Sometimes you talk to God like a friend, maybe even out loud. Other times you can just turn your thoughts into a prayer. It doesn’t have to be an audible prayer.
Either way, God hears you. He knows. He cares.
If you place prayer high on your list of priorities...
God will place you high on HIS list of priorities!
affirmation: I talk to God like a friend, and He leads me and guides me throughout my day.
verse: “Seek ye the Lord while He may be found. Call ye upon Him while He is near.” (Isaiah 55:6)

title: BE BOLD
body: Be bold! Believe in love.
Be bold!
Believe in the miracles love can do.
In your life; in the lives of others.
Be bold enough to dare to try.
Be bold.
BELIEVE!
affirmation: I believe we can change the world with love. I believe it can be done!
verse: “If thou will believe, thou will see the glory of God.” (John 11:40)

title: CLOSE TO ME
body: Why does God allow natural disasters?
Some no doubt are the result of man creating an imbalance of nature through his pollution of the planet.
Others are for reasons not understood by our finite minds. And with all the other things going on in the world right now, life can get scary.
But one thing is for sure: God will see you through the storm. God will take care of you no matter what may come your way.
He is able to take care of His own, those who seek Him, those who put their trust in Him.
Between natural disasters, economic woes, and political uncertainty, we certainly are living in trying times.
But through it all God says,
“Stay close to Me.”
affirmation: I seek You and Your help, Lord, throughout my day.
verse: “He shall cover thee with His feathers, and under His wings shalt thou trust.” (Psalms 91:4)

title: MORE TO LIFE
body: We all like to have a good time. We all like to have fun.
But look back at your life. How many fun things and good times do you even remember anymore?
We remember only the most outstanding.
We really remember very few compared to all the good times we had.

The thrill of our sports team winning the big game was short lived. Our excitement over that blockbuster movie quickly faded. The delicious meal we devoured for lunch is scarcely a memory come dinner time. And that night out on the town? Long forgotten.
There’s more to life.
What we make out of it depends on us.
affirmation: I want the most out of life and don’t settle for just temporary thrills.
verse: “The world will pass away and its pleasures, but He that does the will of God will live forever.” (1 John 2:17)

title: HE GIVES THE VERY BEST
body: Sometimes we try to run ahead of God.
Sometimes we stubbornly seek something that is not His will.
But where God guides, God provides.
And if God is not supplying in some area or direction of our lives, maybe it is not His will!
Or maybe it is God’s will, but just not God’s timing.
When we let God choose, when we put God first and let Him direct our lives...
He always gives us the very best!
affirmation: I know God knows what’s best for me, and I let Him guide my life.
verse: “Man’s goings are of the Lord; how can a man then understand his own way?” (Proverbs 20:24)

title: TALK LESS, LISTEN MORE
body: Are you a “listener”? Or are you a “talker”?
When you talk:
You can’t learn anything.
You can’t hear from God.
You don’t grow.
When you listen:
You learn fun, useful things.
You hear from God, feel His presence in your life, and receive His blessings.
You grow in wisdom and understanding.
affirmation: I am going to talk less and listen more.
verse: “Let your speech be always gracious and pleasant, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.” (Colossians 4:6)

title: A BLESSING IN DISGUISE
body: It’s amazing how disasters and suffering bring out good things in people.
They make you grateful for your many blessings.
Things that you took for granted suddenly become special when faced with the possibility of losing them.
You realize loved ones are more important than your material possessions. Family and friends can’t be replaced.
It’s never easy, but suffering, in a funny way is good for us in that it makes us reassess our lives, look up to God, and resolve to be a better person.
Lord help us to remember these things during the good times also!
affirmation: I am grateful Lord. I am thankful for my many blessings.
verse: “Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.” (Ephesians 5:20)

title: HAHAHA!
body: Do you smile a lot? Do you smile ever?
People smile when they are happy. People smile when they are grateful.
Laughing is good too.
You know what they say, “Laugh and the world will laugh with you. Cry and you’ll cry alone.”
The Bible says that laughter does us good like a medicine.
Let’s all stop taking ourselves so seriously, learn to laugh more...
And enjoy this marvelous life that God has given us!
affirmation: Lord, fill me with Your joy, and let it shine through my countenance, that I may be an inspiration to others.
verse: “A merry heart doeth good like a medicine, but a broken spirit drieth the bones.” (Proverbs 17:22)

title: SEEKING JUSTICE
body: It seems like many people out there today are seeking justice.

You see them every day on TV. They work themselves up into a frenzy and often times resort to violence. They get on their own personal crusade to find justice for real, perceived, or imagined injustices.
People are becoming bitter and more and more narrow minded, judgmental, inflexible, condemning, and unloving.
Growing hard is a choice. And letting life’s circumstances and injustices harden your heart is not God’s solution.
You can’t right all of man’s wrongs. But you can do the right thing in your life.
Ask God to give you a tender heart. Ask God to help you be a vessel of His love...
To heal, and not to hurt.
affirmation: Make me an instrument of Your peace, Lord.
verse: “Where there be hate, let me bring love. Where there be discord, let me bring harmony. Where there be despair, let me bring hope.” (St. Francis of Assisi)

title: THIS LITTLE LIGHT OF MINE
body: The flame of one little candle can be seen a mile away in the middle of the night.
Don’t ever feel that because there is so much darkness your little flame will not make a difference. A tiny seed doesn’t grow into a big tree overnight. But it will eventually.
Every bit of love we give, every kind word, every good deed we do will bring forth good fruit some day.
We will harvest the things we plant.
Light will brighten the darkness, and love will conquer all men’s hearts.
That day WILL come!
affirmation: I know that the love I give will come back to me some day. I know my efforts are not in vain.
verse: “So shall My Word be that goes forth out of My mouth: it shall not return unto Me void... it shall prosper in the thing whereto I sent it.” (Isaiah 55:11)

title: A REAL COMPANION
body: Studies show a tiny baby needs to be held to be healthy. Babies need human contact, warmth, and love.
We as adults are no different. We all need to be held and cuddled, yet many are too embarrassed to admit it.
People are starving for love. People are starving for that same human warmth. So many people are lonely.
God can supply all your needs, including a companion. But you have to put God first in your life. And then, wait for someone who is doing the same thing while they are waiting for you.
God will bring the two of you together in His time!
affirmation: The Lord is my everything, and I have put Him in first place in my life.
verse: “No good thing will He withhold from them that walk uprightly.” (Psalms 84:11)

title: BUSY ALL THE TIME
body: A lot of people stay busy. They are always working, going, rushing, doing. They rarely take a break and would have you believe that God wants them to be that way.
But all of this endless motion isn’t of God.
Instead of getting closer to Him they are keeping Him away with all of their frantic self effort.
God lives in the quiet place, the still, and the calm. And to get closer to Him we have to cease from our own labor, our own works, and all the noise.
God doesn’t force anyone.
You’ve got to stop everything, get calm, and come before Him with an open mind and a yielded spirit. And stop being so busy all the time!
When you do, you will find the guidance and direction that you seek in your life.
affirmation: I find peace and direction in my life when I slow down and take more quiet time with God.
verse: “Better is a handful with quietness, than both the hands full with travail and vexation of spirit.” (Ecclesiastes 4:6)

title: LIKE JESUS
body: Pride and arrogance are great sources of stress and tension. They also contribute to all kinds of health issues.
Jesus was a wise man. He had the solution to all of man’s problems, yet He was humble. Jesus was right. But He wasn’t arrogant about it.
Lord help us to be humble and realize that without You we are nothing. Lord help us to be a blessing to those around us.
Lord help us to be more like YOU!
affirmation: I want to be more like You, dear Jesus.
verse: “Come unto Me and learn of Me for I am meek and humble and ye shall find rest for your souls.” (Matthew 11:28-29)

title: A FUN LIFE
body: Serving the Lord is not a life of hardships and sacrifice. It is a life of joy, gratitude, blessings, and miracles! It really is an easier life in many ways.
The whole image of serving God portrayed throughout history as being a life of drudgery, sacrifice, pain, and doing without is simply not true.
It is a life where the Lord shows you the best choices and helps you make good decisions. The wisest decisions. And it’s fun!
It is a life where God’s there to protect you, guide you, and help you in every facet of your life.

From simple grocery shopping to making life decisions concerning a mate, a career, or a house purchase, serving God and putting Him first in your life is a never ending journey of miracles, as He leads you every step of the way.
No other life is worth living.
No other life could be more FUN!
affirmation: Thank You for this incredibly interesting life you have given me!
verse: “The Lord hath done all things well.” (Mark 7:37)

title: FOR A REASON
body: All of us seek a lasting relationship. But things often don’t turn out as we hope.

Many people feel they made a mistake, because they got involved in a relationship that didn’t last more than a few months or years. There’s a prevalent attitude that if a relationship doesn’t last for a lifetime, then it was a mistake. People blame themselves.
They see their unrealistic romantic expectations dashed when a relationship doesn’t play out as they hoped and suff	great emotional stress as a result.
Everyone comes into our lives for “a reason, a season, or a lifetime” and every relationship can be a stepping stone, and serve as a lesson to prepare us for greater things, if we will allow it.
May we be grateful for all the experiences we have in this life and not let seeds of regret choke out the gardens of our hearts.
Love purely. Live unselfishly. Do good.
And look forever forward!
affirmation: I know that everything in my life has been for my good, and I don’t look back with regrets.
verse: “The path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.” (Proverbs 4:18)

title: HEALTHIER AND HAPPIER
body: The best way to prevent illness is to obey God’s natural laws: Live right, eat right, work right, play right, rest right, love right, and maintain a right relationship with Him.
Do what you can, and God will do what you can’t.
So work on the ounce of prevention now, rather than worrying about the pound of cure later.

Follow these common sense rules for good health by taking care of His Creation and giving it the proper maintenance its Creator knows that it needs. Observe these things as best you can, and they will help you be healthier and happier.
God bless and keep you in good health!
affirmation: I make a real effort to eat healthy and find a balance in all aspects of my life.
verse: “Beloved, I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health.” (3 John 2)

title: SHADES OF GRAY
body: People nowadays are getting more and more extreme.
Not everything in life is so right or wrong, all good or all bad. And focusing on our differences is only dividing us.
Some things in life are not so clear cut, not so black or white, but more often than not one of many shades of gray.

Many things are relative, and sometimes it is only if we look at things from different perspectives that we can truly see the entire picture. We each have our opinion. We all have our story. We all have our individual point of view.
Lord help us to be more open, more loving in our dealings with people. Lord help us to put ourselves in the other guy’s shoes and try to relate to him.
And realize there is often more to things than meets the eye!
affirmation: I am understanding of others, slow to judge, and slow to speak.
verse: “Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.” (John 7:24)

title: ONLY LOVE
body: Nations pressure other nations into doing what they want.
Companies pressure us into buying their products.
Fear, threats, and intimidation are common tactics people use to get their way.
People get into romantic relationships and then feel disillusioned, because the other person doesn’t live up to their expectations.
Everyone wants things and they even get mad when they don’t get them. No wonder people are miserable. Greed and selfishness are killing us.
It’s time we woke up.
Only LOVE will set us free.
affirmation: I am loving to others. I know that love is the answer!
verse: “If the Son therefore shall make you free, you shall be free indeed.” (John 8:36)

title: THE ROAD TO GOD’S BLESSINGS
body: We all have so many things to be grateful for, and God is so good to those who let Him and His love rule in their lives. How could there ever be room for any of us to complain?

If we are not grateful for what God has already given us, He’s sure not going to feel like giving us much more. And if we murmur and complain long enough, He may even take away the blessings we already have. Boy, then we’ll have something to whine about!
The Bible says, “In EVERYTHING give thanks.”
The surest way to keep God’s blessings flowing is by having a grateful attitude, counting our blessings, and thanking God continually for them.
Remember, “Keep your eyes on the donut and not on the hole!”
affirmation: I am grateful. I am thankful. I will not complain. I am truly blessed!
verse: “Oh magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt His name together.” (Psalms 34:3)

title: LIKE A ROCK
body: Life is full of surprises.
And this world is full of uncertainties.
The uncertainty of work, financial uncertainty, the uncertainty of relationships...
The dynamics of life can change suddenly, and things can catch you by surprise!
This world is shifting sand.
Only God and His love will remain forever true.
And only God can guide you through the storm.
affirmation: I know You are true, Lord. I know You will never leave me or forsake me.
verse: “Trust in the Lord and do good. So shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.” (Psalms 37:3)

title: DO IT TODAY
body: When people find themselves in danger they immediately think of their family and loved ones.
They wish they could tell them, “I love you.” They wish they could hold them. They regret having done some things, having said some things.
Tragedy has a funny way of putting things in the right perspective.
Don’t wait for your world to get scary to be kind to others.
Show them your love. Show them you care. Seek forgiveness. Show them how special they are to you.
Do it today.
Tomorrow may be too late!
affirmation: I show my love to those that I love and who love me.
verse: “Blessed is he that readeth, and they that hear the words of this Book, and keep those things which are written therein: for the time is at hand.” (Revelation 1:3)

title: TRUE BEAUTY
body: A lot of people today suffer from lack of self-esteem. And this is only made worse by movies and television that make people feel they have to be as physically attractive as Hollywood celebrities and the rich and famous.
But we can be set free from that bondage and the pressure that makes us feel we have to be like everyone else.
True beauty is more than just physical appearance.
A person with love in their heart that cares for others will always be attractive. This inner beauty will always be found irresistible to the opposite sex.
A loving person will never be a lonely person.
Ask God to give you great love for others, and you will be more beautiful than ever.
If you give love, you’ll get love!
affirmation: The closer I get to You, Lord, the more attractive I am to others.
verse: “A meek and quiet spirit is in the sight of God of great price.” (1 Peter 3:4)

title: GET OUT OF THE WAY
body: We all seem to be under so much pressure these days:
Pressure at work. Pressure at school. Pressure at home.
So we look for relief in food, in drink, in music, drugs, or even violence! It’s enough to drive anyone crazy.
The secret is: to give up!
Ultimately a lot of the pressure in our lives is caused by trying to control things, situations, and people; by insisting on having things our way.
But relief and real freedom come when we give up, when we stop trying to control things that are beyond our control.
God only has the opportunity to show His greatness when we surrender...
When we get out of the way!
affirmation: I surrender my all to You, Lord, and let You work in my life.
verse: “Casting all your care upon Him, for He careth for you.” (1 Peter 5:7)

title: TODAY IS THE DAY
body: We should all appreciate this life we have as we’re living it.
There are so many things we will look back on and realize how good we had it.
So what keeps us from enjoying life now? What keeps us from being grateful today?
Our spirit of busyness keeps us down. Our negative mindsets keep us from enjoying life.
Today is the day we were waiting for!
Let’s be grateful for what we have right now. Let’s be grateful that we don’t have it worse. Let’s be grateful for our many, many blessings.
And enjoy life and what we have... today!
affirmation: I take time to enjoy life and everyone in it and am grateful for what I have today.
verse: “Let all those that put their trust in Thee rejoice. Let them ever shout for joy. Let them that love Thy name be joyful in Thee.” (Psalms 5:11)

title: LET IT GO
body: Have you been treated unfairly? Has someone accused you falsely? Been mean to you?
Do you have trouble forgetting it and leaving it behind?
Sometimes it seems it can’t be done, but you must let it go. Until you do, it will only eat away at you.
Bitterness will hurt you more than it will hurt them. Let it go.
Don’t do it for them. Do it for yourself.
Experience the freedom that comes from truly giving it to the Lord. Freedom from the enslaving spirit of bitterness. Freedom from the bondage of holding on to the injustice.
The day will come when all of life’s wrongs will be made right.
Your day will come.
But until then, you must learn to...
LET IT GO.
affirmation: Lord, I put these things in Your hands. Help me to be more like You.
verse: “The Lord is longsuffering, and of great mercy, forgiving iniquity and transgression.” (Numbers 14:18)

title: HOW CAN YOU KNOW?
body: People often wonder what life is all about.
Well, to a large degree life is about learning to choose between good and evil.
What does that even mean?
In this upside down world how can we know when something is good, or something isn’t?
So many things are subject to interpretation. People’s perceptions can vary a lot and languages, customs, time, and context also come into play.
So how can you know if something is good, or if it’s bad? In God’s eyes, it all gets down to love.
If your actions are kind and unselfish, if what you do is motivated by love, then it is good.
And if not... then it is bad.
Pretty simple!
affirmation: I live a life of love toward God and share His love with those around me.
verse: “He that loveth his brother abideth in the light.” (1 John 2:10)

title: WHAT ARE YOU MADE OF?
body: Music is powerful.
Music has a real effect on people, more than they realize.
Some music inspires and excites, and motivates you in a positive way. It is soothing, brings you peace, and makes you feel good.
But some music can really stir you up with hateful and belittling lyrics. It sows discord, strife, and makes you tense and frustrated, even violent!
So watch out what you listen to.
We actually turn into the things we expose ourselves to over time.
Whether it be the food we eat, the movies we watch, or the music we listen to...
We become the sum total of these things!
affirmation: I am prayerful about the things I watch, the things I read, and the music I listen to.
verse: “He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: but a companion of foolishness shall be destroyed.” (Proverbs 13:20)

title: FOOLISH CHRISTIANS
body: I see many dumb Christians out there making such fools of themselves, no wonder a lot of people don’t want anything to do with Jesus, the Bible, or God.
There are people who say they are Christians bombing abortion clinics saying God told them to. There are churches preaching racism.

A lot of people preach abstinence from everything pleasurable, which they paint as sin. Many Christians’ entire service to God has been reduced to the furtherance of their individual political stance or ideology, instead of furthering the gospel.
And the so-called Christian world is fragmented and divided with their fussing, fighting, and pet doctrines.
These things do not draw people to the Lord.
They push them away from Him.
Oh foolish Christians...
What’s it gonna take?!
affirmation: I spread God’s message of love and light, and stay away from fussing, fighting, and division.
verse: “That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God.” (Romans 15:6)

title: ABOVE ALL ELSE
body: I don’t go to church.
I don’t see anything wrong with going to church. I just don’t feel the need. That could change some day. Who knows?
There are many, many people in this world who love God and are being used by Him as vessels of His love. Some go to church. Others don’t.
I believe that the most important thing is to put God first, and to be loving to those around us.
I don’t condemn anyone for going to church and I believe no one has the right to condemn someone for NOT going!
Jesus said, the most important thing, above all else is...
To LOVE one another!
affirmation: It is not my place to judge my neighbor. Only to love my neighbor.
verse: “For all the law is fulfilled in this: Thou shalt love.” (Galatians 5:14)

title: YOU CAN’T BUT GOD CAN
body: God is loving and merciful, and He will never give you a load too big for you to bear. Jesus said, “My yoke is easy, and My burden is light.” And, “Come unto Me, and I will give you rest.”
So if you feel overburdened with something, maybe it’s not God’s will.
Or maybe you are taking too much upon yourself and trying to do things in your own strength instead of letting God do it through you with His power, His love, His grace, and His strength.
“His yoke is easy, and His burden is light.” It’s not difficult and it’s not unbearable. So quit trying so hard!
Rest in the Lord and trust in Him. Let Him work and He will do it through you.
Try it. Put Him to the test!
affirmation: I rest in the Lord and let Him carry my load.
verse: “My presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest.” (Exodus 33:14)

title: REMEMBER THE FARMER
body: A farmer plants his seeds. But he doesn’t see any fruits right away.
He has to water them. He has to nurture them. He has to protect them from bugs, plagues, and diseases.
He has to be patient, very patient. And if he doesn’t give up, he will eventually be rewarded. He will see the fruits of his labor.
Everything in life costs something.
Few things are really free. They cost someone something.
So if you want your dream to come true, remember the farmer. You are going to have to work for it...
And you’re going to have to have a LOT of patience!
affirmation: I do what I can, and the Lord always does for me what I what I can’t.
verse: “If you want your dream to be, take your time, go slowly. Small beginnings, greater ends. Heartfelt work is holy.”

title: FRUSTRATED?
body: Life can be frustrating. And people can be especially frustrating. Downright annoying!
What can be done about it?
One solution is to move to some remote location, find an uninhabited cave, and live in it all alone!
But let’s be honest with ourselves: Most of us wouldn’t survive very long in such inhospitable surroundings... At least not without the Internet!
So the only other option most of us have, short of murder, which I do not advise, is to stop whining, be brave, and BE the EXAMPLE of love, patience, and tolerance we expect others to be!
If we do, God will bless us!
affirmation: I am grateful for the people in my life, even when they try my patience.
verse: “Be ye therefore followers of God as dear children.” (Ephesians 5:1)

title: WHAT DOES GOD WANT?
body: For thousands of years people in the name of religion have told us “what God wants” of us!
They tell us all kinds of *rules *that God supposedly wants us to keep. Yet they can’t even make up their mind as to what those rules are!
Many paint God as distant, as a heartless tyrant. Some things they say about God don’t make any sense. Others are just plain dumb!
No wonder so many people don’t want God. At least not the God portrayed by these people!
So then, just exactly what DOES God want of us? Jesus taught us to love God and our neighbor. The only law, the only *rule *we now have is to LOVE.
So don’t worry about religion’s “rules”.
Be loving, and you will be fulfilling God’s ONLY rule!
affirmation: I please God by giving unselfish love to others.
verse: “He that loveth not, knoweth not God, for God is love.” (1 John 4:8)

title: HOW DO YOU SPEND YOUR TIME?
body: Not watch and criticize!
Life has gotten so complex, and none of us can presume to see the whole picture about anything really.

Whether it be with politics, religion, or what our next door neighbor is doing, we often can only see the tip of the iceberg. We just really don’t have enough understanding to accurately judge people here in this life. Only God sees everything.
We would be better off if instead of criticizing other people and being so condemning, we spent even half of that energy praying for them. We sure would see some positive results that way!
affirmation: I seek You continually, Lord, to see things as You see them.
verse: “Teach me Thy way, O Lord, and lead me in a plain path.” (Psalms 27:11)

title: GOD AND SCIENCE
body: Many people think they have to choose between science and God, as if they were somehow not compatible. But such is not the case.
They are opposite, but they compliment each other. God and science are simply two sides of the same coin.
This natural world that we are living in is a mere reflection of the spiritual world that surrounds us. And both are just as equally wondrous, unfathomable...
And beautiful!
affirmation: I praise you, O God, for the intricate beauty, harmony, and precision of Your creation, both physical and spiritual.
verse: “For the invisible things of Him are clearly seen and understood through the things that He hath made.” (Romans 1:20)

title: WHAT ARE YOU INTO?
body: So many people today are so into themselves. Their whole world revolves around them.
No wonder they are unhappy. No wonder they are so depressed.
This is the culture we are living in. And it is only made worse by television, movies, music, and the Internet.
It’s not surprising that loneliness abounds.
Because true happiness comes not in living selfishly, but in thinking of others. And people are what bring meaning and purpose to life.
Are you lonely? Look for ways to bring joy into other people’s lives.
Are you depressed? Discover what joys can be had by putting others’ needs above your own.
GIVE love, and you’ll receive love!
affirmation: I open the windows of my heart and life to others.
verse: “Give and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over shall men give back to you. For with the same measure that you give, it shall be given back to you again.” (Luke 6:38)

title: NOT THE ONLY ONE
body: Do you love the Lord? You are not the only one.
There are many, many hundreds of millions of people that love Him too. There are many who believe in the Bible and find help and guidance in its pages.
There are many who hear from the Lord and have a close walk with Him. There are many who follow Him, and many who share the same hope that you do.
There are many more that God uses day after day to lift up and encourage others. There are many who have forsaken self and are putting Him first.
Many people are pleasing God and are fulfilling their mission on Earth.
Cheer up.
You are not the only one!
affirmation: I am a soldier in God’s mighty army of love.
verse: “Let us not be weary in well doing for in due season we shall reap if we faint not.” (Galatians 6:9)

title: “VIVE LA DIFFÉRENCE!”
body: Getting along with the opposite sex is not only challenging; it can be downright frustrating! And sometimes we may be tempted to give up trying.
Since the beginning of time men and women have had difficulty understanding each other, because we are different. God made us different.
And the problem comes when we insist on trying to make the opposite sex see things the way we do, when we expect them to be like us.
It would be boring to live in a world where men and women were all the same!
We are not supposed to try to be like each other. We are here to learn from each other, enjoy each other. That’s the beauty of love.
As the French say... “Vive la différence”!
affirmation: Thank you Lord for my mate, for this wonderful opportunity to learn from our differences, and to grow in Your love as a couple.
verse: “Male and female created He them. A man shall cleave unto his wife and they shall be one flesh.” (Genesis 1:27, 2:24)

title: I BELIEVE IT!
body: God is love. God is the spirit of love. And love is God. I believe that.
I also believe that everything God does, He does in love. God can take away or God can give, but whatever He does, whichever it is, He does it in love. I am convinced of that.
This is what the Bible calls the walk of faith; trusting in an invisible God.
Sure, there are many things we don’t understand right now, in this life, but the day will come when we will see the glory of God in the things that we do not now understand.
Love WILL win in the end. There WILL be a happy ending.
I believe it!
affirmation: All these things come from a loving Father. All these things come from a Father of love.
verse: “Blessed are they that believe, for there shall be a performance of those things from the Lord.” (Luke 1:45)

title: SHOW THEM YOU CARE
body: How do we show someone we care?
The best way is simply to pay attention to them.
It takes a real effort to stop what we are doing and listen to a friend or loved one.
It isn’t always easy.
It takes an effort to not let your mind wander, to listen and not be formulating your answer while the other is talking.
People are starving for attention.
Be willing to listen...
Show them you care!
affirmation: I show people that I care by listening more to them.
verse: “My children, let us not love in word, neither in tongue; but in deed and in truth.” (1 John 3:18)

title: STOP IT RIGHT NOW!
body: Some people are workaholics. They work non-stop, drink a lot of coffee, and get very little sleep.
They are tormented by a spirit of constant unrest. They feel they can’t stop.
But one of the central lessons we are here to learn is to let God move in our lives. And He can’t do this as long as we are in this state of constant busyness.
We have to stop doing things in our own understanding, in our own reasoning, and in our own energy and strength.
Then, and only then, can God start moving mightily in our lives.
It is only when we get out of God’s way that He can bless us, provide for us and show us His power.
Stop trying so hard.
Give God some space!
affirmation: I give you room to work in my life, dear Lord, by stopping and looking to You first in all I do.
verse: “He that is entered into His rest has ceased from his own works.” (Hebrews 4:10)

title: GREAT
body: God’s idea of greatness is not money. God’s idea of cool is not how popular you are. God’s idea of good is not self glorification, arrogance, and pride.
God only uses humble people. Men and women who are small in their own eyes are great in God’s.
Those who know they are nothing without Him. Those who know that everything they have is because of Him, and comes from Him. These are the truly great on this earth.
And these will be the greatest in the world to come!
affirmation: I know that everything I have and everything I am is thanks to God. I walk humbly, because I owe everything to Him.
verse: “Every good and perfect thing comes from above.” (James 1:17)

title: SHAMEFUL
body: A woman paid $130,000 for a hand bag. Another paid half a million for a watch.
A man shelled out $1.6 million for a bottle of whiskey. People pay millions for iPhone cases covered with hundreds of diamonds, and the most expensive cars can be had for $20 million or more.
Expensive houses go for hundreds of millions. And the most expensive yacht has a hull completely covered in gold and is worth $4.5 billion!
The world’s richest man recently increased his fortune by $2 billion... in ONE day. But over 80% of the world’s population makes under $10. a day.
Still, people idolize the wealthy. They justify them and even exalt them as role models.
We should all be ASHAMED!
affirmation: I do not glorify material riches and the people who have them. I rather praise the Lord and exalt His name.
verse: “He who dies wealthy, dies disgraced.” (Andrew Carnegie, Billionaire business magnate, philanthropist and pioneer of the American steel industry.)

title: THE QUESTION
body: The question from many people is: “Does God exist?”

If God doesn’t exist, then let’s eat and drink and be merry. Let’s gorge ourselves with a never ending barrage of food, drink, drugs, good times, travel, and unbridled fun. We would be stupid not to do so!
But if God does exist? Whoa!
If God DOES exist, if there IS more to this life, and if something else awaits us after it, then wouldn’t we be wise to start getting ready for it?
So, we should all ask ourselves:
“Do I really live as if I believe that God exists?”
affirmation: I am doing the most that I can for the Lord in service to others in my daily life.
verse: “Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon Earth. But rather lay up for yourselves treasures in Heaven.” (Matthew 6:19-20)

title: WELCOME HOME
body: Home is a place where you are always welcome. Its doors are always open, and you feel safe.
Home is where you feel you are heard, you feel you count, and you feel loved.
Home is the bosom of a mother, the faith of a child, and a glimpse of Heaven.
Home is a place of ease, a place of rest, and a place of refilling.
That’s what Jesus is like:
With Jesus in your heart you are home.
affirmation: I find comfort in your arms, O Lord. Thou art my hiding place.
verse: “The name of the Lord is a strong tower. The righteous run into it and are safe.” (Proverbs 18:10)

title: NEED HELP?
body: Do you know why some people don’t hear from God? Because they are lazy. They prefer to have others tell them what to do. They want someone else to give them the solution to their problem.
It’s hard work sometimes hearing from God. You have to make a real effort. You have to quiet your own spirit, stop all your feverish activity.

In short, you have to get yourself out of the way for God to be able to get through, because God speaks in a small, quiet, firm and loving voice. But if you don’t quiet your own spirit you’ll never be able to hear Him!
Other people can’t solve your problems. No one else can fully understand the scope of your situation.
So, instead of looking to people to help you, get alone in prayer, bring a hush to your own spirit, wait for His answer... And you will get it!
affirmation: Lord, You know what’s best. I look to Thee alone for help and guidance.
verse: “Be still and know that I am God.” (Psalms 46:10)

title: ANGER IS EASY!
body: Anyone can get mad, get up in arms and fly into a fit of rage. And some people can get all bent out of shape over anything, and almost nothing!
But it takes guts to keep it together. It shows real worth to control yourself.

Now, we are not talking about the kind of self control where you are inwardly ready to explode any minute. But coming to a place in your life where you realize that nothing good has ever come out of those times you blew up and said something rash, or did something stupid.
You’re smart when you realize that you ended up not changing anything, but only hurt people including yourself! It’s not only smart, but it’s also a lot better for your health and happiness.
So, why worry? Let’s stop trying so hard to change things we cannot change.
God can set you free from anger. But you have to ask Him to!
affirmation: Take away from me, O God, everything that’s not of You.
verse: “He that has no rule over his own spirit is like a city that is broken down, and without walls.” (Proverbs 25:28)

title: IN THE END
body: When all is said and done, when we come to the end of our lives...
What will we be able to take with us? What will remain?
That glorious new building of today will be in ruins tomorrow. The good we do today will be forgotten by future generations.
Popularity is transitory. Fame is fleeting. Man forgets. Only God remembers!
And only what we do for LOVE will remain...
In the end.
affirmation: I believe in love and I am loving to those whose paths I cross in this life.
verse: “And there is faith, hope, love, these three; but the greatest of these is love.” (1 Corinthians 13:13)

title: BACK TO LOVE
body: Instead of bringing the world together, religion is only creating more division.

Petty rivalries, jealousies, and bickering over doctrines and even politics these days are common in churches. Fussing, fighting, and feuding have always been rampant in the religious world. And these things are simply not of the Lord!
Jesus came in love.
Jesus came for love, that we might learn to love and help each other.
Not in competition, but in loving cooperation and fellowship as one body. Not with empty words, but by loving deeds: His love through us for our fellow man.
How far we have fallen. We need to get back to the teachings of Jesus, pure and simple.
We don’t need more religion.
We need more love!
affirmation: I lift up Christ by being an example of patience, tolerance, and love to others.
verse: “Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem others better than themselves.” (Philippians 2:3)

title: BLAH BLAH BLAH
body: Talk is cheap. And the cheapest talk of all is talking about others. Gossip.
Gossip comes from idle people and empty minds: losers.
Great souls simply don’t have time for gossip. They dwell on the positive. They are busy doing better things, more productive things. They are winners.
Sooner or later we reap what we sow. What goes around comes around.
We don’t waste time on gossip!
affirmation: I don’t spend my time talking about others. I have better things to do.
verse: “A gossip can separate the best of friends.” (Proverbs 16:28)

title: ONLY GOD KNOWS!
body: Sex, booze, short dresses, and dancing. The tooth fairy, Protestants, Catholics, Jews. Marijuana, Muslims, Joel Osteen, nudity, pumpkins, the Pope.
Not going to church, or temple, or mosque, or going to church, or temple, or mosque.
Conservatives, liberals, Krishna, and Buddha. Abortion, the flag, makeup, a thong. Cleavage, guns, pork, and red meat. Birth control, tattoos, cats, and mother-in-laws!
All of the above things are GOOD to some people. And all of the above things are sinful, evil, or BAD to others.
So...
WHICH is right? WHO is right? There is no cookie cutter response to these and other questions.
Time, context, and other factors must be taken into account when it comes to many things in life.
Only GOD knows.
Only God sees the whole picture, and
only God sees the heart.
Who are any of us to think we know... MORE THAN GOD?!
affirmation: I know nothing, Lord, except what You show me.
verse: “Judge nothing before the time, until the Lord comes, who will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts.” (1 Corinthians 4:5)

title: A SURE THING
body: There will be war no more.
There will be no more fear.
Man will have ceased to view his neighbor as his enemy.
In God’s tomorrow.
affirmation: The day will come when fear will have been conquered by love!
verse: “Nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.” (Isaiah 2:4)

title: YOU CAN’T GO WRONG
body: You can’t please everyone.
Many people love my writings. But some say I don’t talk about God enough. And yet others say I talk about God too much.
Funny world isn’t it!
Many people don’t want much to do with God nowadays. They have been turned off by religious fanatics and have big misconceptions about God, and what He is like, and what He expects of us.
I try to keep my writings simple.
I know I can’t please everyone, but I believe that if you give people hope and if you give them love...
You can’t go wrong!
affirmation: Help me to be an example of hope and Your love, dear Lord.
verse: “Love ye one another as I have loved you.” (John 13:34)

title: GIMME MORE!
body: Seems like people are never satisfied these days!
They always have to have another beer, another portion of mashed potatoes, raise the volume even more on their sound system.
They want more. They want another. They want bigger, better, fancier, cooler.
More caffeine, more drugs, more pleasure, faster, faster, and faster!
But something’s gotta give!
Sooner or later each one of us will have to come back down to the ground. Sooner or later we will each have a wake up call.
We will all be humbler then. We will all be more loving.
Then, and only then, will we finally all be HAPPY.
affirmation: I know that peace has to come from within, and that in You I can have that peace.
verse: “Delight thyself in the Lord, and He shall give thee the desires of thine heart.” (Psalms 37:4)

title: WHEN?
body: When you realize life’s not all about you. When you don’t have all the answers.
When you stop feeding your senses. When you stop pursuing nonsense.
When you come to the end of yourself.
When your best isn’t good enough.
When you finally let go...
Then God has a chance.
Then God can show you.
Then God can fill you.
Then God can heal you. Then God can THRILL you!
affirmation: I have stopped doing things in my own strength and am letting God do them through me.
verse: “My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.” (2 Corinthians 12:9)

title: NO TIME FOR YOU GOD
body: There are 168 hours in a week.
Surveys break this down to the following for the average person:
50 hours of sleep
40 hours of work
35 leisure (television, computer, sports, etc.) 14 meals
12 household activities
5 commute
5 personal care
5 shopping
2 civic & religious activities
No wonder people have so many problems... They give less than 1.19% of their time to
GOD!
affirmation: I put You first in my life, dear Heavenly Father.
verse: “No man can serve two masters. For he will either hate the one or love the other. Ye cannot serve God and riches.” (Matthew 6:24)

title: SPECIAL THINGS
body: I think God inspired chocolate.
Is there anything better? Is there anything more yummy? Chocolate stands in a class all by itself. Chocolate is special.
I think God made roses.
Is there anything more beautiful? Is there anything more fragrant? Roses are divine. So special.
I think God made babies!
Is there anything purer? Is there anything more Heavenly than a tiny baby? Babies are like little angels dropped right out of Heaven. They’re so special.
And I think God made you and me.
God made us to love. God made us to reflect His love to the world around us.
I think WE are special indeed!
affirmation: I glorify You, Lord, in everything I do and I try to be an example of Your love to others.
verse: “For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.” (Ephesians 2:10)

title: WHAT, ME WORRY?
body: None of us can see the whole picture of why we are here on Earth.
And when we come to the end of life’s road probably all of us will be sad to some degree when we see the opportunities we lost, when we realize the time we wasted on *nothings*.
But no one is perfect.
And thankfully God knows our weaknesses. God knows our limitations, temptations, and trials. And all that is really required of us is that we just do the best we can.
As long as we try to do the right thing, as long as we try to be considerate and kind and loving to others...
We’ll have NOTHING to worry about!
affirmation: I do my best for God and others, and know that my efforts are not in vain.
verse: “Well done thou good and faithful servant. Enter thou into the joy of thy Lord.” (Matthew 25:21)

title: WHY PRAY?
body: I really don’t understand how prayer works, but I do pray to God.

To some people it may seem a little crazy, talking to an invisible being. But the best way I can explain it is: When I pray, coincidences happen. And when I don’t pray, they don’t. Or not as many, at least.
I talk to God as a friend. God is my friend. Throughout the day I tell Him things, ask Him things. And He guides me. He helps me.
That’s why I talk to God.
He’ll do the same for you!
affirmation: Thank You, Lord. You always hear me, You always help me, and You always answer my prayers.
verse: “All things that ye shall ask in prayer, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.” (Mark 11:24)

title: A HAPPY ENDING
body: Heaven is REAL! It exists.
When all is said and done, when our lives are done and through, only what we have done for LOVE will last.
We all know this deep down inside. We have just had this blurred by all the noise and confusion of the world.
Yes, the good news is that love will win, in the end. Love is the currency in Heaven, and in Heaven the only yardstick will be love.
So, what are you waiting for?
LOVE, here and now. Let all of your actions be guided by love... and you can’t go wrong.
You will have a happy ending!
affirmation: Thank You dear Heavenly Father. I know that my labor of love here and now is not in vain.
verse: “There is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord shall give me at that day.” (2 Timothy 4:8)

title: OUR PRIORITIES
body: Greed is flaunted in our faces in movies and on television to where many people now accept it as normal.
It’s no wonder that more and more people think nothing of living selfishly.
But there is only one thing we can take with us from this life; and that is what we do unselfishly for others.
Only what we do for love, and for the Lord will last.
We are here to enjoy life. That’s not bad. Just so long as we don’t lose sight of what is real...
The things that will last,
Forever!
affirmation: I know I am only passing through this world, Lord. I keep my eyes on the Heavenly vision.
verse: “For we look not at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen. For the things which are seen are temporal, but the things which are not seen are eternal.” (2 Corinthians 4:18)

title: MOVE WITH GOD!
body: You can’t please the old.
The old is implacable, immovable, set in its ways. The old always resists change.
Each new generation has had to deal with this. Every new generation has had to buck the status quo, go against the norms of their day. It’s never been easy.
But God is with pioneers.
God is with those who are brave enough to challenge the norm. Because God is all about change!
Our God is a moving God. Are YOU moving with Him?
affirmation: I am open to the changes that God wants to bring about in my life.
verse: “A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you.” (Ezekiel 36:26)

title: EXCITING TIMES
body: Aren’t we living in exciting times?
Technology is advancing so quickly that if you blink you miss something!
There certainly is a good side to this.

But all of the cool gadgets, devices, inventions, and breakthroughs are coming at a price: People are having to choose between traditional values of family, friendship, and love; or selfishness, indifference, and greed.
Jesus said that people would grow cold. But He said that we are not to worry. He said that this would be one of the signs to look for before His return.
Aren’t we living in exciting times!
affirmation: Thank You Lord for being with me in these times that we are living in. Thank You for always being by my side.
verse: “The love of many shall grow cold... And then shall appear the Son of God coming in the clouds of Heaven with power and great glory.” (Matthew 24:12,30)

title: DON’T WORRY
body: Why do we worry about tomorrow?
When a need arises, You Lord will provide.
When obstacles come, You Lord will help us through them or around them. Or over them!
Why do we worry so?
Because we are not truly trusting Him. We have not yet surrendered our all.
Thank you Lord for Your peace.
Thank you Lord for the rest of body, mind, and soul that comes from letting YOU take control of my life.
affirmation: I take life one day at a time and trust God to take care of tomorrow.
verse: “The Lord redeemeth the soul of His servants: and none of them that trust in Him shall be desolate.” (Psalms 34:22)

title: LOOK TO GOD, NOT MAN.
body: The world today is so divided.
Some people believe that God doesn’t want a man to enjoy a glass of wine, or a woman to wear pants, or dance. To some, many things are taboo!
A lot of Christians have turned going to church into a god. They seem to have forgotten that true religion is about a personal relationship with our Creator and loving service to others.
People are so incredibly divided and at odds with each other when it comes to their beliefs. No wonder so many people are turned off to religion.
Don’t look to man. Look to God.
SEEK God: and you will find Him.
LISTEN to God: and He will guide you.
FOLLOW God: and He will give you the desires of your heart!
affirmation: I look to You and You alone, Lord, for guidance in my life.
verse: “It is better to trust in the Lord than to put confidence in man.” (Psalms 118:8)

title: CLEANING HOUSE
body: We all have to do dirty laundry. We all have to brush our teeth. We all bathe.
We wash, we scrub, we shave. We keep clean to take care of our bodies, our health, our well being. If not, we get ill. We get stinky. We lose friends.
The same holds true for us spiritually. We get yucky if we are not cleaning ourselves regularly by making time for the Lord.
You will be unpleasant to be around if you do not take time to think about Him and bathe yourself spiritually with inspiring things from His Word.
Time for a scrub?
affirmation: As soon as I wake up the first thing I do is spend time with the Lord.
verse: “Now ye are clean through the Word which I have spoken unto you.” (John 15:3)

title: I DON’T WANT TO WAIT!
body: My God! It is hard to wait!
Whether it be for that job, or that mate. Whether it be for the sun to shine, the wound to heal, or for the bell to ring: it’s HARD to wait!

But patience seems to be one of the main lessons we are here to learn. And just when we think we have learned it, God gives us another batch of things that try our patience. He shows us just how far we have to go, how much more we have to learn. Kind of funny isn’t it.
But you know...
It’s ALL for our good. Everything is for our spiritual growth. Everything is under control. All things will turn out for the best.
And we will be so happy some day, that we trusted Him, TODAY!
affirmation: I know that everything is going according to Your highest will.
verse: “But let patience have her perfect work that you may be perfect and entire, lacking nothing.” (James 1:4)

title: STRANGE PEOPLE
body: Some people sure are strange.
It’s amazing to see how they can misinterpret things. They read between the lines. They want to interpret your words and find hidden meaning. It’s quite funny at times. It’s also sad.
It’s sad how people have been led away from the simplicity of life. They have become captives of snares created by their own minds, products of their own thoughts.
It’s wise to be simple.
That’s what makes a little child so beautiful: He hasn’t learned to overly complicate things... yet.
Only Jesus can set you free. Free from the things that keep you down.
Free to live.
Free to love.
affirmation: I stopped analyzing and trying to understand everything, and now let You have free reign in my life Lord.
verse: “If the Son therefore shall make you free, you shall be free indeed.” (John 8:36)

title: “MAÑANA”
body: It will be a better day “Mañana” when the world is free of fear.
Concrete and glass, plastic and crime, steel and starvation, pollution and guns will no longer be the bane of man’s existence.
Greed, jealousy, envy and self will no longer rule man’s heart.
Man will feel no need to kill his neighbor. No one will be a stranger.
And the child in us all will be set free.
“Mañana.”
When the world is free of fear.
affirmation: A better day awaits me, and a world where love will reign at last.
verse: “They shall not hurt nor destroy... for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the Lord.” (Isaiah 11:9)

title: HAPPY SOULS
body: Are you a happy soul? Or are you a sourpuss?
Do you go through your day with a smile? Or do you trudge through it with a frown?
People with positive outlooks are not only more successful: They also live longer!
So, unless you have a death wish, start looking on the positive, be grateful for what you have, enjoy life, and SMILE more!
You will not only have more fun...
But you will also be more fun to be around!
affirmation: I dwell on the positive and look on the bright side of life.
verse: “A merry heart doeth good like a medicine.” (Proverbs 17:22)

title: LIVING IN HARMONY
body: God always seems to bless people who live in peace with each other, people living in harmony, helping each other.
It takes an effort sometimes. It’s not always easy, but there is real strength in unity.
Life is hard enough as it is, but God blesses unity. That’s why the devil attacks it so much.
Let’s try to get along better with those around us.  Let’s be positive and lift each other up. Let’s make an effort to dwell on the things we have in common, things that unite us.
We need each other.
Let’s strive for harmony.
affirmation: I need and am grateful for the people that God has put in my life.
verse: “How good and how pleasant it is to dwell together in unity.” (Psalms 133:1)

title: OPEN UP!
body: Some people only think of themselves. They are only looking to receive. They are complainers. They are always unhappy, depressed, and have many problems.
Other people give, and give, seeking nothing in return. They forget themselves. They think of others and put others’ needs before their own.
They are happier because they have discovered the joy that comes from giving. They even enjoy better health!
It is in giving of ourselves that we find the truest joy.
It is in opening our hearts to others that we find the FULLNESS of God.
affirmation: I help others without seeking anything in return.
verse: “He that plants sparingly shall reap also sparingly, but he that plants bountifully shall reap also bountifully.” (2 Corinthians 9:6)

title: IF I WERE GOD
body: “If I were God I wouldn’t let people suffer.”
You hear this all the time. That’s people’s attitude! They are always accusing God of being unfair, unkind, unloving.
If they were God they would do things differently. What they are saying is that they are more righteous than God. Imagine that!
Man’s natural mode is to get proud sometimes, to get arrogant. We think we know more than God. We think we know how to do things better.
Well, God doesn’t expect us to be perfect. But He does expect us to be yielded. And it is as we become yielded that we begin to see God’s love for our lives.
It’s only when we are yielded to Him that we see the wisdom of His plan.
affirmation: Only you are wise, O God. I trust Thee and Thy plan for all mankind.
verse: “Now we see through a window lens partially, but then we will see clearly. Now I know in part, but then will I know fully.” (1 Corinthians 13:12)

title: TO BE CONTINUED
body: We should all ask ourselves: “What are we doing now in this world that we will be able to take with us into the next?” That is the question.
If we are happy with the answer, and if we think we will be comfortable when we stand before God’s throne some day, because that day will come, then we have nothing to worry about.
A very wise person once said:
“We cannot be selfish and unloving in this life, and unselfish and loving in the next. The two are too closely linked. One is but a continuation of the other.”
affirmation: I live my life every day in such a way that I will not be ashamed when I appear before God.
verse: “Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep His commandments, for this is the whole duty of man. For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing, whether it be good, or whether it be evil.” (Ecclesiastes 12:13-14)

title: STOP THE NOISE!
body: Sometimes we don’t know what to do. Sometimes we don’t know where to go.
Life is full of uncertainties, surprises, curve balls, and detours. And so much noise!
We often feel lost, or at least not sure as to which course of action to take.
At times like these we must make a real effort to not lean to our own understanding, but to get down to business with the Lord, and seek His solutions in prayer.
God always answers the hungry heart. God never fails to show the way. But we’ve got to first stop all the NOISE, get quiet...
And wait for HIS answer!
affirmation: When I don’t know what to do, I seek Your help and guidance, dear Lord.
verse: “Ye shall seek Me, and find Me, when ye shall search for Me with all your heart.” (Jeremiah 29:13)

title: DIFFERENT PATHS
body: Many of our problems in life come from trying to control people. Many of our heartbreaks come from expecting things of them.
We try to control things. We place expectations on people. Family members do it, sometimes even those we love the most.
It just seems to be human nature to want to take charge and be in control. But often that only leads to stress and frustration for all.
We must remember that each one is here on their own personal mission, and God doesn’t force anyone to do anything.
Each of us has a life to live. Each of us walks our own unique path in life. We should support each other along those paths.
And let God have HIS perfect way in people’s lives.
affirmation: I leave everything in Your hands, Lord. Only You know what is best for every one of us.
verse: “The Lord will perfect that which concerns each one.” (Psalms 138:8)

title: HOW MUCH DO YOU LOVE?
body: When this life is over and the angels beckon you, the only thing you will be able to take with you is what you have done with love.

No matter what anyone says, in spite of all the great things you may have done, in spite of all the money and material wealth you have accumulated in this life, if you didn’t have love you didn’t accomplish much. Because only love will last.
Mother Teresa unselfishly loved the most needy in one of the poorest countries on earth. Mother Teresa was a good example of love. She gave her life for love.
Imagine how she must feel now that she is in God’s presence!
Mother Teresa sure was a smart woman!
affirmation: I believe in love. I know that God’s love is the answer to all of man’s problems, and I am a good ambassador of His love.
verse: “Let your light so shine before men that they may see your good works and glorify your Father which is in Heaven.” (Matthew 5:16)

title: THAT THING YOU DO
body: Someone once said: “Don’t tell me what you believe. Tell me what you DO all day and I’ll tell you what you believe!”

Our lives speak so much louder than our words. No matter how you see yourself, no matter how many lofty ideas you have, what you actually DO is what’s important. That’s what defines you. You become the things you do day in and day out.
We all, each and every one of us, are creating our eternal destiny at this very moment by the things we do.
We can live selfishly, or we can have the life we long for by being loving and giving and caring.
And that begins with how we live, and what we DO, today!
