affirmation: I am creating the life that I want to live right now, every day by the things that I do.
verse: “Be ye doers of the Word and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.” (James 1:22)

title: THE PATH TO CHANGE
body: People are so afraid of saying the wrong thing, of doing the wrong thing. People are worried about being politically correct. And it is stifling us all!
We are all afraid that anything we say may be misinterpreted, or blown out of proportion. It is becoming ridiculous.
Yet this is the world that we are creating. We are responsible for many of the very chains that bind us. Only man is to blame for having let himself get to the state he is currently in.
We are not getting better. We are getting worse. Things have got to change.
But that change must begin with US!
affirmation: I focus on doing what I know is right, and I don’t worry about what others do or say.
verse: “We ought to obey God rather than man.” (Acts 5:29)

title: WHO’S WAITING ON WHO?
body: Many people say they wonder why they are here on earth. They say they are waiting for God to show them.
They are waiting for God to give them some big sign, a flash of lightning or something. But they aren’t doing anything meanwhile.
God doesn’t work that way.
God doesn’t want you to just sit around doing nothing. He wants you to get busy and be a blessing to someone else.
We are here to be a channel of God’s love.
We are here to serve others.
It is when we get busy doing what we *can do*, that we put ourselves in a position where God can start moving. And, little by little, His path for our life becomes clear.
God is only waiting on YOU!
affirmation: As I do what I can today, God shows me the next step.
verse: “For we walk by faith, not by sight.” (2 Corinthians 5:7)

title: IT’S A CHALLENGE
body: It’s nice to get away from all the confusion, rest in the Lord, and find the peace He gives when our minds are stayed on Him.
The challenge is learning to take that peace with us and spread it throughout our day to others, especially in situations that would otherwise try our patience. It’s not always easy.
That’s the problem most of us have.
But isn’t that what life is really all about?
Learning to be like Him and being a living example of His love?
It’s that simple.
But oh, at times, how hard it is!
affirmation: I meditate on the Lord and let Him fill me with His peace as I go through my day.
verse: “The peace of God which passes all understanding will keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.” (Philippians 4:7)

title: SETTLE DOWN
body: You see it everywhere these days: People getting hysterical. Hysteria is becoming the norm.
People get bent out of shape over immigration. They get self righteous over abortion. Not to mention women’s rights, gay rights, racism, or a thousand other things.
People shout. They get violent.
The press doesn’t help at all. They just make it worse with their hysteria and sensationalistic news coverage.
So much contention is getting us nowhere.
People need to just settle down.
But believe it or not, everything is going according to God’s perfect plan in spite of man. God is still in control, and He is letting man come to the end of his rope.
Yes, the Lord is letting the entire universe see what happens when man tries to do things his own way, do things in his own strength... Without God!
affirmation: I keep my thoughts on the good and positive in the world. I know that criticizing others will not change anything.
verse: “Glorify God in your body and in your spirit which are God’s.” (1 Corinthians 6:20)

title: THE SECRET TO MORE
body: Some people are never satisfied. No matter what they have, they always want more. No
matter where they are, they always wish they were somewhere else.
This spirit consumes them. It overtakes them and robs them of all of life’s joys. It is a spirit of discontent. And it is not of the Lord.
The secret to God’s blessings is to be content with where we are, and to be grateful for what we have.
When we accept this truth and embrace it, we put ourselves in a place where God can really start moving in our lives.
And give us MORE!
affirmation: I am grateful for and content with my life and everything You have given me, Lord. I know You want the best for me, and what will make me the happiest.
verse: “In everything give thanks for this is the will of God.” (1 Thessalonians 5:18)

title: HOW DO WE SPEND OUR TIME?
body: We all like to eat. We all like to drink. We all like to have a good time; we like sports, the beach, movies, and more. And, “Oh yes, we believe in God.” Or so people say.
But how is it that we find time for so many things we want to do, but somehow find so little time for God?
It’s not about whether we go to church or not.
It’s not about going through the motions.
It’s about letting the Lord lead us throughout our day, no matter what we’re doing. It’s about letting Him use us to be a blessing to others.
How much of our time did we spend on things that won’t last? How much time did we spend on things that are eternal?
That is a question we will ALL have to answer some day.
affirmation: At the start of each day I direct my thoughts to You, Lord, in prayer and Your Word, and You guide me throughout my day.
verse: “Set your affection on things above, not on things of the earth.” (Colossians 3:2)

title: YOU CAN
body: People are losing hope. People feel let down with the system, with religion, with other people.
The world has become fake, and the people flakey.
But many are looking for solid ground in this quicksand world, something to hold on to. They are starving for *real*.
YOU can encourage, lift up, and inspire. You can be brave enough to think of others and put their happiness above your own.
You can be a beacon of hope to them.
You CAN!
affirmation: I make a real effort to lift up and encourage the people in my life.
verse: “Wherefore comfort each other and edify one another.” (1 Thessalonians 5:11)

title: PATIENTLY HE AWAITS
body: God never forces anyone.
He is always loving, never condemning. When we seek Him, He is there. He waits for us patiently with open arms.
God loves us. But He cannot make us love Him. Yet that is the only thing He wants of us: our love.
God doesn’t try to control us. Yet many try to control Him and tell Him how He should do things.
God’s ways are not our ways. And it is only by giving up ours, that we can fully come to understand His!
Patiently He AWAITS.
affirmation: I love You, Lord. You are my best friend. I put myself fully in Your hands.
verse: “My thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, saith the Lord.” (Isaiah 55:8)

title: WHAT TO DO?
body: Sometimes you find yourself with a problem or in a difficult situation, and you want so badly to find the solution, or have the answer. You just don’t know what to do.
Well, the Bible says, “They that seek the Lord understand all things.” (Proverbs 28:5)
Now the key word here is *seek*. When we seek God for guidance and really want to know, and have no will of our own, He will answer.

Sometimes God answers “yes”. And sometimes He answers “no”. And yet other times God answers “wait”. He always shows you what to do; even if many times He tells you to stop everything and do nothing and wait. Time has a curious way of solving problems.
God will answer your prayer if you will but stop, take a pause, and in quietness and calmness of spirit seek Him.
He always answers the hungry heart.
affirmation: When I don’t know what to do I seek the Lord. As I follow His guidance He works miracles in my life.
verse: “Call unto me and I will answer thee and show thee great and mighty things which thou knowest not.” (Jeremiah 33:3)

title: YOU THINK YOU’RE COOL?
body: It seems like everyone these days is trying to be cool.
People seem to be willing to do anything to be cool, to attract attention.
They brag about themselves. They show off their material possessions. They flaunt their riches.
And the sad part is, being selfish is now considered cool. They call it hedonistic!
That’s how far man has fallen.
But in God’s eyes it’s cool to care about others. It’s cool to be unselfish and giving.
To God, loving people are the smart ones.
Loving people are the COOL ones!
affirmation: I want to be cool in God’s eyes, not in man’s.
verse: “A meek and loving spirit is of great value in the sight of God.” (1 Peter 3:4)

title: LIKE A FLOWER
body: Our lives are like flowers: Our days are numbered.
We bask in the sunlight for a season, but inevitably we must wither and die.
We rejoice when someone comes into this life. We are happy when a baby is born. But we get sad when someone dies.
We are all here on a mission. We are all here to learn. When we learn our lessons, the bell will ring, and we will go home.
Just like when a baby is born, leaving this life should be a day of rejoicing...
It’s our graduation day!
affirmation: I know death is no cause for sorrow. When I or any of my loved ones leave this earth we will have learned and accomplished what we came here for.
verse: “There shall be no more sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain. Enter thou into the joy of thy Lord.” (Revelation 21:4, Matthew 25:23)

title: THE TWO PATHS
body: The world says, “Be successful.” (That means make a lot of money and be greedy.)
Jesus says, “Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon Earth.” (That means invest in Eternity by giving His love to others.)
The world says, “Grow up!” (That means become cold, cynical, and selfish.)
Jesus says we should be like little children. (That means trusting, loving, and pure.)
The world says, “Seeing is believing.” (That means God doesn’t exist.)
Jesus says, “Believing is seeing.” (That means you have ALL of God’s power and promises on your side!)
affirmation: I choose to see things God’s way, and not the way of this world. I know His is the right perspective. As I follow Him, He strengthens me.
verse: “It is God that fills me with strength and makes my way perfect.” (Psalms 18:32)

title: SLEEPY TIME PRAYER
body: Sleeping is funny: You lie down, close your eyes. Maybe you have a dream. Then the next thing you know you wake up after having taken a trip to some unknown place. Time has passed, but to you it seems like mere seconds.
Throughout the Bible God spoke to His prophets and children through dreams.

Why do we sleep? Well, other than for the obvious reason of replenishing our energy, some think that God has ordained sleep to keep man from committing too much evil. To keep man in line somewhat, so to speak. Who knows?
Whatever the reason, it is a good idea to say a little prayer before going to sleep to ask the Lord for His protection, to ask Him to protect your dreams.
Who knows? Maybe God will even speak to you through one of them!
affirmation: Before going to sleep I ask God to give me good dreams, and speak to my spirit through them.
verse: “In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men... Then He openeth their ears and instructs them. (Job 33:15-16)

title: EFFECTIVELY
body: God is love.
And our challenge is: How to communicate that God is love to a world that can’t help but see Him as the many negative things religion makes Him out to be!
How do we destroy the false image people have of Him? That image of God as a tyrant who wants you to keep all kinds of dumb rules?
God’s only law is love. God’s only wish is for us to be loving to each other.
When we come to the end of this life we will see that God was not a tyrant, but the very essence of love itself. And that LOVE was in fact the only thing that really mattered!
And to effectively communicate *this *is our challenge.
affirmation: By being an example of God’s love to all who cross my path in life, I show what God is truly like, and accomplish His highest will.
verse: “For all the law is fulfilled in this: love thy neighbor as thyself.” (Galatians 5:14)

title: LET’S ENCOURAGE EACH OTHER
body: “To be able to laugh when you feel like crying.” That’s how one person described a sense of humor.

The times we are living in are certainly no laughing matter, but keeping the right balance, counting our many blessings and retaining a sense of humor help us to cope. They give us strength, stamina and the grace to endure.
A sense of humor makes the ride through this life more pleasant.
We are all in this together.
Let’s encourage and support each other... and let’s not forget to laugh!
affirmation: I make an effort daily to be a positive influence and encourage those around me.
verse: “Bear ye one another’s burdens and so fulfill the law of Christ.” (Galatians 6:2)

title: ALONE WITH GOD!
body: Everything is noise nowadays! People keep their televisions blaring, or music going non stop. No wonder they have trouble hearing God. The Bible says you have “to be still to feel God’s presence.” (See Psa. 46:10)
You go into the country surrounded only by His creation, and you see His hand in nature, you feel His peace, and then He can speak to your heart.
God is speaking all the time, and every time we get quiet enough and bring a hush to our own spirits we will hear Him.

But you are going to have to get alone with God, some place, some time during your day if you are to fi	His peace and the guidance you seek in your life. You have to not only stop the noise around you, but also your own feverish activity. And if you do, you will not be disappointed.
God WILL answer. And it will thrill your heart!
affirmation: No matter what is going on in my day, I make time for quiet moments with You, Lord.
verse: “For he that is entered into His rest, has ceased from his own efforts.” (Hebrews 4:10)

title: JUST DON’T
body: “Judge not!” People say that all the time. They also don’t like you telling them anything: “Don’t criticize me.”
So people say we should not criticize others.
We should not be judgmental. They get all self righteous about it. Yet they think nothing of criticizing others!
A lot of people spend their time criticizing celebrities, sports figures, and artists. They criticize their presidents, mayors, congressmen, neighbors and bosses. They think that’s ok.

Well, negative thinking is more detrimental to ourselves, than to anyone else. The Bible says we are not to cut down people. Speaking against people is simply not of God. It really accomplishes nothing.
We need to criticize people less and PRAY for them more!
affirmation: Only God can judge others, because only He sees everything. I wait on Him and do not speak negatively of others.
verse: “Speak evil of no man.” (Titus 3:2)

title: MAN’S WORLD
body: We have no more circus.
Fur coats are out of style.
We want to save the whales, snakes
and alligators.
We pollute our cities.
We deforest the jungle.
We dump plastic into the seas.
And we mow down our neighbor.
Our press is biased.
Our religions are meaningless.
Our music is noise.
Our systems are corrupt.
Earthquakes abound.
Hatred, anger, and greed.
Morals decline.
Decay all around I see.
Welcome to MAN’S WORLD!
affirmation: Only God can set things straight. Only He can make it all right.
verse: “And God said... let man have dominion over all the earth.” (Genesis 1:26)

title: SERIOUSLY NOW!
body: Life can get serious at times. It can get downright scary!
But worrying about it really doesn’t get us anywhere.
They say that laughter is the best medicine. That may be true. And the world would probably be a better place if we all laughed more!
God has given us a sense of humor. He wants us to be happy, trust Him for our lives, laugh more...
And not take ourselves so SERIOUSLY!
affirmation: I know God is in control of my life, and I am so grateful and happy that He is.
verse: “The joy of the Lord is our strength.” (Nehemiah 8:10)

title: WHY DO WE WORRY?
body: Have you ever noticed that most of the things we worry about never happen?
Why do we worry so much?
We can stop worrying, and find the rest that we long for.
God IS in control.
And things will turn out all right!
But first we must surrender everything to Him,
And TRUST!
affirmation: I give all of my problems to You, dear Lord. In Thee do I trust.
verse: “Come unto Me all ye that labor and are heavy burdened. Learn of Me... and ye shall find rest.” (Matthew 11:28-29)

title: INTEGRITY: IN DANGER OF EXTINCTION
body: We often hear people say, “Our thoughts and prayers are with you.”
But saying that doesn’t do anything unless you get down to business with God and specifically pray for that person or situation.
People also say, “We love what you’re doing.” But that doesn’t mean much either if they aren’t willing to lend a hand or chip in to the cause.
Words are cheap nowadays, and smiles are fake. We live in a world full of BS, lies, deception and sham. Superficial, fake, phony, and false dominate the media.
Integrity, humility, and truthfulness are virtues that are quickly vanishing.
Let’s be more honest with our words, pure with our thoughts, and sincere with our compliments.
affirmation: My actions back up my words. I not only say, but also do.
verse: “Show me your faith without your works. I will show you my faith by my works.” (James 2:18)

title: LASTING WORTH
body: People are busy getting an education, furthering their careers, looking for mates, starting families, raising children. They have hopes, dreams, projects, and plans.
Many people have high and lofty goals. Some are good, and some not so much.
So what’s the yardstick as to real worth?
When we get to Heaven I think we’ll see it was not so much about WHAT we did, but rather if we did it with love and an unselfish giving of ourselves to others.
Those are the only things that we will be able to take with us when we die. Those are the only things that will have eternal worth.
Those are the only things that will LAST!
affirmation: My success in this life and all my goals and plans are of little importance, compared to the love I give and the kindness I show.
verse: “The world passeth away and the things thereof, but he that doeth the will of God abideth forever.” (1 John 2:17)

title: INVESTING IN ETERNITY
body: We travel a lonely road here on earth.
Sometimes it’s a solitary journey.

Let’s face it, in this life you will never get the recognition you deserve. No one will ever fully appreciate even a fraction of the sacrifices you make. If you are looking for the praise of man, you will be disappointed.
But we are investing in Eternity with every kind word, every good deed. And the time will come when we will see that we did the right thing by being loving.
We are not to look to others for help. We are to look up. Up to the Lord, where we will find the strength and comfort that only He can give.
Some day our sacrifices WILL be acknowledged!
affirmation: I look to You alone for the strength I need, dear Lord. I know You see my sacrifices of love, and my reward is waiting for me in the next life.
verse: “We look not at the things which are seen but at the things which are not seen. For the things which are seen are temporary, but the things which are not seen are eternal.” (2 Corinthians 4:18)

title: WILL YOU BE MY NEIGHBOR
body: They say, “The only sure way of getting rid of an enemy is by making him your friend!”
Imagine if everyone believed and practiced that.

Let’s just imagine what the world would be like if instead of spending such vast sums of money to protect ourselves from each other, we spent it on helping each other, on making life easier for each other.
Imagine more parks, museums, concerts, and leisure time. Imagine food and shelter for all, education, amusement, and fun.
The real challenge is to stop treating others like enemies and to start treating each other like neighbors.
Mr. Rogers sure had that RIGHT!
affirmation: By showing love and reaching out to others in friendship I am making the world around me a better place.
verse: “Be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another.” (Ephesians 4:32)

title: THE TRUTH ABOUT TELEVISION
body: I was watching some old television shows and thinking about how much nicer people were back then.
They were kinder. They had morals. They had more integrity. How times have changed!
People say that television is bad today. But it really isn’t any different than it ever was.
It’s not television that is bad; it is US that have gone downhill.
The world is not becoming better. The world is becoming worse and worse, and television is merely reflecting the present state of man.
And it will only get even worse...
As long as man glorifies HIMSELF, more than God!
affirmation: I keep my eyes fixed on You, Lord, and let You use my life for Your glory.
verse: “Evil men shall become worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.” (2 Timothy 3:13)

title: HOW INSANE ARE YOU?
body: Why do people talk so much!
Some people talk and talk and talk. And they don’t even seem to think there is anything wrong with it.
They can go on for hours incessantly. They are selfish and so full of themselves.
They say the insane only think of themselves.
They rarely think of anyone else.
They live in their own little worlds where everything revolves around them.
How insane are YOU?
affirmation: I show others that I care more about them than myself by being quiet and listening to them.
verse: “He that hath knowledge spareth his words, and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.” (Proverbs 17:27)

title: THE OLDER WE GET
body: The world has gotten complicated, complex.
Many things have become so multifaceted that none of us can presume to know everything about hardly anything anymore!
When we were young we thought we knew everything. Most of us did anyway. We didn’t realize how little we knew.
The older we get the more we should realize, “We don’t know.”
It takes a lot of hard knocks to bring us down and make us realize that we need an attitude adjustment.
The Bible says that we should not lean to our own understanding.
And it is only when we have no will of our own, that we can see GOD’S plan for our lives.
affirmation: I surrender my pride and my way of thinking to God. I know He often works in unexpected ways.
verse: “If any man think that he knoweth anything, he knoweth nothing yet as he thinks he ought to know.” (1 Corinthians 8:2)

title: WHAT REALLY MATTERS
body: There are a lot of people in this world who don’t go to church.
We recently read that a lot more believers in God don’t go to church, than do.
Today many people equate pleasing God with going to a church and following what it teaches.
But whether you go to a church or not, Jesus said what really matters is that we love God, and that we love each other.
In God’s eyes love is what’s most important. So then, Love!
And you’ll be OK!
affirmation: I know that as long as I show love to others, even when it isn’t easy, I am pleasing God.
verse: “Love the Lord thy God with all thy heart... And love thy neighbor as thyself. These two commandments cover all the law.” (Matthew 22:37-39)

title: OUR DAILY CHOICE
body: Gossip,
ungratefulness,
finding fault,
complaining,
worry,
and negativity:
Defeat, stifle, and kill!
Dwelling on the positive,
speaking kindness,
counting your blessings,
seeing the good,
and being grateful:
Build, prosper, and bring life!
There is only ONE healing energy: LOVE.
affirmation: I choose a positive, uplifting, happy life by living in love.
verse: “The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance: against such things there is no law.” (Galatians 5:22-23)

title: FINDING PURPOSE IN LIFE
body: They say that to be happy you need: something to do, someone to love, and something to hope for.
We all want to feel useful. We find satisfaction contributing to a project or being part of a team. That’s why many people decide to become cops, firemen or join the armed forces.
They find satisfaction in being part of a noble cause and helping others.
Jesus said we should love our neighbor. Because it is in thinking of others and not of ourselves that we will find true happiness. It even cures depression!
Only God and His love can fill the heart of man. And it is only in giving His love and helping others that we can find true purpose in our lives.
affirmation: When I help others, my own problems fade away. I know I am being truly useful and fulfilling my purpose in life, and I do not feel lonely.
verse: “Pure religion before God is this, to visit the fatherless and the widows in their affliction.” (James 1:27)

title: ONE WAY OR ANOTHER
body: God wants us to draw closer to Him.
But most of the time when God is blessing us greatly, we tend to forget Him. We don’t find time for Him. We don’t seek Him, His help, and His strength when things are going well for us.
That’s why God sometimes permits illness, bereavement, or troubles: to get us to cry out to Him, to get us to stop thinking of ourselves and start thinking about Him.
But He would prefer that we come to Him in the good times as well as the bad. He wants us to come to Him out of love and gratitude for His many blessings.
One way or the other, it will happen: If we don’t draw closer to God out of love, circumstances may force us to...
Out of necessity!
affirmation: I seek Your help in the good times, Lord, so that You don’t have to get my attention through troubles.
verse: “Whoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.” (Matthew 21:44)

title: WHEN WE DON’T KNOW
body: What is prayer?
It’s not telling God what you want. It’s finding out what God wants.
It’s creating an empty place in your heart, and wanting God to fill it. It’s realizing you don’t know, and acknowledging that you need Him.
It’s when you want an answer, and come to God with no will of your own, and no preconceived ideas of how things should be. You are open to Him and whatever His will may be for you.
God doesn’t answer when we think we have the solution, but when we know we don’t!
When we know that we don’t have the answers...
That’s when God will answer!
affirmation: I come to God with a blank page waiting for Him to fill in His plan. I know He will answer and give me what’s best for me.
verse: “We know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us.” (Romans 8:26)

title: LOOKING FOR A MATE?
body: Did you know that Jesus never said we should seek a mate?
Yet that is what many people are doing these days. That seems to be the main focus in their lives.
Television and movies only make it worse with their fairy tale depictions of finding someone and living happily ever after.
More often than not people end up disillusioned when their would be partner doesn’t live up to their unrealistic romantic expectations.
It really is sad that so many people are lonely.
But whether we want a better job, or healing or freedom from debt, God’s formula remains the same:
If we put Him first in our lives, He will supply ALL of our needs... including a mate!
affirmation: I am waiting for the perfect mate in Your perfect time, Lord, because I am putting You first in my life.
verse: “Seek first the kingdom of God... then all things will be added unto you.” (Matthew 6:33)

title: BE THE EXAMPLE
body: You can’t make people be good.
You can’t force people to not be racist, not be sexist, not be prejudiced, nor be critical or judgmental.
You can’t force to people be *good*.
We each individually need to work towards being the way we wish others were. We need to be the example.
The only way to get rid of darkness is to let in the light. Only with His love can we ALL have a better world!
affirmation: Today I will let the light of God’s love shine through me, and influence others by my example.
verse: “Not by might, nor by power, but by My spirit saith the Lord.” (Zachariah 4:6)

title: HOW QUICKLY WE FORGET
body: After a recent devastating hurricane in the Caribbean people had no electricity. They had no television, no Internet. So in the evenings they took chairs out and sat curbside in front of their houses.
Children played ball. They rode their bicycles.
Adults played dominoes and board games.
People got to know their neighbors! Life was simple again and people were forced to get back to the basics. They helped each other.
No one denies the fact that they suffered a lot, and that they received a tremendous blessing through that suffering.
But now that it’s over, they also acknowledge that the spirit of community they had has largely faded. How quickly we forget.
Let’s all make a real effort to have that same spirit of love. Lord help us show compassion to our neighbors not only during the bad times...
But also during the GOOD!
affirmation: I am a friend to others in both the good times and the bad.
verse: “A friend loveth at all times and a brother is born for adversity.” (Proverbs 17:17)

title: FORGIVENESS
body: We will only be forgiven to the same degree that we forgive others.
That is something hard to accept. Especially when we have been wronged, when we have been treated unfairly.
Jesus did no wrong. He came for love, lived in love and died for love. He was falsely accused. He was despised, abused, and horribly treated.
They killed Him in the cruelest way imaginable. Yet He never stopped loving. Jesus never stopped forgiving.
We will only be forgiven to the same degree that we forgive others.
There’s no way around that. It’s the only way we can win in the end.
It’s the only way we can have the LAST LAUGH!
affirmation: I need to be forgiven for many things. I follow Jesus’ example by forgiving others.
verse: “If ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.” (Matthew 6:14)

title: SPIRAL
body: Spiraling inflation, natural disasters, sensationalistic press.
Unscrupulous politicians, perverted morals, rampant greed.
Everything is spiraling, downward, ever downward.
Crime is on the increase. Terror abounds.
Fear is widespread, as are plagues, illness,
and drugs.
Looking for answers and a reason to live.
Many are lost, nowhere to go.
Things get bigger, scarier, faster.
Only God’s love can straighten everything
out.
affirmation: I share the love of God with others. Only He and His love can bring peace.
verse: “The Lord is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust.” (Psalms 18:2)

title: BLESSED RELEASE
body: Obedience brings down the power and blessings of God.
It’s when we know that we are yielded that anything is possible. It’s when we know we are holding nothing back that God can work miracles in our lives.
But you really can’t expect God to bless you if you know you are disobedient. How can God bless disobedience?
It is a real liberation when you surrender all to the Lord, when you realize that you can’t do it and that only He can.
You find that place of rest. You find that place of peace. You find that release...
That only comes from OBEDIENCE.
affirmation: I unconditionally surrender my all to You, Lord Father God, as I walk in the path of obedience to Thy will.
verse: “If ye be willing and obedient ye shall eat the good of the land.” (Isaiah 1:19)

title: TALK IS CHEAP
body: “Everybody’s talking, can’t hear a word they’re saying...” So go the words to a popular song. Do you ever feel that way?
Sometimes you find yourself with someone who chatters incessantly. Then when they leave you realize: You didn’t get to say anything at all!
The Bible says that we should be “swift to hear and slow to speak.” What words of wisdom in such a noisy world as we are living in today.
To hear from the Lord we have to first bring a hush to our own spirits. We have to get quiet before Him.
We would all be better off if we spoke less and listened more. That’s not only how we learn more, but people will also like us more!
affirmation: As I quiet my spirit, I hear from God. And the less I talk the more I learn.
verse: “Let every man be quick to hear, and slow to speak.” (James 1:19)

title: WHAT FUN!
body: There’s nothing more fun than serving God.
There’s nothing more exciting than watching Him work.
To see your life take shape according to His plan is the coolest thing imaginable.
To have His presence in your everyday life is peace.
God will guide you. God will provide. He will protect, and keep you throughout your day. God encourages, lifts up, and soothes.
Serve God, and He will serve you!
Nothing is more FUN!
affirmation: I find tremendous joy and satisfaction in living for God, following His plan for my life, and seeing Him work.
verse: “Know that you will receive an inheritance from the Lord, for you serve the Lord Christ.” (Colossians 3:24)

title: BE STILL
body: Some people are always busy: Working, making, coming, going.
They no sooner arrive, then they have to leave.
Month after month, year after year their lives pass them by. They spend their whole lives moving but never reaching their destination. How sad.
But God is hardly ever in a big hurry. He takes His time to make a baby, a diamond, or even the tiniest flower.
The Bible says that to know God we must first of all, BE STILL!
affirmation: I rest in the Lord and trust in Him, letting Him work everything out in His perfect time.
verse: “Be still, and know that I am God.” (Psalms 46:10)

title: WHY ARE THEY AFRAID?
body: A lot of people fear death. A lot of people are afraid of dying.
But Jesus’ followers weren’t afraid of dying. When they were persecuted by the Romans they went to their deaths singing and rejoicing! They were not afraid of death.
They didn’t fear death, because they knew they were living the TRUTH. They knew that they were in the RIGHT.
A lot of people today have guilty consciences. So it’s not surprising that so many are afraid of dying.
Now we know why!
affirmation: I am living the truth that God has given me, and look forward to being reunited with Him when the time comes.
verse: “Be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee a crown of life.” (Revelation 2:10)

title: THE MONSTER OF JEALOUSY
body: Jealousy is not a virtue. Jealousy is not proof that you love someone. Jealousy is selfishness.
Jealousy is the spirit of feeling that you’ve been mistreated, that you deserve better.
Jealousy is a monster. People have even killed the person they say they love, out of jealousy. Imagine that!
Jealousy is not of God.
Jealousy is not love: It is the opposite of love. Because love is preferring someone else’s happiness over your own, even if it hurts.
And that kind of love requires the divine supernatural love of Christ.
Only GOD can give us that type of LOVE.
affirmation: Help me Lord to not be selfish and to be a living example of Your love.
verse: “Love beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things. Love never fails.” (1 Corinthians 13:7-8)

title: LIFE’S WRINKLES
body: Wrinkles are inevitable in life.
When we are born we are full of wrinkles. Then we lose them. Over time as we get older we get more and more.
Men don’t seem to care much. But women hate them. A woman will even see a wrinkle that no one else sees. A lot of young people today
are losing respect for people with gray hair and wrinkles, sad to say.
As we grow old we learn lessons that are not attainable in youth.
How praiseworthy it is to grow in truth and in spiritual things. How beautiful it is to age with dignity, even if it means getting a few wrinkles.
We shouldn’t be afraid of wrinkles!
affirmation: Lord help me to grow in wisdom and virtue and knowledge of Thee as the years go by.
verse: “Man looketh on the outward appearance, but God looketh on the heart.” (1 Samuel 16:7)

title: IT DIDN’T MATTER
body: It didn’t matter how smart we were, what we knew, or who we knew. It didn’t matter how famous.

It didn’t matter what we had, what we were, or where we were, nor our lofty dreams, visions and goals. The good causes we championed, the wrongs we made right. The things we accomplished, the times we shined bright.
It didn’t matter which church we went to, or if we ever went. If we didn’t have love in our hearts.
What mattered was: helping our neighbor, patience with the ignorant, how giving we were to the needy, how loving we were to the unlovely.
We will ALL be judged by one yardstick.
We will all receive praise from God for ONE thing alone...
How much love we had.
affirmation: All my efforts, all my sacrifices, all my good works will be in vain, if they are not done with love.
verse: “Without love I am nothing.” (1 Corinthians 13:2)

title: HIS STAMP
body: Most people, sad to say, don’t really want to do God’s will. Most people want to do their own.
Most people have their own agenda and only want God to give His stamp of approval to what they are already doing.
But for those who surrender their own will, God will move mightily. To those few who are willing to put Him and His Work first, God will put them first.
God will show His power and His protection to them like to no others. God will protect them and provide for them.
And the day will come when God Himself shall exalt them and honor them. They will be glad they put Him first.
Then they WILL have God’s stamp of approval!
affirmation: I only want Thy will, O God. I know this will bring the greatest rewards I could wish for, both in this life and in the one to come.
verse: “To him that overcometh will I grant to sit with Me in My throne, even as I also overcame, and am set down with My Father in His throne.” (Revelation 3:21)

title: TRUE RELIGION
body: The world has become complex, the problems we face so many, and God is so big that there is no one-size-fits-all set of rules, doctrine, or ideology that we should all follow.
None of us should expect everyone else to adhere to our beliefs, because we each, as individuals, are at different levels of spiritual growth.
We all have varying degrees of enlightenment, and we cannot expect anyone else to think and behave like us. Much less that everyone should believe like us.
When unbelievers do by nature the things that please God, when people who don’t even know God and the Bible live loving lives, they are more pleasing to God than believers who are unloving.
True religion in its highest state is LOVE, Pure and simple.
affirmation: I follow the truth that God has given me, and demonstrate true religion by being loving and accepting to all.
verse: “For when unbelievers, which have not the law, do by nature the things contained in the law, these having not the law are a law unto themselves.” (Romans 2:14)

title: LOVE IS SPELLED T-I-M-E
body: You know the easiest way to show someone you love them?
The easiest way to show someone you care?
It’s by spending time with them!
That’s what little children want the most: to spend time with Mommy or Daddy. To be close to them.
So, let’s show people we care. Show them we love them by spending time with them, by making time for them.
Love is spelled T-I-M-E. Give them your time!
affirmation: I make time for the people who are important in my life.
verse: “There is a time to every purpose under Heaven.” (Ecclesiastes 3:1)

title: RELATIONSHIP PROBLEMS
body: Many people have problems with their mate, their children, parents, co-workers, or neighbors. And they are desperate to find a solution.

Well, it may not be all that simple. With the complex dynamics of relationships these days, things are seldom simple and clear cut. There is almost never just one easy answer. But that’s no reason to give up on someone you love.
If you seek Him, God will show you what He wants you to do. God will show you day by day how He wants you to handle your situation.
If you are faithful to pray for your loved ones, read God’s Word, and follow His guidance daily, God will have His highest will in your lives, and work out your personal problems.
What more can you wish for than that!
affirmation: As I follow You, Lord, I know you will give me Your solutions.
verse: “The path of the just is as a shining light that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.” (Proverbs 4:18)

title: WHY WORRY?
body: Why do we worry so much? Worry has never solved anyone’s problems. Worry has never helped anyone do God’s will.
Worrying has destroyed more lives, lives that could have been productive, lives that could have been fruitful. Worrying kills.
We can’t worry and trust God at the same time. We are either doing one or the other.
Give God your problems. He loves you. He cares for you.
Stop worrying!
affirmation: I give You all my problems, cares, and concerns, dear Lord. I know my life is safe within Your hands.
verse: “Cast thy burden upon the Lord, and He shall sustain thee.” (Psalms 55:22)

title: IN A MOMENT
body: A careless word spoken, a sudden act done, in a moment can change a person’s life.
It takes a long time to build. But everything can be lost in mere seconds if we are not careful.
People are fickle and life is short.
We must stay in prayer, be led of the Spirit, and be careful with our words, our thoughts, our deeds.
We all have more influence than we realize. And our words have power to build, or to destroy.
God help us to always be kind... Motivated by His LOVE!
affirmation: I guard my words and actions carefully, making sure all I say and do is done in love and not in haste.
verse: “No man liveth unto himself and no man dieth unto himself.” (Romans 14:7)

title: NOTHING BETTER!
body: You go to a concert, pay for a ticket, enjoy the show, and the next day you’ve forgotten it.

You go to a football game, buy a ticket, see the game, and the next day you have forgotten it. You go to church, enjoy the music, the preaching. You put something in the plate. The next day you have forgotten it. That is *not *what it is to be a believer. That is what it is to be a mere church-goer.
Going to church doesn’t make you a Christian any more than being in a garage makes you a car, or drinking milk makes you a calf.
Religion has become little more than a social event for some people. The world doesn’t need more church-goers. It needs more real Christians.
True Christianity is having an individual and personal relationship with Jesus Christ. It is connecting with God and letting Him take the
reigns of your life. It is having God’s love for your fellow man. Nothing is more praiseworthy.
NOTHING is higher, or more noble.
affirmation: To know You more and more is my one desire, Lord Jesus.
verse: “Follow Him and serve Him in spirit and in truth.” (John 4:24)

title: CROWNS
body: Salvation is a free gift. You can’t “work” for it.
You don’t have to worry about being “good enough” to “make it into Heaven”. You can never be good enough to deserve to go there anyway. You get there by simply accepting Jesus.
But your CROWN is another thing.
A lot of people seem to be confused about this one.
Your crown is your reward for being faithful. It is not your salvation. It is the prize for putting Him first, sharing His love, and being a blessing to others.
So, be faithful, day after day, in whatever place God has placed you.
Remember...
Your “wings” are free.
But you are going to have to EARN that CROWN!
affirmation: I share God’s Word and am a true example of His love to those around me.
verse: “The Son of Man shall come in the glory of His Father with His angels; and then He shall reward every man according to his works.” (Matthew 16:27)

title: BIG IN GOD’S EYES
body: A lot of people say they are waiting for God to use them. They are waiting to do some big glorious mission or job for God.
It’s almost like they are waiting for God to show them with a flash of lightning or do some big miracle for them like He did for Moses.
Well, God doesn’t do it that way. He doesn’t just take a man and make him great overnight. We don’t get to be nothing one day, and big and famous the next without going through the grades.
Don’t forget, Moses had to watch over sheep for 40 years before God could use him.
Before God can make you really great, He wants to see if you will be faithful right where He has you now. He wants YOU to start being a blessing to those around you.
THAT is *big *in God’s eyes.
affirmation: I know I am in the place You want me to be, dear Lord.
verse: “Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that He may exalt you in due time.” (1 Peter 5:6)

title: SIMPLE WISDOM
body: A child is happy, because he doesn’t complicate things. He doesn’t think things to death. He just observes, learns, enjoys, lives.
Life has become complicated. People have gotten so complicated. They over think everything. And they often create problems where there aren’t any.
Such is man’s tendency to want to have order in his life, to have everything in its place, for everything to go his way, to control things.
But sometimes that doesn’t give much room for God to work.
It’s when we relinquish control that God has a chance: A chance to move. A chance to show His power. A chance to bless, to prosper, and to do miracles.
It’s wise to be SIMPLE.
affirmation: I put my trust in You my Heavenly Father, as a child trusts a parent, and let You accomplish Your will in my life.
verse: “Except you become like little children you cannot enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.” (Matthew 18:3)

title: WHAT’S IT GONNA BE?
body: People talk about leaving a legacy for their children. Usually they are referring to leaving them money, houses, cars: THINGS! How sad.

Some people amass riches. They ratchet their lifestyles up and up to ever increasingly higher levels of luxury to where it has gotten crazy the disproportionate opulence and waste some of them enjoy. They think only of themselves and not of others. Even sadder is that they teach these things to their children.
Well, they better enjoy it while they can, because it’s not going to last.
The day will come for all of us when, if what we did was right, we will forever be grateful: grateful that we had the right sense of priorities.
Grateful that we made the RIGHT CHOICE!
affirmation: I live for more than just material things. If I die now I will be happy knowing I have done what I can for the Lord and others.
verse: “Beware of greed: for a man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.” (Luke 12:15)

title: THE JOURNEY
body: Love is what it is all about. In this life, and in the next, it’s all about love.
The only reason we are here, the only lesson we have to learn, and the only thing we can take with us, is the love we give and the love we share.
LOVE and you cannot lose!
GIVE and you will never lack!
LAUGH and you will never be alone!
DREAM big and believe in miracles!
And God will be with you every step of your journey through this life.
affirmation: Lord, help me to love those around me with Your pure and unselfish love, so that they may see Your light shining through me.
verse: “He that plants abundantly will also reap abundantly.” (2 Corinthians 9:6)

title: WHEN NOT TO PRAY
body: I have never been one who prays for hours or even days on end for something as if God didn’t hear me.

I do pray, and I absolutely do believe in the power of prayer, and there’s a time and place for everything, but sometimes I feel big long prayers, instead of being a sign of faith, are a sign of a LACK of faith.
I know God hears me the first time and I just don’t feel any need to ask Him again and again as if He were deaf.
God loves you and hears you the FIRST time.
Believe it and thank Him for the answer even if you don’t see it immediately.
Then, get busy doing something else until you do!
affirmation: I bring my cares to God in prayer, and let Him fill me with His peace. I leave everything in His hands. It’s that simple.
verse: “When ye pray use not vain repetitions as the heathen do, for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking. Be not like them: for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of before ye ask Him.” (Matthew 6:7-8)

title: THE WORD
body: You have to live in God’s Word if you want His power and blessings.
Just talking about God doesn’t do it. Neither does just praying.
It is His Word that brings life, strengthens, and sustains. If you read it, memorize it, and follow its light you will grow in the wisdom and knowledge of God.
If you don’t know where to start, begin with the New Testament.
Read it slowly, prayerfully.
Let it cleanse you, wash you, and free you from old mindsets that would keep you down.
God’s Word is TRUTH and it is LIFE.
And it alone is the secret to His power, His blessings, and everything you want in life.
affirmation: As I spend time in Your Word, O Lord, it brings life to my soul and light to my way. I read it daily to refresh my spirit and guide my actions.
verse: “Thy Word is a lamp unto my feet and a light unto my path.” (Psalms 119:105)

title: OUR WORST ENEMY
body: We become the things we tell ourselves.
We say we are fat, or old. We say we are poor, or dumb. We think we can’t do something, so we don’t even try. We think we are lacking in some area, so we accept that we will fail.
We become the sum total of the things we tell ourselves, both in word and thought.
We actually are our own worst enemy, because we are the biggest thing keeping us back! Our negativity kills us.
Only faith in God works. Only confidence that His power through us can do anything, guarantees success!
Never fear. Without Him you can’t do much. But with Him, you CAN do ANYTHING!
affirmation: I think positive thoughts about myself and my abilities, because I know have God’s power on my side and with Him I am strong and capable.
verse: “Thanks be unto God, who always causeth us to triumph in Christ.” (2 Corinthians 2:14)

title: START PRACTICING!
body: Science has proven that a tiny newborn baby needs to be touched. He needs to be cuddled to grow healthy.
Yet people are starving for affection. People are starving for love.
With so many of us here on the Earth, and with there being roughly an equal amount of both sexes, why are so many people lonely?
Because many think only of themselves. They are looking to receive. They haven’t realized that a happy relationship, a happy marriage, is where two people *give*.
When they both give, then they both receive at the same time. It’s really quite simple.
So if you want to find someone special, if you would like to have a lively, fun, rewarding, and satisfying relationship...
Start practicing for it NOW.
Learn to be GIVING!
affirmation: I look for ways to give to others, not only materially, but also of my time, attention, and caring.
verse: “The generous soul shall be made prosperous.” (Proverbs 11:25)

title: NO MORE RULES
body: Most people equate pleasing God with “legalism”, or a strict, excessive conformity to a set of rules.
That’s because many religions have taught this throughout the ages, and pretty much continue to do so to this day. But that’s not what Jesus taught.
Jesus came to abolish the old law. That means we don’t have to keep a whole big tedious set of religious and moral rules any more.
We now only have to keep one rule. For us now there is only one law: To love God, and love each other.
The ONLY thing that God asks of us now is that we be guided by LOVE in everything we do.
That’s it!
affirmation: I am free from the religious condemnation of the past. Free to do God’s will.
verse: “The Son hath made you free. Free indeed.” (John 8:36)

title: KILLING US ALL
body: People sure complain a lot these days!
It’s getting to be the norm for people to protest and complain. Speaking up is what they call it: in defense of an ever increasing number of so-called righteous causes and injustices.

Well, some people say that being passive isn’t going to fix anything. And that may be true. But everyone getting mad and bent out of shape over anything and everything all the time isn’t solving anything.
It isn’t making things any better. It’s only filling the world with more and more angry people who are bent out of shape! It’s killing us all.
Life is too short to die of the physical ailments caused by all of this stress.
Let’s all take a deep breath. Let’s all collectively count to ten.
We are better than this.
affirmation: I control my emotions and keep a positive attitude, despite the problems and injustices that I see. I have learned that kindness and humility bring more solutions than anger.
verse: “He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he that ruleth his own spirit than he that taketh a city.” (Proverbs 16:32)

title: AS SERVANTS
body: Walk humbly with God.
Let His love fill you, shine through you, and over flow from you, that you may be a blessing to others.
Let’s help one another more. Let’s serve each other humbly, and do it as unto the Lord.
Jesus said that when we are serving another, we are serving (and pleasing) God.
Jesus was a king, yet He walked among us as a servant. He was the greatest servant of all.
Should any of us think ourselves GREATER than Him?
affirmation: I serve You and others in humility, O Lord, knowing that any good in me comes from You.
verse: “Let nothing be done in strife or vainglory, but in lowliness of mind let each esteem the other better than himself.” (Philippians 2:3)

title: WHAT WILL IT TAKE?
body: Isn’t it funny how whenever there’s illness or a natural disaster people pray?
When the going gets rough, people who otherwise wouldn’t be caught praying, cry out to God for His protection and help.
Problems, illness, and bereavement are the only times some people look to the Lord.
The sad part is, they are missing out on so much by not seeking His help and guidance during the good times. That’s their loss.
What will it take to get *you *to pray?
If you are already close to the Lord, you have nothing to worry about.
affirmation: I stay close to you, Lord, when times are good, and not only when difficulties come my way.
verse: “As long as he sought the Lord, God made him to prosper.” (2 Chronicles 26:5)

title: JESUS’ ONLY MESSAGE
body: When it comes to God, many people are really screwed up.
There are tons of religious fanatics, extremists, weirdos, and loonies out there!
So many of them are convinced that they are the only right ones. They think that they alone see the whole truth, and that God is with them more than anyone else.
Some promote their political beliefs and bigotry, racism, hatred, war, and violence in the name of religion. They have lost sight of Jesus’ main message...
Jesus’ ONLY message: LOVE.
affirmation: I show the world what God is truly like by living in His love, rather than arguing over belief and doctrine.
verse: “No man has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God dwelleth in us, and His love is perfected in us.” (1 John 4:12)

title: THE POWERS THAT BE
body: We are supposed to obey the powers that be: our governors, presidents, and kings.
God’s Word says we should pray for them. There is really not much more to say about that.

We, as believers, instead of criticizing the government and our leaders, are here to glorify Christ, to exalt His name, tell others what great things He has done for us, and pray for those that are over us.
That is part of our mission.
That is our strength.
affirmation: I don’t waste my time cutting down the government, but instead I lift up Jesus, and talk about Him and what He is doing in my life.
verse: “Let prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks be made... for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in godliness and honesty.” (1 Timothy 2:1-2)

title: GOD DOES THINGS DIFFERENTLY
body: All through life one of the main things God seems to be trying to show us is the difference between how HE sees things, and how we see them.
So often when we don’t know what to do and we ask Him, He shows us something completely different from what we would have thought.
And what He shows us always works.
So, to find His solutions we first have to be willing to give up our own preconceived ideas.
When we do, He NEVER fails to come through for us!
affirmation: Without You, Lord, I am nothing and don’t know anything. Only You see the big picture.
verse: “God’s ways are not our ways.” (Isaiah 55:8)

title: FALLEN MAN
body: How far man has fallen!
He makes war and kills his neighbor.
He plays video games of making war and killing his neighbor.
He watches movies and television shows of killing his neighbor.
And he learns martial arts so that he can kill his neighbor. This is justified as self defense, of course.
Man studies war. He builds for war and practices war.
Imagine what the world would be like if man put half as much time and effort into learning to LOVE AND HELP his neighbor!
affirmation: I spend my time studying God’s Word, and learning things that will help me better the world around me.
verse: “How good and how pleasant it is to dwell together in harmony.” (Psalms 133:1)

title: THIS CRAZY WORLD
body: Have you ever felt like the world was full of idiots?
Everywhere you look people are doing dumb things, saying dumb things. It’s enough to drive anyone crazy.
Where can you go anymore to escape such madness? Who can you turn to?

The best thing we can do, the best defense we have, the best coping mechanism, is to be able to detach ourselves at times: Go fishing, have a drink or two, take a trip, get alone for a while. Whatever works for you!
Just please don’t forget to include the Lord.
Pray about life. Pray about things. God will answer. God will comfort your heart.
Only HE can give you the GRACE to face this crazy world!
affirmation: When this life seems overwhelming I get away for a while, and find rest, peace, strength, and a new outlook, through quiet contemplation with You, dear Lord.
verse: “In the morning, rising up a great while before day, He went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.” (Mark 1:35)

title: THINK YOU CAN’T?
body: You may think you need to be somewhere else.
You may think you need to be with someone different.
You may think you need to be doing something different, or have something else, or something more.
But time, place, and people don’t dictate our happiness. Nor do they define our usefulness for the Lord.
Most of the greatest thinkers, inventors, artists, and authors throughout history accomplished their greatest works without even leaving home.
And no man or woman is limited if God is on their side.
“God never puts us in a place too small to grow in.”
affirmation: I have everything I need right here and now to be content. With Your help, Lord, I can accomplish great things in my present situation.
verse: “Godliness with contentment is great gain.” (1 Timothy 6:6)

title: GET TO WORK!
body: It seems like everyone is looking for a handout these days.
People want to reap the benefits without putting in the hard work.
But everything that’s worth anything, costs something.
Unless you’re a tree, you’re going to have to get on the ball and make some sacrifices, before you can see any fruit.
You want CHANGE?
Then you are going to have to get to WORK!
affirmation: I am creating the life I want through my daily efforts to change my part of the world.
verse: “If any would not work, neither should he eat.” (2 Thessalonians 3:10)

title: BRIDGES
body: We all make mistakes.
And there are things we wish we could do over in our lives.
Well, we can’t do that. What’s past is past.
But God is in control, and if we learn from our mistakes, they can become opportunities for growth.
Someone once said: “May the bridges I burn light my way.”
And they can!
If we will but let them.
affirmation: I know that even my mistakes are something I can learn from, and that God is having His highest will in my life.
verse: “We know that all things work together for good to them that love God.” (Romans 8:28)

title: WHEN PEOPLE LOVE
body: We believe in love. We believe that God is the spirit of love.
We believe that when people who don’t even know God are loving, God is there.
We believe in a greater God, a God greater than church, religion, dogmas and denominations.
We believe in a simple God. We believe in a God that even a child can understand.
We believe that where there is love, *there *is
God.
We believe that God is the spirit of love. We believe in love.
affirmation: I live in love. God’s love is the guiding factor in all my actions and choices.
verse: “We have known and believed the love that God hath to us. God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.” (1 John 4:16)

title: HURRY HURRY
body: This old world is getting faster and faster every day. And people are in such a hurry. They eat in a hurry. They drive fast. They shop in a hurry. They talk fast.
Music is loud. Patience is thin. Health is made worse. People never stop moving.
Why are we in such a hurry to get nowhere? Why are we afraid to stop, slow down, and enjoy life? It’s a vicious cycle, and we’ll get caught up in it unless we make a break!
To learn from the Lord we have to go slow, stop everything, and listen. And when we do that, He brings peace to our souls.
But WE’VE got to stop the noise, get quiet, and tune in... to HIM!
affirmation: When my world starts spinning out of control, I stop everything and get quiet with God. Slowing down and spending time with Him brings peace and order back to my life.
verse: “In quietness and in confidence shall be your strength.” (Isaiah 30:15)

title: IT WORKS!
body: If you want to have a closer walk with the Lord, YOU are going to have to make more of an effort.
God doesn’t force Himself on anyone.
It is as we draw closer to Him that He reveals Himself more and more in our lives.
It is as we read the words of Jesus that we get to know Him better.
It is as we memorize His words that we become more like Him.
It is as we let Him take the reigns of our lives that He can take over and bestow upon us His love, His protection, His guidance, His healing, and perform MIRACLES in our daily lives.
That’s how it works!
affirmation: As I seek God’s guidance for each step of my life, and draw closer to Him every day, I receive more and more of His blessings.
verse: “Draw nigh unto God, and He will draw nigh unto you.” (James 4:8)

title: HAVE FUN!
body: When we come to the end of our lives God isn’t going to ask us who had the best time here on Earth!
He’s going to ask, “What did we do with our lives?” And it’s not going to be enough to say we had fun.
We are here for one thing: To glorify God in everything we do. We are here to learn to love God and love our neighbor as well.
We CAN have a good time while we are doing that. We can and should have fun while we’re here.
Let’s just not lose sight of the right perspective!
affirmation: Loving God and helping others takes first place in my life. That is my greatest joy.
verse: “Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep His commandments, for this is the whole duty of man. For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing, whether it be good, or whether it be evil.” (Ecclesiastes 12:13-14)

title: WHY DOES GOD? - Part 1
body: People ask all the time why God permits wars and bad things.
That’s a dumb question because if God stopped man from doing these things He would be controlling us. And we wouldn’t like that. People would sure complain then!
God gave man a free will. He doesn’t make us do good. And the hell the world is in today is the direct result of man’s ungodly and selfish choices. It’s that simple.
It is time we realize that being loving has its consequences, and being selfish and unloving does too.
Man is seeing the fruits of his choices today on earth: The results of selfish living and a lack of love toward his fellow man that have been going on for a long time now.
We can change all that.
But God won’t force us to!
affirmation: Rather than questioning God, I use the free will He has given me to be part of the solution to the problems in this world.
verse: “God will give unto everyone according to their works.” (Revelation 2:23)

title: WHY DOES GOD? - Part 2
body: Some people ask why God permits wars and bad things on earth, but they don’t really want to know why. Their minds are already made up.
They blame God and imply that He is unjust. Their attitude is: “If I were God, I wouldn’t permit these things.”
They are basically saying that God is wrong. God is unfair. That they are better than God. What arrogance!
Man himself has created the world’s injustices. Man by his own selfish choices has created the mess we are living in today. Not God.
There cannot be change without love. And things will not get better without God. Because only God’s pure love in man’s heart can ever make man do the right thing, love his neighbor...
And break this vicious cycle of GREED!
affirmation: I try to reflect the light of God’s love in this world to break the cycle of selfishness and greed that has brought about so many of man’s problems.
verse: “From whence come wars and fighting among you? Come they not of your greed?” (James 4:1)

title: EAT OR DIE
body: We eat. We grow.
We eat. Our bodies rebuild. We stop eating, we die.
We need healthy food. Junk food will take its toll sooner or later.
It’s the same for us spiritually.
We are surrounded by all types of spiritual junk food and tempted to eat it.
We find time for everything besides reading the Bible.
But God’s Word is our spiritual food. We eat it, we grow spiritually. We don’t eat it...
We die.
affirmation: I dedicate time to reading and learning from God’s Word, because I want to grow spiritually not just physically.
verse: “How shall a young man cleanse his ways? By taking heed to Thy Word.” (Psalms 119:9)

title: AT THE HELM
body: God wants to guide your vessel.
But you must let Him take the helm. You must let Him steer and not try to steer yourself.
He can guide you through the storm. He can keep you away from the reefs.
Only God can bring you to a safe harbor.
Cry out to God. Seek His help and guidance. You will never make it on your own. It is a liberation to give up our own stubbornness, our own ways.
It is only then, that we will find that place of peace, that place of rest.
But we’ve got to let GOD take the helm of our lives.
affirmation: I give you the helm of my life, Lord. Guide me through the stormy waters of life.
verse: “I will instruct and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go. I will guide thee with mine eye.” (Psalms 32:8)

title: THE WAY WE LIVE
body: True religion is having a personal and individual relationship with the Lord.
It is to open our lives as well as our hearts to God and each other.
It is not about being all holy and looking down on people who don’t meet our standards.
It’s about humbly seeking God’s guidance and letting His love direct our actions, our words, and our thoughts.
True religion is having compassion for the weak, helping the needy, accepting those who are different, and forgiving those who have wronged us.
True religion is about breaking down walls, talking less, listening more, teaching with meekness...
And showing God to others by the way we LIVE our lives!
affirmation: I am teaching more by example than with my words.
verse: “Be ye therefore followers of Christ as dear children, and walk in love.” (Ephesians 5:1-2)

title: STAY LOVING
body: The planet is starving for love. People are starving for tenderness, for compassion and warmth.
People try to replace love with power or position, knowledge, and any number of things that don’t satisfy.
Then they toughen themselves and harden their hearts, and put on a pretense of lacking nothing.
Love is often despised. It is seen by many today as a weakness.
But the day will come when love will win. We will see the day when light will conquer the darkness, right will straighten all wrongs, and the meek will inherit the earth.
Until then... Stay loving!
affirmation: I know love will always triumph. Fill me with Your love, Lord, that I might pour it out to others.
verse: “Love never fails.” (1 Corinthians 13:8)

title: PRAISE
body: Praising the Lord is a funny thing.
Most people seem to not praise the Lord.
Why is that? It’s like they are uncomfortable for some reason. Maybe they worry about what people will think.

Maybe some don’t do it because they don’t want to look like some denominations that make a lot of noise and racket shouting to the Lord in their church services. Whatever the reason may be, we really should praise the Lord.
We praise the Lord because He likes it and there is no quicker way to obtain His favor. We please Him by praising Him and thanking Him for our many blessings.
There are two times to praise the Lord: When you feel like it. And when you don’t feel like it.
So then, we really have no good excuse to NOT praise the Lord!
affirmation: I praise You, Lord, because I love You and I want to make You happy.
verse: “I will bless the Lord at all times: His praise shall continually be in my mouth.” (Psalms 34:1) “Let everything that hath breath praise the Lord.” (Psalms 150:6)

title: A SHOULDER
body: Everyone wants someone to talk to. Everybody wishes someone would listen to them.
When someone cares enough to listen, it makes us feel like we’re important. It somehow validates us. It makes us feel like we’re needed here in this world.
When we find someone who is easy to talk to we immediately like them. And there is no greater snub than to feel that someone is intentionally ignoring us.
We all want a shoulder to lean on. We also all sometimes need a shoulder to cry on. So deep is this desire in man’s heart. So profound is this basic need.
Some of us never fully find it in this life. That’s because God wants to fill all our needs. He wants us to find comfort in Him. He is our refuge.
God wants us to lean on Him, not man.
affirmation: You, Lord, are the only one who is always here for me, loves me unconditionally, and never lets me down.
verse: “I love the Lord, because He hath heard my voice and my supplications, because He hath inclined his ear unto me. Therefore will I call upon Him as long as I live.” (Psalms 116:1-2)

title: HALFHEARTS
body: Whatever we do, we should do with all of our hearts. If we are going to do something we might as well do it cheerfully.
That’s the problem nowadays: Everywhere you look you see people going through the motions. They are looking at the time-clock just waiting to go home.
The world is full of half-hearts.
If you want to stand out as exceptional, start doing everything wholeheartedly. Start doing everything with love. Go the extra mile.
People will like you more. You will like yourself more.
And God will bless you more!
affirmation: Whatever I do, I put my whole heart into it, and really give it my best effort.
verse: “Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do; do it with all thy might.” (Ecclesiastes 9:10)

title: YOU HAVE IT BAD?
body: You think you have it bad? You don’t have it bad! Just read any history book.

Since the beginning of time war has been waged. Countries were invaded. Their cities were plundered and burned. Their men killed. Their women raped and taken into captivity. All kinds of ugly things happened. You can read about them in history books, and they are even happening today!
But now people whine if their train is late, when the Internet is slow, if their coffee is not hot enough, gas prices go up, or they don’t get the window seat on an airplane.
People complain about all kinds of frivolous things. They are easily offended. People call *foul *for the most ridiculous reasons.
If we say we love God, let’s be better examples to the world by being more forgiving, more tolerant, not whine, and by taking things more in stride.
We never had it so GOOD!
affirmation: I stand firmly upon the Rock. I do not whine or complain, because I am mightily blessed.
verse: “Let us run with patience the race that is set before us, looking unto Jesus.” (Hebrews 12:1-2)

title: MERE WORDS
body: How did Jesus change the world?
With His words.
Jesus changed the course of mankind with His words.
How can we change the world?
We can spend our lives trying to feed the poor. We can spend our time looking for a cure to cancer, AIDS, malaria, or depression.
We can throw trillions of dollars at scientific research, political parties, space exploration, or war.
But we will never come close to making an impact anywhere near as big as Jesus did in only three years with nothing more than His words.
His words made the difference.
And it’s only by spreading His Word that we can TOO.
affirmation: I believe in You, Lord, and the power of Your Words. I am doing my part to share them with those around me.
verse: “The words that I speak unto you are spirit and they are life.” (John 6:63)

title: THE ONLY THING
body: Man has followed his own path for so long now, that even the most basic truths and knowledge of God have become foreign concepts to many.
Morals have eroded. Integrity is growing scarcer. Religion has so many people confused as to what God is really like and what He expects of us all.
In such a rapidly changing world as ours, what can you hold on to?
LOVE is the anchor.
LOVE is the answer.
LOVE is the only thing that brings meaning and hope to man’s heart.
LOVE is the only thing that matters.
affirmation: When I give love, I can’t go wrong, because love is the most important thing in life.
verse: “He that loveth not knoweth not God, for God is love.” (1 John 4:8)

title: TALK TO GOD
body: When you don’t know what to do, the worst thing you can do is to share your problems with everyone around you.
Your friends, co-workers, and relatives, more often than not, can’t help you. They have their own problems. They really can’t begin to fully understand your situation and what you are going through.
Only God knows. Only God understands.
Only God walks ahead of you and sees the pitfalls, knows where the dangers lie, and only He can lead you to your safe haven.
So when you have a problem, the first thing you should do is take it to the Lord in prayer. God wants a little credit.
Talk to HIM!
affirmation: I seek counsel and guidance from You, dear Jesus. You help and instruct me like no other ever can.
verse: “I will bless the Lord, who hath given me counsel, and instructs me in the night seasons. I have set the Lord always before me. Because He is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.” (Psalms 16:7-8)

title: DON’T LISTEN!
body: It’s not wise to engage in gossip.
Some people spend their lives talking about others, complaining about their mate, child, friend, neighbor, or loved one.
If you listen to them and try to get involved, you will be the only one to lose. You may even end up losing a friend.
The only way you can win is to not take sides. Don’t even listen to gossip in the first place.
Change the subject. Think of something urgent you have to do, and walk away. Do this consistently. Let it be known that you are not interested in criticizing others.
People will respect you more. They’ll know that you will never be talking about THEM behind their backs.
As Christians, we should lift people up in prayer. We should PRAY specifically for them.
Enough said!
affirmation: I don’t participate in gossip. I refuse to even listen to it.
verse: “In the multitude of words there lacketh not sin. But he that closeth his lips is wise.” (Proverbs 10:19)

title: LIKE WATER
body: Many people keep God out of their decision making process. No wonder they fail. They cause themselves needless pain.
They may listen to God once in a while, and then He helps them. He solves their problem, or gets them out of some mess.
But they forget Him quickly, and the next time around they go ahead without Him and they fail.
When we seek God’s help He answers.
His Holy Spirit is like water. It flows to the lowest places. And it’s when we realize we don’t have all the answers that He has a chance to work in our life.
God wants to help us in our daily lives, but we have got to let Him.
affirmation: I can’t do anything without You Lord. Thank You for always being there for me.
verse: “Seek the Lord and His strength. Seek His face continually.” (1 Chronicles 16:11)

title: HOW WE SEE GOD
body: You can tell a lot about where a person is at spiritually by the way they pray.
Some people pray to God as if He were high and lofty and distant. They see God as strict, rigid, formal, and far away.
That describes their relationship with God.
Others pray to God like they would talk to a dear friend. They feel relaxed, comfortable, and at home with Him. They see God as someone close, someone who is always there for them.
That describes their relationship with God.
Yes, you certainly can tell a lot about where a person is at spiritually, by the way they pray.
affirmation: I know you are always near, dear Lord, that You hear me, and I have nothing to fear.
verse: “How far away God seems to you is a measure of the distance YOU have put between yourself and God.”

title: ONE OR THE OTHER
body: A Christian is either: Looking for encouragement, or encouraging others.
Murmuring about what he *thinks *he lacks, or counting his blessings.
Wasting time trying to find *the way*, or being a blessing right where he is at.
Seeking to be fed spiritually, or feeding others.
He analyzes, or he enjoys.
He agonizes, or he lives.
He is restless, or he has peace.
His life is drudgery, or a dream.
He is one or the other.
How do you know which one you are? By the WORDS that come out of your mouth!
affirmation: Thank You for how wonderfully You are moving in my life, Lord. I testify of Your goodness to others.
verse: “Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.” (Romans 14:19)

title: TOO LATE
body: When we grow old and tired and our usefulness is waning, what kind of memories will we have of our lives?

We have visited many nursing homes and seen a lot of sad and lonely people. Some are sad because they have been forgotten by their families. Others because of failing health. But saddest of all are those who realize it’s too late. They wasted their lives and now realize that time has run out for them to do anything significant for God or His Work.

They may have lived selfishly making a lot of money. They may have wasted their lives on drink. They maybe had been powerful and commanded respect of everyone around them. But now they are all in the same boat and the clock is ticking.
NOW is the only time we own. What will YOUR memories be?
affirmation: I am putting You, Lord, in fi	place during my life here on earth.
verse: “Remember now thy Creator in the days of thy youth, while the evil days come not. Before the silver cord be loosed, and man goeth to his long home. Then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.” (Ecclesiastes 12:1,5-7)

title: WHO CAN UNDERSTAND?
body: Some things can’t be changed.
Many things we may never fully understand in this life.
Men will never completely understand women. Nor will women ever understand men. And none of us can understand all the mysteries of the universe.
Here on Earth natural disasters, famines, man made calamities, and wars seem to only be more common every day. And who can understand them?
The bigger man’s woes grow, the smaller we feel.
We may not fully understand God, but that doesn’t mean we cannot benefit from His help.
God did not promise that we would understand it all. He just promises to be with us every step of the way.
Trust Him.
affirmation: There are many things in life which I will never understand, but I know my God has never and will never let me down.
verse: “For the mountains shall depart, and the hills be removed, but my kindness shall not depart from thee.” (Isaiah 54:10)

title: A LOT HAPPIER!
body: Some people think that drinking is “a sin”, or dancing, sex, or any number of other things. They criticize what other people are doing. Even breast feeding a baby is dirty and shameful to some of these people. They are seriously messed up.
But it’s really not the things we do that matter to God as much as if we have love in our hearts, if we do things with love, or with a lack of love. God’s only law is love.
