Love is the ONLY yardstick by which our lives will be judged.
If people spent less time condemning others for things that maybe for them are not even sins, if they spent even half their time focused on being more loving, the world would be a much better place.
And we would ALL be a lot happier!
affirmation: What we do doesn’t matter as much to God as whether or not we do it with love.
verse: “Let all your things be done with love.” (1 Corinthians 16:14)

title: COME EMPTY, LEAVE FULL
body: If you seek an answer from the Lord you will find it. If you knock, the door will be opened. If you really want the solution you will have it.
But the key is: you have to really *want *it.
You must want it enough to be willing to give up any preconceived ideas and conditions you have.
You must be open to God’s answer.
You must come to Him with an empty heart, an open heart, and accept whatever He wants to show you.
If you do you will not be disappointed.
God always answers the hungry heart.
ALWAYS!
affirmation: I come to You, Lord, with an open heart, willing to do whatever You may ask. I know You will not fail to answer and show me Your way.
verse: “Ask and it shall be given you, seek and ye shall find, knock and it shall be opened unto you.” (Matthew 7:7)

title: HARD OR SOFT?
body: We have hard liquor, hard lemonade, hard bodies, and hard hearts.
We are supposed to be “tough”, not let anyone “push us around”, and “show no mercy”. Guns are “great”, so are fists and big bikes. Being hard is “cool”.
“Soft” is weakness. Soft is for sissies, losers and toilet paper. Showing love is “wimpy”. Showing affection is “not cool”.
That’s many people’s attitude today.
But our war is not physical. Our battle is in the spiritual. We are soldiers of the cross. Truth, love, and kindness are our weapons.
Real men love Jesus, and love will win in the end.
Until then...
Keep a tough spirit, but a soft heart.
affirmation: I stay strong, but am guided by compassion.
verse: “The wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and easy to be entreated, full of mercy, and good fruits.” (James 3:17)

title: EACH ONE OF US
body: We will each be held accountable for the truth we’ve received.
Someone who lives in the wilderness and has never even seen a Bible will be judged by how much love they had in their heart during this life, by how caring they were in their dealings with others.
And those who know God and His Word, will be held responsible for living up to the amount of Truth they each had.
He who received much will be held more responsible than he who received little.
God is fair. God is just.
Each one of us will be judged equally, by the same yardstick of love: Love for God, and love for our fellow man...
God is good, isn’t He!
affirmation: I live in love, according to the truth and guidance I have received from God and His Word.
verse: “Unto whom much is given, of him shall much be required.” (Luke 12:48)

title: MADE UP MINDS
body: Do you know why many people don’t seek God’s help?
It’s because they’re afraid God will tell them something different from what they want to hear.
Their minds are made up. They have their own agenda, and they want to do things their way. How sad it is that they don’t realize His love for them. They miss out on so much.
God can’t bless you as long as you think you can do things without Him.
It is only when we forsake our own will that we can find His. It is only when we give our all to God that He gives His all to us!
affirmation: Have Your way in my life, Lord, and may Your will be done, not mine.
verse: “Cast all your cares upon Him, for He cares for you.” (1 Peter 5:7)

title: IN THEIR SHOES
body: People criticize the government. People criticize law enforcement. And people criticize their neighbors. People just like to criticize.
But I imagine it’s not easy to run a government. And cops sure don’t have an easy job. And how can anyone know what your neighbor is really going through?
Let’s show more compassion. Let’s show a little more understanding, and not be so quick to judge.
And instead of being so quick to criticize people...
Put ourselves in their shoes!
affirmation: Instead of criticizing others I speak positively of them, and try to put myself in their shoes.
verse: “Speak not evil of one another, brethren.” (James 4:11)

title: DON’T NEED THEM!
body: Many people go to church on Sundays.
But for every person you see in church there are a dozen more who don’t want anything to do with church. Why is that? It’s because they are fed up with people telling them what to do.
Some people say that organized religion has turned more people away from God than toward Him.

It seems that some people are never going to wake up and stop shoving their religion on others. They are never going to stop trying to guilt trip people into their way of thinking. Jesus said to avoid them. (See Matthew 15:14)
Love God. Read your Bible, the New Testament especially. Get guidance from the source, the pure crystalline water of God’s Word.
That’s all you need. Not THEM!
affirmation: God leads and guides me directly through His Word, and I follow His voice.
verse: “Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.” (John 15:3)

title: WHAT WILL LAST
body: King Solomon said: “The heart of man is never satisfied.” He should know. He could have anything he wanted.
Look at the rich, the famous, the powerful today. They always want more!
Billionaires want to get more and more money. Celebrities want to always be the center of attention. The powerful can’t get enough control.
And it’s not only them. Many of the poor are trying to be like the rich, and are getting greedier.
What a sad world. Look at what our world has become. Thank God this will not last forever.
The things of this world are fleeting. Things can never truly satisfy. They last mere moments and are quickly forgotten.
Only God remembers.
Only what we do with love for others will
last.
affirmation: You, Lord, are all I want and need in this life. You are the only thing that truly satisfies.
verse: “The world will pass away and the desires thereof, but he that does the will of God abides forever.” (1 John 2:17)

title: DON’T LOOK BACK
body: Are you having trouble letting go of
your pride? Do you find it hard to forgive? Are bitterness and negative thinking keeping you back?
You’re not alone.
Do you want to get rid of these things? Are you tired of them controlling you? Do you want this change?
You cannot do it alone.
But you are already halfway there. Now, give them all to God in prayer. Ask Him to take them away and to fill you with His Holy Spirit of love.
Let God cleanse you. Let Him set you free.
Believe that He is able. Receive it from Him as done.
Then don’t look back.
affirmation: I receive Your freedom of forgiveness, Jesus. Cleanse me and fill me with Your Holy Spirit.
verse: “He is able to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by Him, seeing He ever liveth to make intercession for them.” (Hebrews 7:25)

title: DO WHAT YOU CAN
body: A lot of people seem to think that they’re supposed to sit around doing nothing while they wait for God to do something.
They’re waiting for God to do something *for* them, rather than with them.
But they are not really waiting on God: God’s waiting on them to get busy!
Finding God’s will is not just sitting around doing nothing. It’s doing what you can. The secret is: to get busy DOING something while you’re waiting.
If you don’t know where to go or how to do something, just get started.
And then God will guide you with the rest!
affirmation: I keep busy doing what I know I can today, and trust God to guide my future.
verse: “Thine ears shall hear a word behind thee saying: This is the way, walk ye in it.” (Isaiah 30:21)

title: OUR CHEESE PUFF WORLD
body: Cheese puffs are a perfect illustration of what our world has become.
Light and airy. Enticing smell. Full of artificial, cheesy, zero nutrition nothingness.
Yet you can’t get enough of them!
Everything in this world is becoming veneer. Skin deep. Temporary. Bombard your senses... for mere moments.
All show. No go. Fancy wrappers. Zero substance. Eye appeal. Perfect smell. Tantalizing texture. Empty ending.
Only Jesus offers substance. Only His love can satisfy...
FOREVER!
affirmation: Lord, You are the One who brings meaning and substance to my life, and makes everything worthwhile.
verse: “He hath called you out of darkness into His marvelous light.” (1 Peter 2:9)

title: THE ROOT
body: People dream of it. People lust for it. People work for it, play for it, cheat for it, and kill for it.
Couples split up over it. Neighbors fight because of it. Teams compete for it. Nations start wars for it.
It is some people’s inspiration. It is others’ infatuation. And it is everyone’s degradation.
Money has ruined more people, broken up more homes, perverted more hearts, and destroyed more lives than anything else on Earth. Greed is the cause of so much of man’s suffering.
The love of money is the root of ALL of man’s ills.
affirmation: I keep money in its proper place. I do not allow greed into my life.
verse: “For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.” (1 Timothy 6:10)

title: PEACE IN THE MIDST OF NOISE
body: Noise, noise, noise! Everywhere you go these days there is noise: people talking, televisions blaring, traffic raging, music playing.
We went to the mountains the other day hoping to enjoy the peace and quiet. We wanted to hear to the birds singing, to listen to the whisper of the wind through the trees.
But we couldn’t get away from the noise!
People take noise with them everywhere it seems! And there is not much we can do about it.
It is important that we find a quiet place, some time, somewhere, if we are to hear from the Lord.
It is only when we are still, that we receive His guidance...
And partake of HIS peace.
affirmation: Each day I make time to be alone with the Lord, and receive His peace and His instructions for my day.
verse: “Commune with your own heart, and be still before the Lord.” (Psalms 4:4)

title: LOVE HONESTLY
body: Many people have trouble being honest and expressing their feelings. This is especially true when it comes to love and romance.
We get in all kinds of trouble when we are not honest with ourselves. We create many problems by not being honest with our loved ones and those closest to us.
It is when we lovingly share and open our hearts, that God has an opportunity to work. And it is when we come into His presence in prayer, together, that God can show His power:
Power to strengthen. Power to build. Power to heal.
affirmation: With Your help, Lord, I can have open, honest, and loving communications with my loved one and work out all problems.
verse: “A three fold cord is not quickly broken.” (Ecclesiastes 4:12)

title: GET IT OUT!
body: Get out the message! Start today.
The pen IS mightier than the sword.
Getting out God’s message of hope and love is the most important thing you can do.
More important than making money, getting things, or having fun.
It is one of the few things you can take with you from this world, and you will never regret it.
What you do to share God’s love will last for Eternity!
So, get your priorities straight:
And do something to get out the message.
Today!
affirmation: I take each opportunity I can to share God’s message of love with others. I know that is the best gift I can give them.
verse: “They that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament; and they that turn many to righteousness as the stars forever and ever.” (Daniel 12:3)

title: IT CAN ONLY BE EXPERIENCED
body: The thing about the Lord is...
When you receive Him into your life, you know He is with you. And you know that no matter what could possibly happen, He will ALWAYS be there for you.
This is something difficult to explain to those who don’t know Him. But it’s true.
It is something that you can’t explain: you can only experience.
His is the purest love. His is the truest love.
Open your heart to Him and you will see.
You will KNOW!
affirmation: You are the One who gives purpose, meaning, and direction to my life, Lord. Thank You for being my best friend.
verse: “O taste and see that the Lord is good: Blessed is the man that trusteth in Him.” (Psalms 34:8)

title: GIVE UP!
body: Until we come to the end of ourselves, God will never be able to use us as much as He would like.
We have to get to the point in our lives where we realize that we don’t know. We have got to realize that our best efforts are not enough.
God can’t use us as long as we are full of ourselves, full of our own ways, full of our own ideas.
It is only when we surrender all, that God can
That’s the mystery of serving the Lord.
That’s the beauty of God.
Let go. Give up! You have nothing to lose... And EVERYTHING to gain!
affirmation: I give You everything, Lord. I need Your help and Your strength in my life.
verse: “He must increase, and I must decrease.” (John 3:30)

title: WHAT ARE YOU PLANTING?
body: If we plant corn, we will reap corn. We reap the fruits of what we plant.
How we treat others, affects how others treat us.
If we treat others bad, we can expect the same. The same holds true if we are kind and considerate.
There’s no way out of it.
What GOES out COMES back to us.
affirmation: I am investing in the future by planting good, positive things in my life.
verse: “Whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.” (Galatians 6:7)

title: CHOOSE YE
body: Devils and demons, witches, vampires, and sorcerers are increasingly commonplace today. “Wicked” is somehow cool now, as are darkness, spells, and the super powers of super heroes.
Kids of course are enchanted with their spell, and parents sit back and do nothing, convinced that it’s all just make believe fun and games.
We are in a battle royal between good and evil for the hearts and souls of men not only in this world, but also in the world to come.
You can laugh and say it’s not so, but in the end you will see how real it all was.
Choose WISELY.
affirmation: I glorify God and His works of light. I know the Devil and his darkness is not just a game, and I want nothing to do with them.
verse: “Be strong in the Lord, and in the power of His might. For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places.” (Ephesians 6:10,12)

title: MANY WAYS
body: God speaks in many ways.

God can speak through His Word. Sometimes when you are reading the Bible all of a sudden a particular passage may stand out to you. And you just know that at that specific moment in time it is the answer to something you were asking God about.
God may speak through family, a friend or neighbor, sometimes without them even realizing it.

God can speak in funny ways. Maybe you hear a few words in passing by someone on the street, and something clicks: You know it was for you. God can speak through a song, a poem, anything. God can speak to us any way He wants.
The Bible says He even spoke through an ass one time. Read it! (Num. 22:20-35)
Stay close to the Lord and sensitive to His voice, and He will speak to YOU!
affirmation: I am open to Your voice, Lord. Thank You for always answering my prayers and speaking to me.
verse: “God answers anywhere, any time. Every time we believe.”

title: LIFE TO THE FULL
body: Some people believe in God and the spiritual.
Other people believe in science.

But people are mistaken when they think they have to choose between the two. They err in thinking that the two are different, that the two are incompatible. Science and the spiritual are like two sides of the same coin. One could not exist without the other.

We live in physical bodies, and our spirits also live inside these same bodies. We think with our minds, but we also have a spiritual side that feels, perceives, and senses the unseen realities of the spirit world.
Our physical world is a mere reflection of the spiritual. And it is when we are in tune with both that the fullness of life is found.
affirmation: I am grateful there is more than the physical, and for the deeper realities of the spiritual realm and their benefits in my life.
verse: “For the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen being understood by the things that are made, even His eternal power and Godhead; so that they are without excuse.” (Romans 1:20)

title: STOP CONTROLLING
body: Many people keep God at arm’s length. Even people who profess to know God only give Him a few minutes of their day. That’s why God can’t bless them more.
How can God lead you and guide you when you don’t even take time to look to Him for guidance?
To the degree you put God first in your life, not in word, but by your actions, He will bless you. He will strengthen you, and guide you. He will become real to you.
But you’ve got to give up your own ways, your own plans. You have to give up your own agenda, and stop trying to do things in your own strength.
You’ve got to stop telling God how to do things. You’ve got to stop trying to control God...
And let GOD start controlling YOU!
affirmation: I give You my time, my will, and my plans, dear Lord. I need your guidance and help in my life.
verse: “As long as he sought the Lord, God made Him to prosper.” (2 Chronicles 26:5)

title: WHAT DO YOU SEE?
body: We each, individually, choose how we see things.

We choose to see things in a loving context and try to relate to people and agree with them, or we find fault, take offense, disagree with something and even sometimes demonize the person who did it or said it.
In general we tend to see either good or bad.
What we see depends a lot on what we are looking for. We can actually sometimes bring out the good or the bad in someone, just by how we treat them and interact with them.
What are YOU looking for?
affirmation: I choose to see the good in the people and situations around me, and to show love and tolerance.
verse: “Love... thinketh no evil.” (1 Corinthians 13:5)

title: THE BIBLE TELLS US SO
body: Some people have made a god out of their religion, their particular church, denomination or belief. They think they are the only right ones.
But they have lost sight of what God really wants of us. They seem to not realize Christ’s teachings: that LOVE is more important. To love God, and to love eachother.
They have replaced this truth with trying to be “good” on the outside. They think they can be pure enough, sinless enough, perfect enough, and holy enough to make it into Heaven.
But beloved, we won’t be able to take our self righteousness with us after this life.
ONLY what is done in LOVE will last.
Humility will be rewarded.
The Bible tells us so!
affirmation: Lord, let all my actions be guided by Your love and spirit of humility, and not by the opinions of others.
verse: “He that is greatest among you shall by your servant. And he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.” (Matthew 23:11-12)

title: OH OH!
body: There are a lot of problems in the world today, and they do need to be addressed.
But it seems like most of the time all you hear are people complaining about petty little things.
A word is misspoken, something’s done unintentionally, and they make such a big stink out of it! They get on their high horse and rant and rave... over nothing! People are so unforgiving.
It’s really ridiculous.
We all need to take things more in stride. We need to realize that we are all guilty of the same things, and that none of us should think we are any better than anyone else.
Only to the degree that we forgive others, will we ourselves be forgiven.
Oh oh!
Think about THAT one!
affirmation: I give my time and attention to addressing the things that really matter, and leave everything else in the Lord’s hands.
verse: “For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged, and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.” (Matthew 7:2)

title: DISCOVER
body: Stop everything. Get quiet. Ask God to lead you... and then open your Bible to the New Testament.
Read it. Slowly.
Sometimes it helps to read out loud.
If at first you don’t understand something, look a little bit further down the page.
You don’t have to understand everything. Just look for golden nuggets that hit home. Parts that speak to you. Passages that you like.
Underline them with a red pen. Write one down. Memorize it and review it throughout your day. And you will grow in wisdom and knowledge of God.
Discover the treasures within God’s Word!
affirmation: The more I read and study God’s Word, the more treasures I find, and the more He speaks to me and guides me through it.
verse: “For the Word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any two-edged sword, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.” (Hebrews 4:12)

title: TO DO OR NOT TO DO
body: When we come to the end of our lives, more than regretting some things we did, we will regret a lot of things we didn’t do.
No matter how good our intentions were, we will wish we had sometimes done more.
Lord help us to be proactive and do what we can, day in and day out for You and for others. Lord help us to seek out ways to be a blessing.
Lord help us to serve others with love.
Jesus said to: “Do unto others as we want others to do to us.” The key word here is “do”.
When we get to Heaven we are going to see that many things that went wrong were not God’s fault, nor were they even the devil’s, but they were ours because of the things we DIDN’T DO.
How tragic is that!
affirmation: I have the power to create change in the world and with Your help, Lord, I am doing it.
verse: “Be ye doers and not hearers only.” (James 1:22)

title: WHICH AM I?
body: Some people give of themselves unselfishly and sacrificially. They give not only of their substance, but they give of themselves, they help, they put other people’s needs above their own.
Others only think of themselves and what’s in it for them, what they can get out of it. They are always looking to receive.
Probably most of us are a little bit of both, sad to say.
God is a giver. He is always giving life and light to the world. God is always there for us and quick to respond to our needs.
He understands. His love is never ending.
And He always receives us with open arms. Lord, help us all to be more like YOU!
affirmation: God wants me to be more like Him, and it is by giving that I will receive.
verse: “God loves the cheerful giver.” (2 Corinthians 9:7)

title: GOD HEARS YOU
body: It is a common belief nowadays that the more people you have praying for a specific thing, the more likely God will answer you.
But God isn’t deaf.
You don’t have to be down on your hands and knees praying frantically to be heard.
God hears the silent prayer of one little child of His as much as He hears the screaming of a multitude. He’s big enough to rule the universe, yet small enough to live inside your heart!
Jesus said, “Ask and you shall receive. Seek and you shall find.”
God hears each of our prayers, and He hears yours.
God cares.
God DOES answer prayer!
affirmation: I know that God hears me always.
verse: “Call unto me and I will answer thee.” (Jeremiah 33:3)

title: THERE ARE PEOPLE
body: Some people think drinking is a sin. Some think dancing is a sin. There are people who think all kinds of dumb things are sins.
There are people who are pure in their own eyes. Some think they have a monopoly on God. There are some who condemn others and turn them away from God with their poor example.
These people haven’t figured out what God REALLY is all about. Don’t listen to them. Don’t follow them. Stay away from them.
Love God. Do good. Live in His love. After all, you will stand before GOD some day.
Not THEM!
affirmation: I let my life be guided by God and His love, regardless of what others say.
verse: “The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord shall be safe.” (Proverbs 29:25)

title: EASY AS PIE!
body: Many people work day after day, stuck in the meaningless drudgery of a 9 to 5, month after month, year after year. They have little more than television as an escape to help take the edge off their existence: help them cope with it all.
They wish they could do more to make a difference. But they have so little free time. They have little strength left after the physical toll from their grueling jobs.
If you sometimes feel this way, remember, the Internet has opened up doors of opportunity from which we can touch the lives of many from the comfort of our own homes.
It doesn’t take much. A few positive thoughts sent out day after day, over time, can make a huge difference in people’s lives. It takes only a moment.
Why not start doing that today? You can make a difference by sharing the truth you already have with your loved ones. It’s easy as pie!
affirmation: I share with others the truth You, Lord, have given me.
verse: “He that withholds will become poor, but he that shares shall be made rich.” (Proverbs 11:24)

title: THIN LINES, & THIN SKINNED!
body: There are so many thin lines in the world today. And we have to walk carefully to navigate them all. You have to walk a thin line when it comes to everything you say and everything you do.
And what seems to be a thin line to you, may not be the same to someone else. You may inadvertently cross a line that you didn’t even realize was there!

These thin lines are never well defined. They are not always so black and white. Perceptions differ. Beliefs are defined by upbringing and past experiences. And what seems perfectly acceptable to one person may not be to another.
It’s getting increasingly harder to please every- one, and there are some people you can never please.
One thing is certain: All of these thin lines are not making anyone happier. They are just making everyone more miserable.
We need to stop being so THIN SKINNED!
affirmation: Lord help me to lighten up, and not take myself or others too seriously.
verse: “Every man ought not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think.” (Romans 12:3)

title: MAN’S LONGING
body: Birds frolic in the sky. Squirrels play in the trees, as do horses in the field, fish in the sea...
Multiplication is the name of the game. From the tiniest microorganism to the largest of jungle animals, each one seeks its mate.
No wonder people feel lonely. Every other creature seems to find their mate.
That’s because man’s lonely feeling was put there by God. And that deep longing in his heart can only be filled with God.
But once you fill it...
Then God may very well give you the PERFECT mate!
affirmation: I love You, Lord, and put You first in my life. I trust that You will give me what’s best for me in Your perfect time.
verse: “Seek ye first the Kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things will be added unto you.” (Matthew 6:33)

title: GOD IS NEAR
body: You live in a state of constant confusion. Your day is full of noise and people talking. No wonder it’s hard to hear the Lord.
You scarcely give Him a few minutes of your time.
You’re surprised when things unravel and you are exhausted at the end of your day.
The weeks pass by, the months and years. Your life is wasting away. Your health too.
Something has to change.
You have to stop running. You have to slow it down. You, and ONLY you can make that change.
You are dear to God.
And God is very near to the soul that cries out to Him with their WHOLE heart.
affirmation: I am the only one who with God’s help can bring the change I want in my life.
verse: “Those who seek Me early shall find Me.” (Proverbs 8:17)

title: LOVE IS SAYING YOU’RE SORRY
body: Have you noticed how some people have trouble saying “I’m sorry”?
It’s sad that people seem to consider it a weakness. How totally wrong that is.
When we feel injured or slighted by someone or something they said or did, it sure means a lot to all of us, it sure does a lot of good to hear those magic words, “I’m sorry.”
Those two magic words can mend many a mess.

Let’s be more generous with our apologies, and not make excuses. Let’s be more loving, considerate, and kind to those around us and truly do as Jesus said: “Do unto others as we would have them do unto us.”
Love is saying, “I’m sorry.”
affirmation: I seek forgiveness of others when I have wronged them, because I know I would like to be treated the same.
verse: “Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful.” (Luke 6:36)

title: KNOW IT ALLS
body: Do you know why people are so empty nowadays? It’s because everyone is so full of themselves!
People seem to think the whole world revolves around them. They think they know everything. Everybody yaks non stop.

They voice their opinion on all kinds of things, most of which they know very little about. They think they should give their opinion on just about every situation, event, and person imaginable. They think that’s somehow cool.
But in God’s eyes we would be smarter to realize we know very little and that only He sees everything.
We need to seek Him more, have His mind, and give people His answers.
And not be such “know it alls”!
affirmation: Lord help me to be more humble, and remember that only You know and see everything.
verse: “If any man think that he knows anything, he knows nothing yet as he ought to know.” (1 Corinthians 8:2)

title: OUR MESSAGE
body: There are many people today who want nothing to do with God.
This is largely because they have a distorted image of God as a merciless tyrant who wants us to keep a lot of dumb rules.
This image has been ingrained in us all for generations by over zealous religionists.
People who say they love God should be less critical, less condemning. They should stop thinking they are so right and others wrong.
THEN, maybe people would get a clearer picture of what God is REALLY like.
God is LOVE.
Jesus taught love.
Should not LOVE really be OUR main message also?
affirmation: I know that God loves me and I love Him, and that’s all that really matters.
verse: “By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.” (John 13:35)

title: TO HAVE OR NOT
body: Have you noticed that sometimes those who have the most to be grateful for, complain the most? And those who have less are often the happiest? They are happy because they are grateful. Grateful for what they have.
It seems like the key to being happy is not having more, but being grateful. It must grieve God’s heart to see people who are ungrateful.
So, the biggest sin is not so much *having* things...
As much as not being grateful for what you already have!
affirmation: What I have is what God knows I need, and I am content and grateful.
verse: “Be content with such things as ye have: for He hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.” (Hebrews 13:5)

title: THINK YOU’RE TOUGH?
body: You have guns and tattoos. You have a fancy new car. You know how to fight. You think you’re tough!
You have your job. You have your power and wealth, pretty women, friends, reputation, and pride. People fear you!
Then you get sick. Flat on your back. Down, out, nowhere to look now but up. Your friends desert you!
You whimper like a baby cause you’re facing death. Your life’s come and gone and you have so little to show.

You finally wake up! And you cry out to God who with His love everlasting picks you up and makes you again. You cry out, God makes you a man, dependent on Him, with love and integrity, well rounded and whole.
Now THAT... took guts!
affirmation: Lord, make me and mold me into the person You want me to be.
verse: “A new heart will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you: and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you a tender heart.” (Ezekiel 36:26)

title: WHAT DO YOU WANT?
body: God doesn’t always give us what we WANT. But God always gives us what we NEED.
We really have such limited eyesight. We only see the “here and now”.
And that’s when we want everything: Right now!
Only God sees ahead. And only God knows what we really need.
Seriously now...
Shouldn’t that be what WE really want?
affirmation: May Your highest and perfect will be done in my life, O Lord.
verse: “Commit thy ways unto the Lord. Trust also in Him, and He shall bring it to pass.” (Psalms 37:5)

title: HOMEWARD BOUND
body: Every bit of dirt belongs to someone, and you cannot walk on it without their permission. Every bite of food belongs to someone, and you have to pay them if you want any of it.
The few wealthy are getting richer and richer. Greed is growing. Hearts are becoming colder.
Man gets more and more knowledge, yet has fewer and fewer answers. So many governments are dysfunctional. Families are fragile.
People are surrounded by a continual barrage of activity, but they are dying of loneliness. Yet, oddly we defend this reality and the very things that contribute to our unhappiness.
Only God’s love can set us free. Like a flickering candle the lights of this world are growing dimmer. God’s Word shines brighter and brighter.
Only His Word can lead us home. Stay close to Him.
affirmation: God’s Word gives me the right focus in my life.
verse: “Thy Word is a lamp unto my feet and a light unto my path.” (Psalms 119:105)

title: STAY CLOSE
body: A lot of people are afraid of talking about the Endtime and Jesus’ second coming.
Why is this?
Most people have heard a few things here and there about these topics but don’t have a clear picture of what lies ahead for us all.
We are living in troublous times, and things are going to get worse before they will get better.
Only a close personal relationship with the Lord will give us the wisdom, strength, and grace we will need to weather the storm.
Put Him first in your life, and He will protect you and keep you. Stay close to Jesus,
He will NEVER fail you.
affirmation: You Lord, are my refuge, no matter what troubles I encounter. I trust in You to see me through.
verse: “Thou hast been a shelter for me, and a strong tower from the enemy. I will trust in the cover of thy wings.” (Psalms 61:3-4)

title: A WORLD OF APPEARANCES
body: It seems like a lot of people are fake on Facebook and other social media. It’s like they are in a contest to try to show everyone how happy and successful they are.

You see this also all the time on television. Celebrities come out grinning, self satisfied with their so called achievements, and then sit and chitchat with the TV host about nothing of any real substance.

We’re in a world where people try to outdo each other flaunting their wealth, and showing everyone they have a better life than the rest of us. They even brag about their excesses. Selfishly having a good time seems to be their only goal.
Well, things of this world won’t last. They can only be enjoyed for a little while.
Only God, love and serving others bring lasting satisfaction.
affirmation: I don’t pretend to be something I’m not. What I do for God and others is what really matters.
verse: “This world will pass away and the things thereof, but he that does the will of God abides forever.” (1 John 2:17)

title: WE WILL JUDGE
body: When we come to the end of our lives, each one of us will see our lives flash before us.
In an instant, and in the Lord’s presence we will see everything we did. We will each judge ourselves based our own individual conscience.

At that moment we will see our lives from the standpoint of God’s love, and how much love we had, or didn’t have. We will realize to what degree we had let love guide our lives. And we will judge accordingly.
How fair and righteous of God to have us judge ourselves.
Who could do it better than us?
affirmation: I live my life in a loving way, and I will not be ashamed when I come to the end of it.
verse: “For nothing is secret, that shall not be made manifest; neither anything hidden, that shall not be known.” (Luke 8:17)

title: GIVE GOD A CHANCE
body: Do you have problems at home? Problems with your spouse, your mate, your child, or parent?
Here is something that may help. Something so simple, people underestimate its effectiveness.

Take a few minutes before you start your day to share a little something with that person you love. Sit down together and read one of our Reflections. Comment on what you just read. Then say a little prayer together before starting your day.
If you do this, you WILL grow closer to each other. You will start getting along better.
Give God a chance to work in their lives. Give God a chance to work in yours. And He will, through this simple act of sharing together for a few minutes each day. Only God can heal your relationship.
God is the One who unites. God is the One who heals.
But YOU have got to give God a CHANCE!
affirmation: Each morning I take time to read and pray together with my loved ones.
verse: “The family that prays together, stays together.”

title: LOVE TO THE FULLEST
body: So strong is the desire to find a mate that we can be attracted to a complete stranger in a matter of seconds.
We get almost hypnotized with a glance. We are infatuated when another soul resonates with ours, however fleeting that may be.
It’s crazy when you think about it. But it’s also beautiful.
Yet we condemn ourselves. People sometimes relegate these feelings to dark, base, primal instincts that are surely “not of God”.
Or so they would have us believe.
We, as God’s creatures, are here to love and live life to the fullest.
God does not condemn us.
Why should we?
affirmation: Love in all its forms is of God, and is pure and beautiful and praiseworthy.
verse: “If we love one another, God dwelleth in us and His love is perfected in us.” (1 John 4:12)

title: ONE STEP AT A TIME
body: You come to points in your life sometimes when you feel you’re not going to make it.

Maybe you have debts. Maybe you’re overworked, or have problems with your spouse, children, or neighbors. Your car is in the garage, or maybe you don’t even have one. Problems seem to surround you, and you see no solution.
Life can be overwhelming at times. But God gives us power for the hour, strength when we need it and not before. He will never forsake us.
The secret is to take it one day at a time.
We may not always see the full path ahead of us, but God is always faithful to show us the next step.
If we get alone sometime, someplace, and seek the Lord, He will give us the grace.
And He will always show us what to do, one step at a time.
affirmation: You never fail me, Lord. You are always there for me. I receive Your instructions.
verse: “This poor man cried, and the Lord delivered him out of all his troubles.” (Psalms 34:6)

title: THE SECRET TO MORE MONEY
body: Do you want more money? Do you need more money? The simple secret to getting more is: to give of what you already have!
I know it seems illogical, but it’s true!
Set aside a portion of every bit of money that comes in over the month. At the end of the month ask the Lord who you should give it to. And do it!
Give it away. And God will give you more!
Just don’t forget to tell them that the Lord told you to give it to them. Give God the glory.
Help a friend, a neighbor, a relative, or maybe someone you don’t even know, God will bless it. And you will feel good for doing it.
No one is too poor to give. That’s the key to God’s blessings and provision: “Give, and ye shall receive.”
But first YOU have got to do the GIVING!
affirmation: I faithfully give of what I have to those the Lord leads me to.
verse: “The liberal soul shall be made to prosper.” (Proverbs 11:25)

title: GOD’S LOVE
body: Do you love your son? Do you love your daughter?
Don’t you want the best for them? You share their joy, and feel their sorrows.
Aren’t you happy when they do something good, and doesn’t it tear your heart out when they get into problems?
You feel for them. You love them. Your children are your treasure.
NOW you know how God feels about YOU!
affirmation: I know You love me like no other, Lord. Your love is pure and everlasting.
verse: “If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall our Father which is in Heaven give good things to us?” (Matthew 7:11)

title: EAT, DRINK, BE GRATEFUL
body: Some people eat only vegetables. Others only meat.
There are so many diet plans, weight loss schemes, and food philosophies out there.
People swear by them.
Then there are people who have seemingly unhealthy eating habits yet live long and healthy lives. Who can you believe nowadays?
We believe moderation is the key.
We pretty much eat everything but just try not to go to extremes... At least not too often.
God gave us all kinds of things to enjoy.
Let’s just not forget to thank Him for it all.
affirmation: I enjoy this life that God has given me. My heart is full of gratitude to Him.
verse: “Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatever ye do, do all to the glory of God.” (1 Corinthians 10:31)

title: FREE AT LAST!
body: The majority of the people on this planet spend their lives working to make someone else rich.
They work day in and day out. They have little time left for the important things in their lives. Their sacrifices more often than not go unnoticed. It’s sad.
Man, out of greed, has turned his fellow man into his servant, his slave. And the saddest part of all is that we collectively have grown so used to it that we have accepted this as normal.
How far we have gone astray.

God didn’t create us to live this way. We are supposed to help our neighbor, and share each other’s burdens, as well as share the fruit of our labors. Not in greedy competition, but in loving cooperation.
Only God’s Love can pull us out of the pit that we ourselves created.
And FREE us ALL... at last!
affirmation: I am kind, helpful, and giving to everyone I encounter.
verse: “Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.” (Galatians 6:2)

title: HERE? or THERE?
body: Our legacy depends on the things we do in this life, day after day, deed after deed, as much as it depends on the things we don’t do.
We can glide through life filling it with temporary excitement and food, fun and games, or we can get down to business investing in things that will last, things of eternal value.
That’s why Jesus said to labor not for the things of this world, but for the things that we can take with us to the next.
That’s why we are here. It’s our choice.
It’s never too late to begin!
affirmation: I am investing in the world to come by putting God first in my life, and helping others.
verse: “Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon Earth. But rather lay up for yourselves treasures in Heaven.” (Matthew 6:19-20)

title: NO HIGHER CALLING
body: Many people throughout the world lovingly help the needy and feed the poor.
God bless them.
But Jesus said that we would always have the poor with us. And no matter how much we give, no matter what we do, as long as man is motivated by greed there will always be many people in great need.
So how can we make the biggest difference? How can we impact people’s lives the most?
We can choose to help them materially. Or we can help them spiritually. Which is best?
There are many good causes, but if you help people have a closer walk with the Lord, you not only help them in THIS life...
But also in the world to COME!
affirmation: I share my knowledge of God and His Word with others, that they may come to know Him and His love.
verse: “The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and he that winneth souls is wise.” (Proverbs 11:30)

title: IT’S ALWAYS FOR THE BEST
body: God always answers prayer.
Sometimes He says “yes”. Sometimes He says “no”. And sometimes He says “wait”.
God answers prayer but not always in the way we think He should.
People equate God answering prayer with them getting what they want. God may not always give you what you want. But He does always give you what you need.

Every time I’ve wanted one thing and God gave me something different, the day eventually came when I saw that God was right. I realized God had given me something better, something that I, due to my nearsightedness was unable to see at the time.
God will deny no good thing from the man or woman who puts Him FIRST in their life.
Everything works out for the best... in the end!
affirmation: I know You always have what’s best for me, Lord, and what will make me the happiest.
verse: “The Lord will give grace and glory: no good thing will He withhold from them that walk uprightly.” (Psalms 84:11)

title: SOLID GROUND
body: We live in a world where one news organization can fi	nothing good to say about someone. And then another news outlet can fi nothing but good things to say about him.
One country accuses another of using underhanded online tactics to hack their computer systems, yet they themselves do these same things to other countries that are perceived as their enemies.
We constantly hear of the positive or negative effects of a certain food, drug, procedure, habit, or concoction on our health, only to have it refuted months or years later.

Big business, governments, religions, ethnic and special interest groups, and loonies are pushing their agendas, some with good intentions, others not so much. So how can you know “up from down”? Good from bad? Which is right? What is true?
Where can you find solid ground? There is only one way: You are going to have to get it straight from God, in prayer. Man will let you down.
Only in GOD, can you trust!
affirmation: I trust in God alone for truth in the shifting sands of this old world.

title: UTTERLY DEPENDENT
body: You know what to do. You know what God wants you to do, what He expects of you. Yet it is really hard at times doing it!
We know we are supposed to be patient, loving, considerate, nice, kind, and gentle... But sometimes it’s just not easy!
The apostle Paul in the New Testament had the same problem. He knew what God wanted him to do, but he sure sometimes had a hard time doing it. He felt frustrated. He felt like a failure.
But he also knew he couldn’t do it. He realized that he couldn’t do it on his own. He realized that he needed God’s help. He realized he needed God’s strength, and God’s grace.
He realized the nothing he was and depended utterly on God for Him to do it through him.
That’s why God mightily used Him!
affirmation: I let you live and move and think through me Lord. With Your strength I am strong.
verse: “My strength is made perfect in (your) weakness.” (2 Corinthians 12:9)

title: WHO’S RIGHT
body: A lot of people in this world think that their religion is the only right one. They say everybody else’s is wrong.
There are Catholics who think that they have the only true religion. And evangelicals believe that only they are on the right path.
Jehovah’s Witnesses, Mormons, Pentecostals, Adventists, Baptists... the list goes on of all the people who think they are a notch above the rest of us because of their beliefs.
Jews are the same way. And Muslims. And Hindus. But they can’t ALL be right!
Why are people so obsessed with trying to prove that their religion is right, and everybody else’s is wrong? How weird is that!
They are ALL wrong!
God doesn’t want us to compete with each other... God wants us to LOVE one another!
affirmation: All that matters in this life is the love we share with others, not which church or religion any of us belong to.
verse: “This is My commandment, that ye love one another, as I have loved you.” (John 15:12)

title: LITTLE MAN. BIG GOD.
body: What is one tiny grain of sand compared to all the sand in the sea?
How tiny is our planet Earth when compared with not only the vastness of our own universe, but with all other possible universes combined?

Think of all the fish in the sea, all the animals on land, and the birds in the sky. Think of all the ants in the ground, bugs that crawl, and insects that fly. Think of all the hairs on your head. Think of all the hairs on all the heads of everyone that has ever lived!
Think of the vastness, of the “bigness” of everything that exists and has ever existed.
Now, think about this: It’s only when a man or woman realizes how little they are...
That they are on the path to discovering how big GOD IS!
affirmation: God is greater than anything and everything I can even imagine.
verse: “When I consider the heavens, the works of Thy hands, the moon and the stars which Thou hast ordained, what is man that Thou art mindful of Him?” (Psalms 8:3)

title: WANT THINGS YOUR WAY?
body: Atheists have all kinds of excuses for not believing in God. And they are not the only ones. Many Christians really don’t believe much either, sad to say!
That’s because they all want God to do things *their *way. They’re insisting on God doing things the way they want Him to, the way they think He should do them.
But God doesn’t work that way. To say God should do things your way is to say He should be bowing down at YOUR feet, that you know better than Him. What arrogance!
God has His conditions.
It is in keeping them, and tuning in to Him, that we discover His wonderful loving plan for us all!
affirmation: I trust in Your plan for my life, Lord. I know You will guide me as I surrender my all to You.
verse: “For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are My ways higher than your ways, and My thoughts than your thoughts.” (Isaiah 55:9)

title: LIKE THE MASTER
body: Jesus was meek. Jesus was humble. Should not these two traits most characterize us as Christians?
Should not a meek and humble attitude be the main focus of the church?
Should not meekness and humility be their main teaching? Should not the church be an example of meekness and humility to the world?
As followers of Christ, shouldn’t we each follow Jesus’ example of meekness and humility?
Shouldn’t these two things be our main focus? Shouldn’t we be like this above all else?
Meek. Humble. Like the Master.
affirmation: By having a humble attitude and a meek and gentle spirit I show others what Jesus is like.
verse: “Let this mind be in you which also was in Christ Jesus, who took upon him the form of a servant, and humbled himself.” (Philippians 2:5,7,8)

title: ANGER
body: It’s hard not to feel anger at times. It’s hard not to feel bad when someone mistreats us.
It’s hard not to be mad at someone who ignores you or turns a deaf ear to you.

That’s why people get mad and explode at their mates, neighbors, or government. They feel they can’t take it any longer. Some misguided souls even take it to extremes of violence, sad to say. But getting mad isn’t God’s solution.
Life is unfair. And people can be insensitive, even cruel at times.
And most of the time, in most instances we will never be able to set things straight. We will never be able to right all of life’s wrongs.
At these moments of frustration we must draw closer to the Lord. There, we find that safe place, the refuge that we all seek. The haven of peace... where all is well.
affirmation: When I feel frustrated and upset, I run to You, Lord, and find Your grace and peace.
verse: “Cast thy burden upon the Lord, and He shall sustain thee.” (Psalms 55:22)

title: THE ROAD TO HAPPINESS
body: People are in such a rush today. They are always in motion, going, getting.
But are they any happier? Are they really getting anywhere?
Happiness is not a destination.
Happiness is a means of transportation.
Life is NOT in getting everything you think you want...
But in appreciating the many things you already have!
affirmation: I am happy because I have so many things to be grateful for.
verse: “In everything give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you.” (1 Thessalonians 5:18)

title: MANY REASONS TO SMILE
body: Life is not always easy, and being able to smile even through difficulties is a great quality.
It gives strength and encouragement to someone who may be going through hard times too.
Someone once said, “Nothing is more beautiful and powerful than a smile that has struggled through the tears.” That’s so true.
A ray of sunshine is even more beautiful in the midst of rain.
God has blessed us with so much in this life.
Surely we ALL have many reasons to smile!
affirmation: I smile because God is real. I have the Lord in my heart and His help every day in my life.
verse: “In His presence is fullness of joy.” (Psalms 16:11)

title: CHRISTMAS EVE
body: Today is Christmas Eve.
This is the day that Joseph, along with Mary, his pregnant wife, couldn’t find a place to stay. This is the night they couldn’t find “room in the inn”.
They couldn’t find anyone to take them in, on this, of all nights. No one had pity on a poor carpenter and his pregnant wife.
How short sighted they were. Little did they realize what was happening before their eyes. This very night Mary would give birth to the savior of all mankind.
We, today, get so caught up in our lives that we sometimes fail to appreciate our many blessings, we don’t acknowledge our friends and loved ones, or recognize the beauty that is within our midst.
Life is fleeting. So quickly passing.
May we not forget God and His love for a moment.
affirmation: Open my eyes Lord, to see the greater things going on around me.
verse: “And Joseph went to the city of Bethlehem to be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.” (Luke 2:4-5)

title: SOMEONE’S BIRTHDAY
body: The Christmas season is a festive time of the year. In the weeks leading up to Christmas we decorate trees, our houses, our offices, and even ourselves.

December is a happy and joyous time with Santa Claus and his flying sleigh taking center stage, followed by Frosty, Rudolf, and a magical winter wonderland. And let’s not forget all the elves, the Nutcracker, Grinch, and Scrooge.
In the stores we find chocolates, fine pastries, candies, and imported delicacies that are only available at this time of year.

We make cookies and cakes and bake hams, turkeys, and pies. We lay out for ourselves sumptuous feasts which we share with family, friends, and loved ones while we watch our favorite sport on TV and sip eggnog, beer, fine spirits, and wine.
We buy and give each other any number of presents and gifts, ranging from the inexpensive to the extravagant.
Christmas is a time of sharing, a time of giving, and a time for receiving, feeling warm and fuzzy inside and wishing everyone, “Season’s Greetings.”
It is about having a good time.
But in all the noise and confusion of the season we seem to forget that today is someone’s birthday.
Christmas is the only birthday where people FORGET whose birthday it is!
affirmation: Happy Birthday dear Jesus. Thank You for coming into this world to save us from ourselves.
verse: “And she brought forth her first born son, and wrapped Him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger because there was no room in the inn. And there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night. And, lo, the angel of the Lord came upon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them, and they were sore afraid. And the angel said unto them, ‘Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people. For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, which is Christ the Lord.’ And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying, ‘Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.’” (Luke 2:7-14)

title: LEARNING, LEARNING, LEARNING
body: All through life we are learning. From the moment we are born we learn.
We learn to recognize our mother. We learn all about our new surroundings: smells, sights, and sounds. We are like little sponges, taking it all in.
But as we grow older some people stagnate. They lose their curiosity. They stop growing. They dry up. When we stop learning we die. So sad.
We have to always be open to learning new things. We have to stay open to change. That’s what keeps us alive. That’s what makes this life interesting and such a happy, fun experience.
And it doesn’t stop here:
We will keep learning, exploring, and growing, throughout all ETERNITY!
affirmation: I am open to new things, and embrace each opportunity to learn.
verse: “Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness for they shall be filled.” (Matthew 5:6)

title: INTEGRITY GONE
body: We are living in a world where you buy something, and then you hope it works by the time you get home. It’s crazy!

Think about it. You are bombarded by slick and cunning advertising. You buy a car, a computer, a phone, or something electronic. You spend good money for it. You take it home... and you hope it works!
When it doesn’t, you may face an uphill battle to have it made right.
Integrity has gone out the door. Might is right. Money is king. Greed reigns supreme.
In this quicksand world only God remains true. Only He will always be there for you.
His love is everlasting. His mercy sure.
Keep your eyes on HIM!
affirmation: God alone is my rock and my strength, the only one I can count on in this crazy world.
verse: “Jesus says, I am the way, the truth, and the life.” (John 14:6)

title: WHAT IS A CHRISTIAN?
body: A Christian is a follower of Christ.
It doesn’t matter if he is Catholic, Protestant, Evangelical, or nothing. If he accepts Jesus he is a Christian.
A true Christian is a follower of Christ’s teachings. And those teachings can be summed up in one word, love: Love for God, and love for your neighbor.
Are you a Christian? Do you love the Lord and follow His commandment to love others. If you do, you are already living in the blessings and favor of God.
Isn’t it great!
affirmation: I want to be more like You, Jesus. Thank you for Your example of love for us to follow.
verse: “But be ye doers of the Word, not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.” (James 1:22)

title: IT’S THAT IMPORTANT
body: Do you want more peace?
Do you want more strength?
Do you want more love? Spend more time in the Word.
Do you want more friends?
Do you want to know how to get along with your mate?
Do you want more out of life? Spend more time in the Word.
Do you want to know God better?
Do you want more of God’s blessings?
Do you want to know what life is all about?
Spend more time in the Word.
It’s that important!
affirmation: God’s Word is a very important part of my life. I can’t even go one day without it.
verse: “For the word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.” (Hebrews 4:12)

title: HOLD ON TO LOVE
body: A man and a woman meet. They embark upon a romance that may end up lasting only a short while instead of many years.

But when a relationship doesn’t last as long as what we hoped for, it doesn’t mean it was a mistake. Just because what started as a loving relationship didn’t play out as we wanted it to, doesn’t mean it was wrong.

God is love. Jesus came for love. He showed us how to love. Things sure didn’t turn out very nice for Him in the end, did they? But His loving us wasn’t a mistake. Jesus didn’t stop loving and neither should we.
So, love! And don’t look back with regrets. Don’t listen to the enemy’s lies that would rob you of God’s blessing.
Whatever you do for love will last forever!
affirmation: I know that the love I give will come back to me some day.
verse: “Cast thy bread upon the waters, and thou shalt find it after many days.” (Ecclesiastes 11:1)

title: NEW YEAR’S EVE
body: Today is the day so many have waited for.
Tonight is the night to go out and celebrate with family and friends...
Or stay at home.
Whichever you do, let’s not forget how much God has blessed us this last year.
Let’s not forget His goodness.
affirmation: Thank you, dear Lord, for my many blessings, for Your help, protection, and abundant care for me throughout the past year. Keep me close to You in this coming one.
verse: “Serve the Lord with gladness: come before His presence with singing. Know ye that the Lord, He is God: it is He that hath made us, and not we ourselves. For the Lord is good; His mercy is everlasting; and His truth endureth to all generations.” (Psalms 100:2,3,5)
